paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Being-H0.5: Scaling Human-Centric Robot Learning for Cross-Embodiment Generalization, Hao Luo+, arXiv'26, 2026.01
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, MultiModal, Reasoning, OpenWeight, CrossDomain, Robotics, VisionLanguageActionModel, UMM, Physics
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.12993 # Authors - Hao Luo - Ye Wang - Wanpeng Zhang - Sipeng Zheng - Ziheng Xi - Chaoyi Xu - Haiweng Xu - Haoqi Yuan - Chi Zhang - Yiqing Wang - Yicheng Feng - Zongqing Lu # Abstract - We introduce Being-H0.5, a foundational Vision-Language-Action (VLA) model designed for robust cross-embodiment generalization across diverse robotic platforms. While existing VLAs often struggle with morphological heterogeneity and data scarcity, we propose a human-centric learning paradigm that treats human interaction traces as a universal "mother tongue" for physical interaction. To support this, we present UniHand-2.0, the largest embodied pre-training recipe to date, comprising over 35,000 hours of multimodal data across 30 distinct robotic embodiments. Our approach introduces a Unified Action Space that maps heterogeneous robot controls into semantically aligned slots, enabling low-resource robots to bootstrap skills from human data and high-resource platforms. Built upon this human-centric foundation, we design a unified sequential modeling and multi-task pre-training paradigm to bridge human demonstrations and robotic execution. Architecturally, Being-H0.5 utilizes a Mixture-of-Transformers design featuring a novel Mixture-of-Flow (MoF) framework to decouple shared motor primitives from specialized embodiment-specific experts. Finally, to make cross-embodiment policies stable in the real world, we introduce Manifold-Preserving Gating for robustness under sensory shift and Universal Async Chunking to universalize chunked control across embodiments with different latency and control profiles. We empirically demonstrate that Being-H0.5 achieves state-of-the-art results on simulated benchmarks, such as LIBERO (98.9%) and RoboCasa (53.9%), while also exhibiting strong cross-embodiment capabilities on five robotic platforms. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂсЂЋсЂЙсЂќсЂЙсЂфсЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕсЃЌсЃЕсЃЃсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃасЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІт╝итЏ║сЂфсѓ»сЃГсѓ╣сѓесЃ│сЃюсЃЄсѓБсЃАсЃ│сЃѕСИђУѕгтїќсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪтЪ║уЏцсЂесЂфсѓІсЃЊсѓИсЃДсЃ│сЃ╗УеђУфъсЃ╗сѓбсѓ»сѓисЃДсЃ│№╝ѕVLA№╝ЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђЂBeing-H0.5сѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТЌбтГўсЂ«VLAсЂ»сђЂтйбТЁІуџёсЂфуЋ░У│фТђДсѓёсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«СИЇУХ│сЂФУІдсЂЌсѓђсЂЊсЂесЂїтцџсЂёсЂДсЂЎсЂїсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»С║║жќЊсЂ«уЏИС║њСйюућесЂ«уЌЋУиАсѓњуЅЕуљєуџёуЏИС║њСйюућесЂ«ТЎ«жЂЇуџёсЂфсђїТ»ЇтЏйУфъсђЇсЂесЂЌсЂдТЅ▒сЂєС║║жќЊСИГт┐ЃсЂ«тГду┐њсЃЉсЃЕсЃђсѓцсЃасѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓњТћ»ТїЂсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂ30сЂ«уЋ░сЂфсѓІсЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕсѓесЃ│сЃюсЃЄсѓБсЃАсЃ│сЃѕсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсЂБсЂд35,000ТЎѓжќЊС╗ЦСИісЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњтљФсѓђсђЂсЂЊсѓїсЂЙсЂДсЂДТюђтцДсЂ«сѓесЃ│сЃюсЃЄсѓБсЃАсЃ│сЃѕС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЃгсѓисЃћсЂДсЂѓсѓІUniHand-2.0сѓњуЎ║УАесЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«сѓбсЃЌсЃГсЃ╝сЃЂсЂ»сђЂуЋ░У│фсЂфсЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕтѕХтЙАсѓњТёЈтЉ│уџёсЂФТЋ┤тљѕсЂЌсЂЪсѓ╣сЃГсЃЃсЃѕсЂФсЃъсЃЃсЃћсЃ│сѓ░сЂЎсѓІух▒СИђсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсѓбсѓ»сѓисЃДсЃ│уЕ║жќЊсѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсђЂсЃфсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«т░ЉсЂфсЂёсЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕсЂїС║║жќЊсЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓёсЃфсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«У▒іт»їсЂфсЃЌсЃЕсЃЃсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃасЂІсѓЅсѓ╣сѓГсЃФсѓњсЃќсЃ╝сЃѕсѓ╣сЃѕсЃЕсЃЃсЃЌсЂДсЂЇсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂФсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«С║║жќЊСИГт┐ЃсЂ«тЪ║уЏцсЂ«СИісЂФсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»С║║жќЊсЂ«сЃЄсЃбсЃ│сѓ╣сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂесЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«т«ЪУАїсѓњсЂцсЂфсЂњсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«ух▒СИђсЂЋсѓїсЂЪжђљТгАсЃбсЃЄсЃФтїќсЂісѓѕсЂ│сЃъсЃФсЃЂсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»С║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЃЉсЃЕсЃђсѓцсЃасѓњУеГУеѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂ«жЮбсЂДсЂ»сђЂBeing-H0.5сЂ»Mixture-of-TransformersсЃЄсѓХсѓцсЃ│сѓњтѕЕућесЂЌсђЂтЁ▒ТюЅсЂЋсѓїсЂЪжЂІтІЋтјЪуљєсѓњт░ѓжќђсЂ«сѓесЃ│сЃюсЃЄсѓБсЃАсЃ│сЃѕуЅ╣уЋ░уџёсЂфсѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃѕсЂІсѓЅсЃЄсѓФсЃЃсЃЌсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂЎсѓІТќ░сЂЌсЂёMixture-of-Flow№╝ѕMoF№╝ЅсЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сѓњТјАућесЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТюђтЙїсЂФсђЂуЏИС║њсѓесЃ│сЃюсЃЄсѓБсЃАсЃ│сЃѕсЃЮсЃфсѓисЃ╝сѓњуЈЙт«ЪСИќуЋїсЂДт«Ѕт«џсЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂТёЪУдџсѓисЃЋсЃѕсЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІтаЁуЅбТђДсѓњТїЂсЂцтцџТДўСйЊС┐ЮтГўсѓ▓сЃ╝сЃєсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сЂесђЂуЋ░сЂфсѓІжЂЁт╗ХсЂісѓѕсЂ│тѕХтЙАсЃЌсЃГсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃФсѓњТїЂсЂцсѓесЃ│сЃюсЃЄсѓБсЃАсЃ│сЃѕжќЊсЂДсЃЂсЃБсЃ│сѓ»тѕХтЙАсѓњТЎ«жЂЇтїќсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сЃдсЃІсЃљсЃ╝сѓхсЃФжЮътљїТюЪсЃЂсЃБсЃ│сѓ»сѓцсЃ│сѓ░сѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂBeing-H0.5сЂїLIBERO№╝ѕ98.9%№╝ЅсѓёRoboCasa№╝ѕ53.9%№╝ЅсЂфсЂЕсЂ«сѓисЃЪсЃЦсЃгсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ухљТъюсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсђЂ5сЂцсЂ«сЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕсЃЌсЃЕсЃЃсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃасЂФсЂісЂёсЂдт╝итіЏсЂфсѓ»сЃГсѓ╣сѓесЃ│сЃюсЃЄсѓБсЃАсЃ│сЃѕУЃйтіЏсѓњуц║сЂЎсЂЊсЂесѓњухїжеЊуџёсЂФУе╝ТўјсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - Being-H0.5сЂ»сђЂсѓ»сЃГсѓ╣сѓесЃ│сЃюсЃЄсѓБсЃАсЃ│сЃѕСИђУѕгтїќсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪVLAсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂС║║жќЊсЂ«уЏИС║њСйюућесѓњсђїТ»ЇтЏйУфъсђЇсЂесЂЌсЂдТЅ▒сЂєтГду┐њсЃЉсЃЕсЃђсѓцсЃасѓњТЈљТАѕсђѓ35,000ТЎѓжќЊС╗ЦСИісЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњтљФсѓђUniHand-2.0сѓњућесЂёсЂдсђЂтцџТДўсЂфсЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕтѕХтЙАсѓњух▒СИђуџёсЂфсѓбсѓ»сѓисЃДсЃ│уЕ║жќЊсЂФсЃъсЃЃсЃћсЃ│сѓ░сЂЌсђЂсЃфсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«т░ЉсЂфсЂёсЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕсЂїС╗ќсЂ«сЃЌсЃЕсЃЃсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃасЂІсѓЅсѓ╣сѓГсЃФсѓњу┐њтЙЌсЂДсЂЇсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂФсЂЎсѓІсђѓBeing-H0.5сЂ»Mixture-of-TransformersсѓњТјАућесЂЌсђЂуЈЙт«ЪСИќуЋїсЂДсЂ«т«Ѕт«џТђДсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФтцџТДўСйЊС┐ЮтГўсѓ▓сЃ╝сЃєсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сЂесЃдсЃІсЃљсЃ╝сѓхсЃФжЮътљїТюЪсЃЂсЃБсЃ│сѓ»сѓцсЃ│сѓ░сѓњт░јтЁЦсђѓсѓисЃЪсЃЦсЃгсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ухљТъюсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсђЂ5сЂцсЂ«сЃГсЃюсЃЃсЃѕсЃЌсЃЕсЃЃсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃасЂДт╝итіЏсЂфУЃйтіЏсѓњуц║сЂЎсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] STEP3-VL-10B Technical Report, Ailin Huang+, arXiv'26, 2026.01
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, OpenWeight, read-later, Selected Papers/Blogs, VisionLanguageModel, UMM, Initial Impression Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.09668 # Authors - Ailin Huang - Chengyuan Yao - Chunrui Han - Fanqi Wan - Hangyu Guo - Haoran Lv - Hongyu Zhou - Jia Wang - Jian Zhou - Jianjian Sun - Jingcheng Hu - Kangheng Lin - Liang Zhao - Mitt Huang - Song Yuan - Wenwen Qu - Xiangfeng Wang - Yanlin Lai - Yingxiu Zhao - Yinmin Zhang - Yukang Shi - Yuyang Chen - Zejia Weng - Ziyang Meng - Ang Li - Aobo Kong - Bo Dong - Changyi Wan - David Wang - Di Qi - Dingming Li - En Yu - Guopeng Li - Haiquan Yin - Han Zhou - Hanshan Zhang - Haolong Yan - Hebin Zhou - Hongbo Peng - Jiaran Zhang - Jiashu Lv - Jiayi Fu - Jie Cheng - Jie Zhou - Jisheng Yin - Jingjing Xie - Jingwei Wu - Jun Zhang - Junfeng Liu - Kaijun Tan - Kaiwen Yan - Liangyu Chen - Lina Chen - Mingliang Li - Qian Zhao - Quan Sun - Shaoliang Pang - Shengjie Fan - Shijie Shang - Siyuan Zhang - Tianhao You - Wei Ji - Wuxun Xie - Xiaobo Yang - Xiaojie Hou - Xiaoran Jiao - Xiaoxiao Ren - Xiangwen Kong - Xin Huang - Xin Wu - Xing Chen - Xinran Wang - Xuelin Zhang - Yana Wei - Yang Li - Yanming Xu - Yeqing Shen - Yuang Peng - Yue Peng - Yu Zhou - Yusheng Li - Yuxiang Yang - Yuyang Zhang - Zhe Xie - Zhewei Huang - Zhenyi Lu - Zhimin Fan - Zihui Cheng - Daxin Jiang - Qi Han - Xiangyu Zhang - Yibo Zhu - Zheng Ge # Abstract - We present STEP3-VL-10B, a lightweight open-source foundation model designed to redefine the trade-off between compact efficiency and frontier-level multimodal intelligence. STEP3-VL-10B is realized through two strategic shifts: first, a unified, fully unfrozen pre-training strategy on 1.2T multimodal tokens that integrates a language-aligned Perception Encoder with a Qwen3-8B decoder to establish intrinsic vision-language synergy; and second, a scaled post-training pipeline featuring over 1k iterations of reinforcement learning. Crucially, we implement Parallel Coordinated Reasoning (PaCoRe) to scale test-time compute, allocating resources to scalable perceptual reasoning that explores and synthesizes diverse visual hypotheses. Consequently, despite its compact 10B footprint, STEP3-VL-10B rivals or surpasses models 10$\times$-20$\times$ larger (e.g., GLM-4.6V-106B, Qwen3-VL-235B) and top-tier proprietary flagships like Gemini 2.5 Pro and Seed-1.5-VL. Delivering best-in-class performance, it records 92.2% on MMBench and 80.11% on MMMU, while excelling in complex reasoning with 94.43% on AIME2025 and 75.95% on MathVision. We release the full model suite to provide the community with a powerful, efficient, and reproducible baseline. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂсѓ│сЃ│сЃЉсѓ»сЃѕсЂфті╣ујЄсЂеТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФуЪЦУЃйсЂесЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃЅсѓфсЃЋсѓњтєЇт«џуЙЕсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсђЂУ╗йжЄЈсЂфсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣тЪ║уЏцсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђїSTEP3-VL-10BсђЇсѓњуЎ║УАесЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓSTEP3-VL-10BсЂ»сђЂ1.2TсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІух▒СИђсЂЋсѓїсЂЪт«їтЁесЂФтЄЇухљУДБжЎцсЂЋсѓїсЂЪС║ІтЅЇсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТѕдуЋЦсЂесђЂУеђУфъсЂФТЋ┤тљѕсЂЌсЂЪуЪЦУдџсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЂеQwen3-8BсЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсѓњух▒тљѕсЂЌсђЂтєЁтюеуџёсЂфУдќУдџсЂеУеђУфъсЂ«уЏИС║њСйюућесѓњуб║уФІсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂФсѓѕсЂБсЂдт«ЪуЈЙсЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЙсЂЪсђЂ1kтЏъС╗ЦСИісЂ«т╝итїќтГду┐њсѓњуЅ╣тЙ┤сЂесЂЎсѓІсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃЕсЃќсЃФсЂфтЙїтЄдуљєсЃЉсѓцсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсЃ│сѓѓт░јтЁЦсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЅ╣сЂФсђЂсЃєсѓ╣сЃѕТЎѓсЂ«Уеѕу«Ќсѓњсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃФсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂСИдтѕЌтЇћУф┐ТјеУФќ№╝ѕPaCoRe№╝Ѕсѓњт«ЪУБЁсЂЌсђЂтцџТДўсЂфУдќУдџС╗«УфгсѓњТјбу┤бсЂЌух▒тљѕсЂЎсѓІсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃЕсЃќсЃФсЂфуЪЦУдџТјеУФќсЂФсЃфсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓњтЅ▓сѓітйЊсЂдсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЮсЂ«ухљТъюсђЂсѓ│сЃ│сЃЉсѓ»сЃѕсЂф10BсЂ«сЃЋсЃЃсЃѕсЃЌсЃфсЃ│сЃѕсЂФсѓѓсЂІсЂІсѓЈсѓЅсЂџсђЂSTEP3-VL-10BсЂ»сђЂ10тђЇсЂІсѓЅ20тђЇтцДсЂЇсЂёсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕСЙІ№╝џGLM-4.6V-106BсђЂQwen3-VL-235B№╝ЅсѓёсђЂGemini 2.5 ProсђЂSeed-1.5-VLсЂесЂёсЂБсЂЪсЃѕсЃЃсЃЌсѓ»сЃЕсѓ╣сЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсѓесѓ┐сЃфсЃЋсЃЕсѓ░сѓисЃЃсЃЌсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЎсѓІсЂІсђЂсЂЮсѓїсѓњСИітЏъсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсђЂMMBenchсЂД92.2%сђЂMMMUсЂД80.11%сЂ«ТѕљТъюсѓњУеўжї▓сЂЌсђЂAIME2025сЂДсЂ»94.43%сђЂMathVisionсЂДсЂ»75.95%сЂеУцЄжЏЉсЂфТјеУФќсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсѓѓтёфсѓїсЂЪухљТъюсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсЂФт╝итіЏсЂДті╣ујЄуџёсЂІсЂцтєЇуЈЙтЈ»УЃйсЂфтЪ║Т║ќсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂтЁесЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓ╣сѓцсЃ╝сЃѕсѓњсЃфсЃфсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - STEP3-VL-10BсЂ»сђЂті╣ујЄсЂеТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФуЪЦУЃйсЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃЅсѓфсЃЋсѓњтєЇт«џуЙЕсЂЎсѓІУ╗йжЄЈсЂфсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣тЪ║уЏцсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђѓУеђУфъсЂФТЋ┤тљѕсЂЌсЂЪуЪЦУдџсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЂеQwen3-8BсЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсѓњух▒тљѕсЂЌсђЂ1kтЏъС╗ЦСИісЂ«т╝итїќтГду┐њсѓњтљФсѓђсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃЕсЃќсЃФсЂфтЙїтЄдуљєсЃЉсѓцсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсЃ│сѓњт░јтЁЦсђѓСИдтѕЌтЇћУф┐ТјеУФќсѓњт«ЪУБЁсЂЌсђЂУдќУдџТјеУФќсЂ«Тјбу┤бсЂеух▒тљѕсѓњТюђжЂЕтїќсђѓсѓ│сЃ│сЃЉсѓ»сЃѕсЂфсЂїсѓЅсђЂС╗ќсЂ«тцДУдЈТеАсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЎсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсђЂMMBenchсЂД92.2%сђЂAIME2025сЂД94.43%сЂфсЂЕсЂ«ТѕљТъюсѓњУеўжї▓сђѓтєЇуЈЙтЈ»УЃйсЂфтЪ║Т║ќсЂесЂЌсЂдтЁесЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓ╣сѓцсЃ╝сЃѕсѓњсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсЂФТЈљСЙЏсђѓ

maintainer-comment-text.p: сЂЪсЂБсЂЪсЂ«10BсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФсѓѓсЂІсЂІсѓЈсѓЅсЂџсђЂ100B, 200Bу┤џсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»ТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂunifiedсЂфсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂДС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њСИГсЂФтЁесЂдсЂ«сЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњunfrozenсЂфСИісЂД1.2TсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂДтГду┐њсЂЌсђЂPaCoReсЂетЉ╝сЂ░сѓїсѓІRLсЂДтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсЂЪtest time scalingТЅІТ│ЋсѓёсђЂGRPOу│╗сЂДсЂ»сЂфсЂЈPPOсѓњRLсЂДТјАућесЂЎсѓІсЂфсЂЕсђЂсЃдсЃІсЃ╝сѓ»сЂфтиЦтцФсЂїТ║ђУ╝ЅсЂФУдІсЂѕсђЂжЄЇУдЂуаћуЕХсЂФУдІсЂѕсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] HeartMuLa: A Family of Open Sourced Music Foundation Models, Dongchao Yang+, arXiv'26, 2026.01
paper-entry.tags: NLP, MultiModal, FoundationModel, SpeechProcessing, OpenWeight, AudioLanguageModel, Music
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.10547 # Authors - Dongchao Yang - Yuxin Xie - Yuguo Yin - Zheyu Wang - Xiaoyu Yi - Gongxi Zhu - Xiaolong Weng - Zihan Xiong - Yingzhe Ma - Dading Cong - Jingliang Liu - Zihang Huang - Jinghan Ru - Rongjie Huang - Haoran Wan - Peixu Wang - Kuoxi Yu - Helin Wang - Liming Liang - Xianwei Zhuang - Yuanyuan Wang - Haohan Guo - Junjie Cao - Zeqian Ju - Songxiang Liu - Yuewen Cao - Heming Weng - Yuexian Zou # Abstract - We present a family of open-source Music Foundation Models designed to advance large-scale music understanding and generation across diverse tasks and modalities. Our framework consists of four major components: (1) HeartCLAP, an audio-text alignment model; (2) HeartTranscriptor, a robust lyric recognition model optimized for real-world music scenarios; and (3) HeartCodec, a low-frame-rate (12.5 Hz) yet high-fidelity music codec tokenizer that captures long-range musical structure while preserving fine-grained acoustic details and enabling efficient autoregressive modeling; (4) HeartMuLa, an LLM-based song generation model capable of synthesizing high-fidelity music under rich, user-controllable conditions (e.g., textual style descriptions, lyrics, and reference audio). In addition, it provides two specialized modes: (i) fine-grained musical attribute control, which allows users to specify the style of different song sections (e.g., intro, verse, chorus) using natural language prompts; and (ii) short, engaging music generation, which is suitable as background music for short videos. Lastly, HeartMuLa improves significantly when scaled to 7B parameters. For the first time, we show that a Suno-level, commercial-grade system can be reproduced using academic-scale data and GPU resources. We expect these foundation models to serve as strong baselines for future research and to facilitate practical applications in multimodal content production. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂтцДУдЈТеАсЂфжЪ│ТЦйуљєУДБсЂеућЪТѕљсѓњжђ▓сѓЂсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуЏ«уџёсЂесЂЌсЂЪсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«жЪ│ТЦйтЪ║уЏцсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«сЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сЂ»сђЂС╗ЦСИІсЂ«4сЂцсЂ«СИ╗УдЂсѓ│сЃ│сЃЮсЃ╝сЃЇсЃ│сЃѕсЂІсѓЅТДІТѕљсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎ№╝џ(1) HeartCLAPсђЂжЪ│тБ░сЂесЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«сѓбсЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЃАсЃ│сЃѕсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝Џ(2) HeartTranscriptorсђЂт«ЪжџЏсЂ«жЪ│ТЦйсѓисЃісЃфсѓфсЂФТюђжЂЕтїќсЂЋсѓїсЂЪтаЁуЅбсЂфТГїУЕъУфЇУГўсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝Џ(3) HeartCodecсђЂсЃГсЃ╝сЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃгсЃ╝сЃѕ№╝ѕ12.5 Hz№╝ЅсЂфсЂїсѓЅжФўт┐ат«Ът║дсЂ«жЪ│ТЦйсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЄсЃЃсѓ»сЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃісѓцсѓХсЃ╝сЂДсђЂжЋиУиЮжЏбсЂ«жЪ│ТЦйТДІжђасѓњТЇЅсЂѕсЂцсЂцсђЂУЕ│у┤░сЂфжЪ│жЪ┐уџёуЅ╣тЙ┤сѓњС┐ЮТїЂсЂЌсђЂті╣ујЄуџёсЂфУЄфти▒тЏътИ░сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎ№╝Џ(4) HeartMuLaсђЂУ▒іт»їсЂДсЃдсЃ╝сѓХсЃ╝сЂїтѕХтЙАтЈ»УЃйсЂфТЮАС╗Х№╝ѕСЙІ№╝џсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсѓ╣сѓ┐сѓцсЃФсЂ«УфгТўјсђЂТГїУЕъсђЂтЈѓуЁДжЪ│тБ░№╝ЅсЂ«СИІсЂДжФўт┐ат«ЪжЪ│ТЦйсѓњтљѕТѕљсЂДсЂЇсѓІLLMсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«ТГїућЪТѕљсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂ2сЂцсЂ«уЅ╣тїќсЃбсЃ╝сЃЅсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎ№╝џ(i) тЙ«у┤░сЂфжЪ│ТЦйт▒ъТђДсЂ«тѕХтЙАсЂДсЂ»сђЂсЃдсЃ╝сѓХсЃ╝сЂїУЄфуёХУеђУфъсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсѓњСй┐ућесЂЌсЂдуЋ░сЂфсѓІТЏ▓сЂ«сѓ╗сѓ»сѓисЃДсЃ│№╝ѕСЙІ№╝џсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЃГсђЂсЃ┤сѓАсЃ╝сѓ╣сђЂсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЕсѓ╣№╝ЅсЂ«сѓ╣сѓ┐сѓцсЃФсѓњТїЄт«џсЂДсЂЇсЂЙсЂЎ№╝Џ(ii) уЪГсЂЈжГЁтіЏуџёсЂфжЪ│ТЦйућЪТѕљсЂДсЂ»сђЂуЪГсЂёсЃЊсЃЄсѓфсЂ«УЃїТЎ»жЪ│ТЦйсЂесЂЌсЂджЂЕсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТюђтЙїсЂФсђЂHeartMuLaсЂ»7BсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂФсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃФсѓбсЃЃсЃЌсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДтцДт╣ЁсЂФТћ╣тќёсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂтѕЮсѓЂсЂдтГдУАЊУдЈТеАсЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂеGPUсЃфсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓњСй┐ућесЂЌсЂдSunoсЃгсЃЎсЃФсЂ«тЋєТЦГсѓ░сЃгсЃ╝сЃЅсѓисѓ╣сЃєсЃасѓњтєЇуЈЙсЂДсЂЇсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«тЪ║уЏцсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂїС╗ітЙїсЂ«уаћуЕХсЂ«т╝итіЏсЂфсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЂесЂфсѓісђЂтцџсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсЃ│сЃётѕХСйюсЂ«т«ЪућеуџёсЂфт┐юућесѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњТюЪтЙЁсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«жЪ│ТЦйтЪ║уЏцсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсђЂжЪ│ТЦйуљєУДБсЂеућЪТѕљсѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсђѓСИ╗УдЂсЂф4сЂцсЂ«сѓ│сЃ│сЃЮсЃ╝сЃЇсЃ│сЃѕсЂ»сђЂжЪ│тБ░сЃ╗сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсѓбсЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЃАсЃ│сЃѕсђЂтаЁуЅбсЂфТГїУЕъУфЇУГўсђЂжФўт┐ат«Ът║джЪ│ТЦйсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЄсЃЃсѓ»сђЂсЃдсЃ╝сѓХсЃ╝тѕХтЙАтЈ»УЃйсЂфТЮАС╗ХсЂДсЂ«ТГїућЪТѕљсѓњтљФсѓђсђѓуЅ╣тїќсЃбсЃ╝сЃЅсЂДсЂ»сђЂжЪ│ТЦйт▒ъТђДсЂ«тѕХтЙАсЂеуЪГсЂёжГЁтіЏуџёсЂфућЪТѕљсЂїтЈ»УЃйсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂтЋєТЦГсѓ░сЃгсЃ╝сЃЅсЂ«сѓисѓ╣сЃєсЃасѓњтєЇуЈЙсЂЌсђЂтцџсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсЃ│сЃётѕХСйюсѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІтЪ║уЏцсѓњТДІу»Ѕсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] TranslateGemma Technical Report, Mara Finkelstein+, arXiv'26, 2026.01
paper-entry.tags: MachineTranslation, NLP, LanguageModel, SmallModel, MultiLingual, OpenWeight, Selected Papers/Blogs, One-Line Notes, Initial Impression Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - preprint: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.09012 - blog: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/translategemma/ # Authors - Mara Finkelstein - Isaac Caswell - Tobias Domhan - Jan-Thorsten Peter - Juraj Juraska - Parker Riley - Daniel Deutsch - Cole Dilanni - Colin Cherry - Eleftheria Briakou - Elizabeth Nielsen - Jiaming Luo - Kat Black - Ryan Mullins - Sweta Agrawal - Wenda Xu - Erin Kats - Stephane Jaskiewicz - Markus Freitag - David Vilar # Abstract - We present TranslateGemma, a suite of open machine translation models based on the Gemma 3 foundation models. To enhance the inherent multilingual capabilities of Gemma 3 for the translation task, we employ a two-stage fine-tuning process. First, supervised fine-tuning is performed using a rich mixture of high-quality large-scale synthetic parallel data generated via state-of-the-art models and human-translated parallel data. This is followed by a reinforcement learning phase, where we optimize translation quality using an ensemble of reward models, including MetricX-QE and AutoMQM, targeting translation quality. We demonstrate the effectiveness of TranslateGemma with human evaluation on the WMT25 test set across 10 language pairs and with automatic evaluation on the WMT24++ benchmark across 55 language pairs. Automatic metrics show consistent and substantial gains over the baseline Gemma 3 models across all sizes. Notably, smaller TranslateGemma models often achieve performance comparable to larger baseline models, offering improved efficiency. We also show that TranslateGemma models retain strong multimodal capabilities, with enhanced performance on the Vistra image translation benchmark. The release of the open TranslateGemma models aims to provide the research community with powerful and adaptable tools for machine translation. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»TranslateGemmaсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂ»Gemma 3тЪ║уЏцсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфТЕЪТб░у┐╗Уе│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂДсЂЎсђѓу┐╗Уе│сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІGemma 3сЂ«тЏ║ТюЅсЂ«тцџУеђУфъУЃйтіЏсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂС║їТ«хжџјсЂ«сЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЌсЃГсѓ╗сѓ╣сѓњТјАућесЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЙсЂџсђЂжФўтЊЂУ│фсЂфтцДУдЈТеАтљѕТѕљт╣│УАїсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂеС║║жќЊсЂїу┐╗Уе│сЂЌсЂЪт╣│УАїсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњућесЂёсЂдсђЂуЏБУдќС╗ўсЂЇсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњУАїсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТгАсЂФсђЂу┐╗Уе│сЂ«У│фсѓњТюђжЂЕтїќсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂMetricX-QEсѓёAutoMQMсѓњтљФсѓђта▒жЁгсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сѓбсЃ│сѓхсЃ│сЃќсЃФсѓњућесЂёсЂЪт╝итїќтГду┐њсЂ«Т«хжџјсЂїуХџсЂЇсЂЙсЂЎсђѓTranslateGemmaсЂ«ТюЅті╣ТђДсѓњсђЂ10сЂ«УеђУфъсЃџсѓбсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсѓІWMT25сЃєсѓ╣сЃѕсѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂДсЂ«С║║жќЊУЕЋСЙАсЂесђЂ55сЂ«УеђУфъсЃџсѓбсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсѓІWMT24++сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсЂ«УЄфтІЋУЕЋСЙАсѓњжђџсЂўсЂдуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓУЄфтІЋТїЄТеЎсЂ»сђЂсЂЎсЂ╣сЂдсЂ«сѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЂ«Gemma 3сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдСИђУ▓ФсЂЌсЂЪтцДт╣ЁсЂфтљЉСИісѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЅ╣сЂФсђЂт░ЈтъІсЂ«TranslateGemmaсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂтцДтъІсЂ«сЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЎсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњсЂЌсЂ░сЂЌсЂ░жЂћТѕљсЂЌсђЂті╣ујЄсЂїтљЉСИісЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЙсЂЪсђЂTranslateGemmaсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЂфУЃйтіЏсѓњС┐ЮТїЂсЂЌсђЂVistraућ╗тЃЈу┐╗Уе│сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсЂ«ТђДУЃйсЂїтљЉСИісЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓѓуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфTranslateGemmaсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃфсЃфсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ»сђЂуаћуЕХсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсЂФт╝итіЏсЂДжЂЕт┐ютЈ»УЃйсЂфТЕЪТб░у┐╗Уе│сЂ«сЃёсЃ╝сЃФсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуЏ«уџёсЂесЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - TranslateGemmaсЂ»сђЂGemma 3сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈТЕЪТб░у┐╗Уе│сЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂДсђЂС║їТ«хжџјсЂ«сЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЌсЃГсѓ╗сѓ╣сѓњТјАућесђѓтѕЮсѓЂсЂФжФўтЊЂУ│фсЂфСИдУАїсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂДуЏБУдќС╗ўсЂЇсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњУАїсЂёсђЂсЂЮсЂ«тЙїта▒жЁгсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФсѓѕсѓІт╝итїќтГду┐њсЂДу┐╗Уе│тЊЂУ│фсѓњТюђжЂЕтїќсђѓWMT25сЃєсѓ╣сЃѕсѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂДсЂ«С║║жќЊУЕЋСЙАсЂеWMT24++сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсЂ«УЄфтІЋУЕЋСЙАсѓњжђџсЂўсЂдТюЅті╣ТђДсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂУЄфтІЋТїЄТеЎсЂДсЂ»тцДт╣ЁсЂфТђДУЃйтљЉСИісЂїуб║УфЇсЂЋсѓїсѓІсђѓуЅ╣сЂФт░ЈтъІсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»тцДтъІсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЎсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњТїЂсЂАсЂцсЂцті╣ујЄсЂїтљЉСИісђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФУЃйтіЏсѓѓС┐ЮТїЂсЂЌсђЂућ╗тЃЈу┐╗Уе│сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсЂ«ТђДУЃйтљЉСИісЂїта▒тЉісЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓTranslateGemmaсЂ«тЁгжќІсЂ»сђЂуаћуЕХсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсЂФт╝итіЏсЂДжЂЕт┐ютЈ»УЃйсЂфу┐╗Уе│сЃёсЃ╝сЃФсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуЏ«ТїЄсЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] VerseCrafter: Dynamic Realistic Video World Model with 4D Geometric Control, Sixiao Zheng+, arXiv'26, 2026.01
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Controllable, OpenWeight, WorldModels, 4D (Video), Geometric
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.05138 # Authors - Sixiao Zheng - Minghao Yin - Wenbo Hu - Xiaoyu Li - Ying Shan - Yanwei Fu # Abstract - Video world models aim to simulate dynamic, real-world environments, yet existing methods struggle to provide unified and precise control over camera and multi-object motion, as videos inherently operate dynamics in the projected 2D image plane. To bridge this gap, we introduce VerseCrafter, a 4D-aware video world model that enables explicit and coherent control over both camera and object dynamics within a unified 4D geometric world state. Our approach is centered on a novel 4D Geometric Control representation, which encodes the world state through a static background point cloud and per-object 3D Gaussian trajectories. This representation captures not only an object's path but also its probabilistic 3D occupancy over time, offering a flexible, category-agnostic alternative to rigid bounding boxes or parametric models. These 4D controls are rendered into conditioning signals for a pretrained video diffusion model, enabling the generation of high-fidelity, view-consistent videos that precisely adhere to the specified dynamics. Unfortunately, another major challenge lies in the scarcity of large-scale training data with explicit 4D annotations. We address this by developing an automatic data engine that extracts the required 4D controls from in-the-wild videos, allowing us to train our model on a massive and diverse dataset. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - сЃЊсЃЄсѓфсЃ»сЃ╝сЃФсЃЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂтІЋуџёсЂфуЈЙт«ЪСИќуЋїсЂ«уњ░тбЃсѓњсѓисЃЪсЃЦсЃгсЃ╝сЃѕсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуЏ«ТїЄсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсЂїсђЂТЌбтГўсЂ«ТЅІТ│ЋсЂ»сѓФсЃАсЃЕсЂеУцЄТЋ░сЂ«сѓфсЃќсѓИсѓДсѓ»сЃѕсЂ«тІЋсЂЇсЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІух▒СИђуџёсЂІсЂцТГБуб║сЂфтѕХтЙАсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЎсѓІсЂ«сЂФУІдті┤сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂ»сђЂсЃЊсЃЄсѓфсЂїТіЋтй▒сЂЋсѓїсЂЪ2Dућ╗тЃЈт╣│жЮбтєЁсЂДтІЋуџёсЂФТЊЇСйюсЂЋсѓїсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сѓ«сЃБсЃЃсЃЌсѓњтЪІсѓЂсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»VerseCrafterсѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂ»сђЂсѓФсЃАсЃЕсЂесѓфсЃќсѓИсѓДсѓ»сЃѕсЂ«сЃђсѓцсЃісЃЪсѓ»сѓ╣сЂФТўјуц║уџёсЂІсЂцСИђУ▓ФсЂЌсЂЪтѕХтЙАсѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЎсѓІ4Dт»Йт┐юсЂ«сЃЊсЃЄсѓфсЃ»сЃ╝сЃФсЃЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«сѓбсЃЌсЃГсЃ╝сЃЂсЂ»сђЂжЮЎуџёсЂфУЃїТЎ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсѓ»сЃЕсѓдсЃЅсЂесѓфсЃќсѓИсѓДсѓ»сЃѕсЂћсЂесЂ«3Dсѓгсѓдсѓ╣У╗їУиАсѓњжђџсЂўсЂдСИќуЋїсЂ«уіХТЁІсѓњсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂЎсѓІТќ░сЂЌсЂё4Dт╣ЙСйЋтГдуџётѕХтЙАУАеуЈЙсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂёсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«УАеуЈЙсЂ»сђЂсѓфсЃќсѓИсѓДсѓ»сЃѕсЂ«У╗їжЂЊсЂасЂЉсЂДсЂфсЂЈсђЂТЎѓжќЊсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсѓІуб║ујЄуџёсЂф3DтЇаТюЅсѓњсѓГсЃБсЃЌсЃЂсЃБсЂЌсђЂтЅЏСйЊсЃљсѓдсЃ│сЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сЃюсЃЃсѓ»сѓ╣сѓёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃѕсЃфсЃЃсѓ»сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІТЪћУ╗ЪсЂДсѓФсЃєсѓ┤сЃфсЂФСЙЮтГўсЂЌсЂфсЂёС╗БТЏ┐ТЅІТ«хсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«4DтѕХтЙАсЂ»сђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЌсЂЪсЃЊсЃЄсѓфТІАТЋБсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«ТЮАС╗ХС┐АтЈисЂФсЃгсЃ│сЃђсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂЋсѓїсђЂТїЄт«џсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃђсѓцсЃісЃЪсѓ»сѓ╣сЂФТГБуб║сЂФтЙЊсЂБсЂЪжФўт┐ат«Ът║дсЂДУдќуѓ╣СИђУ▓ФТђДсЂ«сЂѓсѓІсЃЊсЃЄсѓфућЪТѕљсѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТ«Іт┐хсЂфсЂїсѓЅсђЂсѓѓсЂєСИђсЂцсЂ«тцДсЂЇсЂфУф▓жАїсЂ»сђЂТўјуц║уџёсЂф4DсѓбсЃјсЃєсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сѓњТїЂсЂцтцДУдЈТеАсЂфсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«СИЇУХ│сЂДсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂжЄјућЪсЂ«сЃЊсЃЄсѓфсЂІсѓЅт┐ЁУдЂсЂф4DтѕХтЙАсѓњТійтЄ║сЂЎсѓІУЄфтІЋсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓесЃ│сѓИсЃ│сѓњжќІуЎ║сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсЂЊсѓїсЂФт»ЙтЄдсЂЌсђЂтиетцДсЂДтцџТДўсЂфсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂДсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂДсЂЇсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂФсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - VerseCrafterсЂ»сђЂсѓФсЃАсЃЕсЂесѓфсЃќсѓИсѓДсѓ»сЃѕсЂ«тІЋсЂЇсѓњСИђУ▓ФсЂЌсЂдтѕХтЙАсЂЎсѓІ4DсЃЊсЃЄсѓфсЃ»сЃ╝сЃФсЃЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњТЈљТАѕсђѓжЮЎуџёсЂфУЃїТЎ»сЂе3Dсѓгсѓдсѓ╣У╗їУиАсѓњСй┐ућесЂЌсЂдсђЂсѓфсЃќсѓИсѓДсѓ»сЃѕсЂ«уб║ујЄуџёсЂф3DтЇаТюЅсѓњУАеуЈЙсЂЌсђЂжФўт┐ат«Ът║дсЂфсЃЊсЃЄсѓфућЪТѕљсѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЎсѓІсђѓУЄфтІЋсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓесЃ│сѓИсЃ│сЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂтцДУдЈТеАсЂф4DсѓбсЃјсЃєсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсѓњжЄјућЪсЂ«сЃЊсЃЄсѓфсЂІсѓЅТійтЄ║сЂЌсђЂсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњТћ»ТЈ┤сђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] UniVideo: Unified Understanding, Generation, and Editing for Videos, Cong Wei+, ICLR'26, 2025.10
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, LanguageModel, Transformer, MultiModal, DiffusionModel, VariationalAutoEncoder, OpenWeight, ICLR, read-later, Selected Papers/Blogs, VideoGeneration/Understandings, Editing
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.08377 # Authors - Cong Wei - Quande Liu - Zixuan Ye - Qiulin Wang - Xintao Wang - Pengfei Wan - Kun Gai - Wenhu Chen # Abstract - Unified multimodal models have shown promising results in multimodal content generation and editing but remain largely limited to the image domain. In this work, we present UniVideo, a versatile framework that extends unified modeling to the video domain. UniVideo adopts a dual-stream design, combining a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) for instruction understanding with a Multimodal DiT (MMDiT) for video generation. This design preserves the MLLM's original text generation capabilities, enables accurate interpretation of complex multimodal instructions, and maintains visual consistency in the generated content. Built on this architecture, UniVideo unifies diverse video generation and editing tasks under a single multimodal instruction paradigm and is jointly trained across them. Extensive experiments demonstrate that UniVideo matches or surpasses state-of-the-art task-specific baselines in text/image-to-video generation, in-context video generation and in-context video editing. Notably, the unified design of UniVideo enables two forms of generalization. First, UniVideo supports task composition, such as combining editing with style transfer, by integrating multiple capabilities within a single instruction. Second, even without explicit training on free-form video editing, UniVideo transfers its editing capability from large-scale image editing data to this setting, handling unseen instructions such as changing the environment or altering materials within a video. Beyond these core capabilities, UniVideo also supports visual-prompt-based video generation, where the MLLM interprets visual prompts and guides the MMDiT during synthesis. To foster future research, we released our model and code. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - ух▒СИђсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсЃ│сЃёсЂ«ућЪТѕљсЂеуиежЏєсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдТюЅТюЏсЂфухљТъюсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсЂїсђЂСИ╗сЂФућ╗тЃЈсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂФжЎљсѓЅсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТюгуаћуЕХсЂДсЂ»сђЂтІЋућ╗сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂФух▒СИђсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњТІАт╝хсЂЎсѓІтцџуЏ«уџёсЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсЂѓсѓІUniVideoсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓUniVideoсЂ»сђЂТїЄуц║уљєУДБсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФтцДУдЈТеАУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕMLLM№╝ЅсЂетІЋућ╗ућЪТѕљсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФDiT№╝ѕMMDiT№╝ЅсѓњухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсЂЪсЃЄсЃЦсѓбсЃФсѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃаУеГУеѕсѓњТјАућесЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«УеГУеѕсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂMLLMсЂ«тЁЃсђЁсЂ«сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕућЪТѕљУЃйтіЏсЂїС┐ЮТїЂсЂЋсѓїсђЂУцЄжЏЉсЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФТїЄуц║сЂ«ТГБуб║сЂфУДБжЄѕсЂїтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂфсѓісђЂућЪТѕљсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсЃ│сЃёсЂ«УдќУдџуџёСИђУ▓ФТђДсЂїуХГТїЂсЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂёсЂдсђЂUniVideoсЂ»тцџТДўсЂфтІЋућ╗ућЪТѕљсЂісѓѕсЂ│уиежЏєсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сѓњтЇўСИђсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФТїЄуц║сЃЉсЃЕсЃђсѓцсЃасЂ«СИІсЂДух▒СИђсЂЌсђЂсЂЮсѓїсѓЅсѓњтЁ▒тљїсЂДУеЊуи┤сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓт║Ѓу»ёсЂфт«ЪжеЊсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂUniVideoсЂ»сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕ/ућ╗тЃЈсЂІсѓЅтІЋућ╗сЂИсЂ«ућЪТѕљсђЂТќЄУёѕтєЁтІЋућ╗ућЪТѕљсђЂТќЄУёѕтєЁтІЋућ╗уиежЏєсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсђЂТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»уЅ╣тїќтъІсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЂетљїуГЅсЂЙсЂЪсЂ»сЂЮсѓїсѓњСИітЏъсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓуЅ╣сЂФсђЂUniVideoсЂ«ух▒СИђУеГУеѕсЂ»2сЂцсЂ«тйбТЁІсЂ«СИђУѕгтїќсѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуггСИђсЂФсђЂUniVideoсЂ»сђЂтЇўСИђсЂ«ТїЄуц║тєЁсЂДУцЄТЋ░сЂ«ТЕЪУЃйсѓњух▒тљѕсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂуиежЏєсЂесѓ╣сѓ┐сѓцсЃФУ╗бжђЂсѓњухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсѓІсЂфсЂЕсЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂ«ТДІТѕљсѓњсѓхсЃЮсЃ╝сЃѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуггС║їсЂФсђЂУЄфућ▒тйбт╝ЈсЂ«тІЋућ╗уиежЏєсЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІТўјуц║уџёсЂфУеЊуи┤сЂїсЂфсЂЈсЂдсѓѓсђЂUniVideoсЂ»тцДУдЈТеАсЂфућ╗тЃЈуиежЏєсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂІсѓЅсЂЊсЂ«УеГт«џсЂФуиежЏєУЃйтіЏсѓњуД╗УАїсЂЌсђЂуњ░тбЃсЂ«тцЅТЏ┤сѓётІЋућ╗тєЁсЂ«у┤аТЮљсЂ«тцЅТЏ┤сЂесЂёсЂБсЂЪТюфУдІсЂ«ТїЄуц║сЂФт»Йт┐юсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«сѓ│сѓбТЕЪУЃйсЂФтіасЂѕсЂдсђЂUniVideoсЂ»УдќУдџсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈтІЋућ╗ућЪТѕљсѓѓсѓхсЃЮсЃ╝сЃѕсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂMLLMсЂїУдќУдџсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсѓњУДБжЄѕсЂЌсђЂтљѕТѕљСИГсЂФMMDiTсѓњсѓгсѓцсЃЅсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓт░єТЮЦсЂ«уаћуЕХсѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂесѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсѓњтЁгжќІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - UniVideoсЂ»сђЂтІЋућ╗сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсЃ│сЃёсЂ«ућЪТѕљсЂеуиежЏєсѓњуЏ«уџёсЂесЂЌсЂЪух▒СИђсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂMLLMсЂеMMDiTсѓњухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсЂЪсЃЄсЃЦсѓбсЃФсѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃаУеГУеѕсѓњТјАућесђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂУцЄжЏЉсЂфТїЄуц║сЂ«УДБжЄѕсЂеУдќУдџуџёСИђУ▓ФТђДсѓњуХГТїЂсЂЌсЂцсЂцсђЂтІЋућ╗ућЪТѕљсѓёуиежЏєсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сѓњух▒СИђуџёсЂФУеЊуи┤сђѓт«ЪжеЊухљТъюсЂДсЂ»сђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕ/ућ╗тЃЈсЂІсѓЅтІЋућ╗сЂИсЂ«ућЪТѕљсѓёТќЄУёѕтєЁуиежЏєсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂуиежЏєсЂесѓ╣сѓ┐сѓцсЃФУ╗бжђЂсЂ«ух▒тљѕсѓёТюфУдІсЂ«ТїЄуц║сЂИсЂ«т»Йт┐юсѓѓтЈ»УЃйсђѓУдќУдџсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈућЪТѕљсѓѓсѓхсЃЮсЃ╝сЃѕсЂЌсђЂсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂесѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂ»тЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] LLaVA-3D: A Simple yet Effective Pathway to Empowering LMMs with 3D-awareness, Chenming Zhu+, ICCV'25, 2024.09
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, Supervised-FineTuning (SFT), InstructionTuning, MultiModal, Reasoning, PositionalEncoding, OpenWeight, OpenSource, PostTraining, Selected Papers/Blogs, ICCV, VisionLanguageModel, 3D (Scene), SpatialUnderstanding, KeyPoint Notes, Grounding
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.18125 # Authors - Chenming Zhu - Tai Wang - Wenwei Zhang - Jiangmiao Pang - Xihui Liu # Abstract - Recent advancements in Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) have greatly enhanced their proficiency in 2D visual understanding tasks, enabling them to effectively process and understand images and videos. However, the development of LMMs with 3D scene understanding capabilities has been hindered by the lack of large-scale 3D vision-language datasets and powerful 3D encoders. In this paper, we introduce a simple yet effective framework called LLaVA-3D. Leveraging the strong 2D visual understanding priors from LLaVA, our LLaVA-3D efficiently adapts LLaVA for 3D scene understanding without compromising 2D understanding capabilities. To achieve this, we utilize the 3D position embeddings to enhance the 2D CLIP Patches with 3D spatial context information and construct 3D patches. By integrating the 3D position embeddings into 2D LMMs and employing joint 2D and 3D vision-language instruction tuning, we establish a unified architecture for both 2D visual understanding and 3D scene understanding. In contrast to previous 3D LMMs, LLaVA-3D supports decoding accurate 3D spatial perception outputs, e.g., 3D bounding boxes, directly from these 3D patches, without relying on the time-consuming off-the-shelf 3D segmentors. Experimental results show that LLaVA-3D converges 3.5x faster than existing 3D LMMs when trained on 3D vision-language datasets. Moreover, LLaVA-3D not only achieves state-of-the-art performance across various 3D tasks but also maintains comparable 2D visual understanding and vision-language conversation capabilities with LLaVA. # Translation (by gpt-5-nano) - ТюђУ┐ЉсЂ«тцДУдЈТеАсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕLMMs№╝ЅсЂ«жђ▓т▒ЋсЂ»сђЂ2DУдќУдџуљєУДБсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІУЃйтіЏсѓњтцДт╣ЁсЂФтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсђЂућ╗тЃЈсѓётІЋућ╗сѓњті╣ТъюуџёсЂФтЄдуљєсЃ╗уљєУДБсЂДсЂЇсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂФсЂЌсЂЪсђѓсЂЌсЂІсЂЌсђЂ3DсѓисЃ╝сЃ│уљєУДБТЕЪУЃйсѓњтѓЎсЂѕсЂЪLMMсЂ«жќІуЎ║сЂ»сђЂтцДУдЈТеАсЂф3DсЃЊсѓИсЃДсЃ│-УеђУфъсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂ«СИЇУХ│сЂет╝итіЏсЂф3DсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЂ«ТгатдѓсЂФсѓѕсѓітдесЂњсѓЅсѓїсЂдсЂЇсЂЪсђѓТюгУФќТќЄсЂДсЂ»сђЂLLaVA-3DсЂетЉ╝сЂ░сѓїсѓІсђЂсѓисЃ│сЃЌсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓісЂфсЂїсѓЅТюЅті╣сЂфсЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЎсѓІсђѓLLaVAсЂ«т╝итіЏсЂф2DУдќУдџуљєУДБсЂ«тЅЇТЈљуЪЦУГўсѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсЂдсђЂLLaVA-3DсЂ»2DуљєУДБУЃйтіЏсѓњТљЇсЂфсЂєсЂЊсЂесЂфсЂЈ3DсѓисЃ╝сЃ│уљєУДБсЂИті╣ујЄуџёсЂФжЂЕт┐юсЂЎсѓІсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂ3DСйЇуй«тЪІсѓЂУЙ╝сЂ┐сѓњтѕЕућесЂЌсЂд2DсЂ«CLIPсЃЉсЃЃсЃЂсѓњ3DуЕ║жќЊТЃЁта▒сЂДт╝итїќсЂЌсђЂ3DсЃЉсЃЃсЃЂсѓњТДІу»ЅсЂЎсѓІсђѓ3DСйЇуй«тЪІсѓЂУЙ╝сЂ┐сѓњ2D LMMsсЂФух▒тљѕсЂЌсђЂ2DсЂе3DсЂ«УдќУдџУеђУфъТїЄуц║сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњтЁ▒тљїсЂДУАїсЂєсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂ2DУдќУдџуљєУДБсЂе3DсѓисЃ╝сЃ│уљєУДБсЂ«СИАТќ╣сЂФт»Йт┐юсЂЎсѓІух▒СИђуџёсЂфсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсѓњуб║уФІсЂЎсѓІсђѓтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«3D LMMсЂесЂ»т»ЙуЁДуџёсЂФсђЂLLaVA-3DсЂ»сЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«3DсЃЉсЃЃсЃЂсЂІсѓЅуЏ┤ТјЦсђЂТГБуб║сЂф3DуЕ║жќЊУфЇУГўтЄ║тіЏ№╝ѕСЙІ№╝џ3DтбЃуЋїсЃюсЃЃсѓ»сѓ╣№╝ЅсѓњсЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњсѓхсЃЮсЃ╝сЃѕсЂЎсѓІсђѓТЎѓжќЊсЂ«сЂІсЂІсѓІсѓфсЃЋсЃ╗сѓХсЃ╗сѓисѓДсЃФсЃЋсЂ«3Dсѓ╗сѓ░сЃАсЃ│сѓ┐сЃ╝сЂФСЙЮтГўсЂЌсЂфсЂёсђѓт«ЪжеЊухљТъюсЂ»сђЂ3DсЃЊсѓИсЃДсЃ│-УеђУфъсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂДУеЊуи┤сЂЌсЂЪта┤тљѕсђЂLLaVA-3DсЂїТЌбтГўсЂ«3D LMMсѓѕсѓі3.5тђЇжђЪсЂЈтЈјТЮЪсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂLLaVA-3DсЂ»сЂЋсЂЙсЂќсЂЙсЂф3Dсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЎсѓІсЂасЂЉсЂДсЂфсЂЈсђЂLLaVAсЂетљїуГЅсЂ«2DУдќУдџуљєУДБсЂісѓѕсЂ│УдќУдџУеђУфът»ЙУЕ▒ТЕЪУЃйсѓѓуХГТїЂсЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - LLaVA-3DсЂ»сђЂ3DсѓисЃ╝сЃ│уљєУДБсЂФт»Йт┐юсЂЎсѓІТќ░сЂЪсЂфсЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсђЂ2DУдќУдџуљєУДБсЂ«уЪЦУГўсѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсЂцсЂцсђЂ3DСйЇуй«тЪІсѓЂУЙ╝сЂ┐сѓњух▒тљѕсђѓ2D CLIPсЃЉсЃЃсЃЂсѓњ3DуЕ║жќЊТЃЁта▒сЂДт╝итїќсЂЌсђЂ2DсЂе3DсЂ«тЁ▒тљїсЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњУАїсЂєсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂУ┐ЁжђЪсЂІсЂцТГБуб║сЂф3DУфЇУГўсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсђѓт«ЪжеЊсЂДсЂ»сђЂТЌбтГўсЂ«3DсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓѕсѓісѓѓ3.5тђЇжђЪсЂЈтЈјТЮЪсЂЌсђЂ3Dсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂДсЂ«ТюђтЁѕуФ»ТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂфсЂїсѓЅсђЂ2DТЕЪУЃйсѓѓС┐ЮТїЂсЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ

maintainer-comment-text.p:
3DсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІspatial understandingсЂ«УЃйтіЏсѓњТїЂсЂцVLMсЂДсђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«тЄ║тіЏсЂасЂЉсЂДсЂфсЂЈсђЂ3DсЂ«bounding boxсѓњтЄ║тіЏсЂЎсѓІт░ѓућесЂ«сЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсѓњТїЂсЂцсђѓ
2DсЂ«CLIPсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«image encoderсЂФсѓѕсѓІТЃЁта▒сѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсЂцсЂцсђЂ2D patchсЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂд3DсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІСйЇуй«ТЃЁта▒(depth№╝Ѕсѓњ3D positional encodingсѓњжђџсЂўсЂдтіасЂѕсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂД3D patchсѓњСйюТѕљсЂЌтЁЦтіЏсЂесЂЌсЂдТ┤╗ућесђѓ3DсЂ«grounding taskсѓњТЅ▒сЂєgrounding decoderсѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂД3DуљєУДБсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІУЃйтіЏсѓњжєИТѕљсЂЎсѓІсђѓтГду┐њсЂ»2stageсЂДсђЂТюђтѕЮсЂ«stageсЂДсЂ»сђЂ2D, 3DтЈїТќ╣сЂ«УЃйтіЏсѓњтљїТЎѓсЂФтГду┐њсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФ2D, 3DсЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐СИАТќ╣сѓњућесЂёсЂдсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњSFTсЂЎсѓІсђѓсЂЮсЂ«тЙїgrounding decoderсЂ»тЅЇТ«хсЂ«SFTсЂДсЂЋтГду┐њсЂЌсЂЇсѓїсЂфсЂёсЂЪсѓЂсђЂgrounding decoderС╗ЦтцќсЂ«сЃбсѓИсЃЦсЃ╝сЃФсЂ»freezeсЂЌсЂдсђЂ3D groundingсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂДdecoderсЂеlocation tokenсѓњтГду┐њсЂЎсѓІсѓЅсЂЌсЂёсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂ2D, 3DсѓисЃ╝сЃ│сЂ«уљєУДБтіЏсѓњТљЇсЂфсѓЈсЂџсђЂgroundingсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњжФўсѓЂсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Epona: Autoregressive Diffusion World Model for Autonomous Driving, Kaiwen Zhang+, ICCV'25, 2025.06
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, Transformer, DiffusionModel, LongSequence, OpenWeight, ICCV, WorldModels, 4D (Video)
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.24113 # Authors - Kaiwen Zhang - Zhenyu Tang - Xiaotao Hu - Xingang Pan - Xiaoyang Guo - Yuan Liu - Jingwei Huang - Li Yuan - Qian Zhang - Xiao-Xiao Long - Xun Cao - Wei Yin # Abstract - Diffusion models have demonstrated exceptional visual quality in video generation, making them promising for autonomous driving world modeling. However, existing video diffusion-based world models struggle with flexible-length, long-horizon predictions and integrating trajectory planning. This is because conventional video diffusion models rely on global joint distribution modeling of fixed-length frame sequences rather than sequentially constructing localized distributions at each timestep. In this work, we propose Epona, an autoregressive diffusion world model that enables localized spatiotemporal distribution modeling through two key innovations: 1) Decoupled spatiotemporal factorization that separates temporal dynamics modeling from fine-grained future world generation, and 2) Modular trajectory and video prediction that seamlessly integrate motion planning with visual modeling in an end-to-end framework. Our architecture enables high-resolution, long-duration generation while introducing a novel chain-of-forward training strategy to address error accumulation in autoregressive loops. Experimental results demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with 7.4\% FVD improvement and minutes longer prediction duration compared to prior works. The learned world model further serves as a real-time motion planner, outperforming strong end-to-end planners on NAVSIM benchmarks. Code will be publicly available at \href{https://github.com/Kevin-thu/Epona/}{https://github.com/Kevin-thu/Epona/}. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - ТІАТЋБсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂтІЋућ╗ућЪТѕљсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдтёфсѓїсЂЪУдќУдџтЊЂУ│фсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂУЄфтЙІжЂІУ╗бсЂ«СИќуЋїсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдТюЅТюЏсЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЌсЂІсЂЌсђЂТЌбтГўсЂ«тІЋућ╗ТІАТЋБсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«СИќуЋїсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂТЪћУ╗ЪсЂфжЋисЂЋсЂ«жЋиТюЪС║ѕТИгсѓёУ╗їжЂЊУеѕућ╗сЂ«ух▒тљѕсЂФУІдті┤сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂ»сђЂтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«тІЋућ╗ТІАТЋБсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂїтЏ║т«џжЋисЂ«сЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасѓисЃ╝сѓ▒сЃ│сѓ╣сЂ«сѓ░сЃГсЃ╝сЃљсЃФсЂфтљїТЎѓтѕєтИЃсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФСЙЮтГўсЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсђЂтљёсѓ┐сѓцсЃасѓ╣сЃєсЃЃсЃЌсЂДт▒ђТЅђуџёсЂфтѕєтИЃсѓњжђљТгАТДІу»ЅсЂЎсѓІсЂ«сЂДсЂ»сЂфсЂёсЂІсѓЅсЂДсЂЎсђѓТюгуаћуЕХсЂДсЂ»сђЂEponaсЂесЂёсЂєУЄфтЏътИ░тъІТІАТЋБСИќуЋїсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂС╗ЦСИІсЂ«2сЂцсЂ«жЄЇУдЂсЂфжЮЕТќ░сѓњжђџсЂўсЂдт▒ђТЅђуџёсЂфТЎѓуЕ║жќЊтѕєтИЃсЃбсЃЄсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ1) ТЎѓжќЊуџёсЃђсѓцсЃісЃЪсѓ»сѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сѓњтЙ«у┤░сЂфТюфТЮЦсЂ«СИќуЋїућЪТѕљсЂІсѓЅтѕєжЏбсЂЎсѓІсЃЄсѓФсЃЃсЃЌсЃФтъІТЎѓуЕ║жќЊтЏатГљтѕєУДБсђЂ2) тІЋСйюУеѕућ╗сЂеУдќУдџсЃбсЃЄсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сѓњсѓесЃ│сЃЅсЃёсЃ╝сѓесЃ│сЃЅсЂ«сЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсѓисЃ╝сЃасЃгсѓ╣сЂФух▒тљѕсЂЎсѓІсЃбсѓИсЃЦсЃЕсЃ╝У╗їжЂЊсЂісѓѕсЂ│тІЋућ╗С║ѕТИгсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«сѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂ»сђЂжФўУДБтЃЈт║дсЂІсЂцжЋиТЎѓжќЊсЂ«ућЪТѕљсѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЎсѓІсЂетЁ▒сЂФсђЂУЄфтЏътИ░сЃФсЃ╝сЃЌсЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІсѓесЃЕсЃ╝сЂ«УЊёуЕЇсЂФт»ЙтЄдсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«Тќ░сЂЌсЂётЅЇТќ╣тГду┐њТѕдуЋЦсѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓт«ЪжеЊухљТъюсЂ»сђЂ7.4\%сЂ«FVDТћ╣тќёсЂеС╗ЦтЅЇсЂ«СйютЊЂсЂФТ»ћсЂ╣сЂдТЋ░тѕєжќЊсЂ«жЋисЂёС║ѕТИгТюЪжќЊсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсЂЌсђЂТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтГду┐њсЂЌсЂЪСИќуЋїсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂсЃфсѓбсЃФсѓ┐сѓцсЃасЂ«тІЋСйюсЃЌсЃЕсЃ│сЃісЃ╝сЂесЂЌсЂдсѓѓТЕЪУЃйсЂЌсђЂNAVSIMсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдт╝итіЏсЂфсѓесЃ│сЃЅсЃёсЃ╝сѓесЃ│сЃЅсЃЌсЃЕсЃ│сЃісЃ╝сѓњСИітЏъсЂБсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂ»тЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсѓІС║ѕт«џсЂДсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - EponaсЂесЂёсЂєУЄфтЏътИ░тъІТІАТЋБСИќуЋїсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсђЂжЋиТюЪС║ѕТИгсЂеУ╗їжЂЊУеѕућ╗сЂ«ух▒тљѕсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсђѓсЃЄсѓФсЃЃсЃЌсЃФтъІтЏатГљтѕєУДБсЂФсѓѕсѓіт▒ђТЅђуџёсЂфТЎѓуЕ║жќЊтѕєтИЃсѓњсЃбсЃЄсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂЌсђЂсѓесЃ│сЃЅсЃёсЃ╝сѓесЃ│сЃЅсЂДтІЋСйюУеѕућ╗сЂеУдќУдџсЃбсЃЄсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сѓњух▒тљѕсђѓт«ЪжеЊсЂФсѓѕсѓі7.4%сЂ«FVDТћ╣тќёсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсђЂТЋ░тѕєжќЊсЂ«жЋиТюЪС║ѕТИгсЂїтЈ»УЃйсђѓтГду┐њсЂЌсЂЪсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сЃфсѓбсЃФсѓ┐сѓцсЃатІЋСйюсЃЌсЃЕсЃ│сЃісЃ╝сЂесЂЌсЂдсѓѓтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЎсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Nemotron-Flash: Towards Latency-Optimal Hybrid Small Language Models, Yonggan Fu+, arXiv'25, 2025.11
paper-entry.tags: EfficiencyImprovement, NLP, LanguageModel, Transformer, SmallModel, OpenWeight, Architecture, read-later, Selected Papers/Blogs, EvolutionaryAlgorithm, Latency, Operator
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.18890 # Authors - Yonggan Fu - Xin Dong - Shizhe Diao - Matthijs Van keirsbilck - Hanrong Ye - Wonmin Byeon - Yashaswi Karnati - Lucas Liebenwein - Hannah Zhang - Nikolaus Binder - Maksim Khadkevich - Alexander Keller - Jan Kautz - Yingyan Celine Lin - Pavlo Molchanov # Abstract - Efficient deployment of small language models (SLMs) is essential for numerous real-world applications with stringent latency constraints. While previous work on SLM design has primarily focused on reducing the number of parameters to achieve parameter-optimal SLMs, parameter efficiency does not necessarily translate into proportional real-device speed-ups. This work aims to identify the key determinants of SLMs' real-device latency and offer generalizable principles and methodologies for SLM design and training when real-device latency is the primary consideration. Specifically, we identify two central architectural factors: depth-width ratios and operator choices. The former is crucial for small-batch-size latency, while the latter affects both latency and large-batch-size throughput. In light of this, we first study latency-optimal depth-width ratios, with the key finding that although deep-thin models generally achieve better accuracy under the same parameter budget, they may not lie on the accuracy-latency trade-off frontier. Next, we explore emerging efficient attention alternatives to evaluate their potential as candidate building operators. Using the identified promising operators, we construct an evolutionary search framework to automatically discover latency-optimal combinations of these operators within hybrid SLMs, thereby advancing the accuracy-latency frontier. In addition to architectural improvements, we further enhance SLM training using a weight normalization technique that enables more effective weight updates and improves final convergence. Combining these methods, we introduce a new family of hybrid SLMs, called Nemotron-Flash, which significantly advances the accuracy-efficiency frontier of state-of-the-art SLMs, e.g., achieving over +5.5% average accuracy, 1.3x/1.9x lower latency, and 18.7x/45.6x higher throughput compared to Qwen3-1.7B/0.6B, respectively. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - т░ЈтъІУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕSLM№╝ЅсЂ«ті╣ујЄуџёсЂфт▒ЋжќІсЂ»сђЂтј│сЂЌсЂёсЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓитѕХу┤ёсѓњТїЂсЂцтцџсЂЈсЂ«т«ЪСИќуЋїсЂ«сѓбсЃЌсЃфсѓ▒сЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂФсЂесЂБсЂдСИЇтЈ»ТгасЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂЙсЂДсЂ«SLMУеГУеѕсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІуаћуЕХсЂ»сђЂСИ╗сЂФсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐ТЋ░сЂ«тЅіТИЏсЂФуёдуѓ╣сѓњтйЊсЂдсђЂсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐ТюђжЂЕсЂфSLMсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂФТ│етіЏсЂЌсЂдсЂЇсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсЂїсђЂсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐ті╣ујЄсЂїт«ЪжџЏсЂ«сЃЄсЃљсѓцсѓ╣сЂДсЂ«жђЪт║дтљЉСИісЂФТ»ћСЙІсЂЎсѓІсѓЈсЂЉсЂДсЂ»сЂѓсѓісЂЙсЂЏсѓЊсђѓТюгуаћуЕХсЂ«уЏ«уџёсЂ»сђЂSLMсЂ«т«ЪжџЏсЂ«сЃЄсЃљсѓцсѓ╣сЂ«сЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓисЂ«СИ╗УдЂсЂфТ▒║т«џУдЂтЏасѓњуЅ╣т«џсЂЌсђЂсЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓисЂїСИ╗сЂфУђЃТЁ«С║ІжаЁсЂДсЂѓсѓІта┤тљѕсЂ«SLMУеГУеѕсЂеУеЊуи┤сЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«СИђУѕгтїќтЈ»УЃйсЂфтјЪтЅЄсЂеТќ╣Т│ЋУФќсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсЂЎсђѓтЁиСйЊуџёсЂФсЂ»сђЂТи▒сЂЋсЂет╣ЁсЂ«Т»ћујЄсђЂсѓфсЃџсЃгсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«жЂИТіъсЂесЂёсЂє2сЂцсЂ«СИГт┐ЃуџёсЂфсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБУдЂтЏасѓњуЅ╣т«џсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтЅЇУђЁсЂ»т░ЈсЃљсЃЃсЃЂсѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓисЂФсЂесЂБсЂджЄЇУдЂсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂтЙїУђЁсЂ»сЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓисЂетцДсЃљсЃЃсЃЂсѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сѓ╣сЃФсЃ╝сЃЌсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«СИАТќ╣сЂФтй▒жЪ┐сѓњСИјсЂѕсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓњУИЈсЂЙсЂѕсЂдсђЂсЂЙсЂџсЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓиТюђжЂЕсЂфТи▒сЂЋсЂет╣ЁсЂ«Т»ћујЄсѓњуаћуЕХсЂЌсђЂтљїсЂўсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐С║ѕу«ЌсЂ«сѓѓсЂесЂДсЂ»сђЂТи▒сЂЈу┤░сЂёсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂїСИђУѕгуџёсЂФсѓѕсѓіУЅ»сЂёу▓Йт║дсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЎсѓІсѓѓсЂ«сЂ«сђЂу▓Йт║дсЂесЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓисЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃЅсѓфсЃЋсЃЋсЃГсЃ│сЃєсѓБсѓбсЂФсЂ»СйЇуй«сЂЌсЂфсЂётЈ»УЃйТђДсЂїсЂѓсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњУдІтЄ║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓТгАсЂФсђЂТќ░сЂЪсЂФуЎ╗та┤сЂЌсЂЪті╣ујЄуџёсЂфсѓбсЃєсЃ│сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂ«С╗БТЏ┐ТЅІТ«хсѓњТјбТ▒ѓсЂЌсђЂтђЎУБюсЂесЂЌсЂдсЂ«сѓфсЃџсЃгсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«тЈ»УЃйТђДсѓњУЕЋСЙАсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЅ╣т«џсЂЋсѓїсЂЪТюЅТюЏсЂфсѓфсЃџсЃгсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњтѕЕућесЂЌсЂдсђЂсЃЈсѓцсЃќсЃфсЃЃсЃЅSLMтєЁсЂ«сЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«сѓфсЃџсЃгсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«сЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓиТюђжЂЕсЂфухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсѓњУЄфтІЋуџёсЂФуЎ║УдІсЂЎсѓІжђ▓тїќуџёТцюу┤бсЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сѓњТДІу»ЅсЂЌсђЂу▓Йт║дсЂесЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓисЂ«сЃЋсЃГсЃ│сЃєсѓБсѓбсѓњжђ▓т▒ЋсЂЋсЂЏсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂ«Тћ╣тќёсЂФтіасЂѕсЂдсђЂсѓѕсѓіті╣ТъюуџёсЂфжЄЇсЂ┐сЂ«ТЏ┤Тќ░сѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЌсђЂТюђухѓуџёсЂфтЈјТЮЪсѓњТћ╣тќёсЂЎсѓІжЄЇсЂ┐ТГБУдЈтїќТіђУАЊсѓњућесЂёсЂдSLMсЂ«УеЊуи┤сѓњсЂЋсѓЅсЂФтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«Тќ╣Т│ЋсѓњухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂNemotron-FlashсЂетЉ╝сЂ░сѓїсѓІТќ░сЂЌсЂёсЃЈсѓцсЃќсЃфсЃЃсЃЅSLMсЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсђЂТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«SLMсЂ«у▓Йт║дсЂеті╣ујЄсЂ«сЃЋсЃГсЃ│сЃєсѓБсѓбсѓњтцДт╣ЁсЂФжђ▓т▒ЋсЂЋсЂЏсђЂСЙІсЂѕсЂ░сђЂQwen3-1.7B/0.6BсЂеТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЌсЂдт╣│тЮЄу▓Йт║дсЂї+5.5%С╗ЦСИітљЉСИісЂЌсђЂсЃгсѓцсЃєсЃ│сѓисЂїсЂЮсѓїсЂъсѓї1.3тђЇ/1.9тђЇСйјСИІсЂЌсђЂсѓ╣сЃФсЃ╝сЃЌсЃЃсЃѕсЂїсЂЮсѓїсЂъсѓї18.7тђЇ/45.6тђЇтљЉСИісЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] LightAgent: Mobile Agentic Foundation Models, Yangqin Jiang+, arXiv'25, 2025.10
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, Supervised-FineTuning (SFT), ReinforcementLearning, AIAgents, SyntheticData, MultiModal, Reasoning, SmallModel, OpenWeight, ComputerUse, PostTraining, VisionLanguageModel, One-Line Notes, GUI
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.22009 # Authors - Yangqin Jiang - Chao Huang # Abstract - With the advancement of multimodal large language models (MLLMs), building GUI agent systems has become an increasingly promising direction-especially for mobile platforms, given their rich app ecosystems and intuitive touch interactions. Yet mobile GUI agents face a critical dilemma: truly on-device models (4B or smaller) lack sufficient performance, while capable models (starting from 7B) are either too large for mobile deployment or prohibitively costly (e.g., cloud-only closed-source MLLMs). To resolve this, we propose LightAgent, a mobile agentic foundation model solution that leverages device-cloud collaboration to tap the cost-efficiency of on-device models and the high capability of cloud models, while avoiding their drawbacks. Specifically, LightAgent enhances Qwen2.5-VL-3B via two-stage SFT->GRPO training on synthetic GUI data for strong decision-making, integrates an efficient long-reasoning mechanism to utilize historical interactions under tight resources, and defaults to on-device execution-only escalating challenging subtasks to the cloud via real-time complexity assessment. Experiments on the online AndroidLab benchmark and diverse apps show LightAgent matches or nears larger models, with a significant reduction in cloud costs. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФтцДУдЈТеАУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕMLLM№╝ЅсЂ«жђ▓т▒ЋсЂФС╝┤сЂёсђЂGUIсѓесЃ╝сѓИсѓДсЃ│сЃѕсѓисѓ╣сЃєсЃасЂ«ТДІу»ЅсЂ»сЂЙсЂЎсЂЙсЂЎТюЅТюЏсЂфТќ╣тљЉТђДсЂесЂфсЂБсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЅ╣сЂФсђЂУ▒іт»їсЂфсѓбсЃЌсЃфсѓесѓ│сѓисѓ╣сЃєсЃасЂеуЏ┤ТёЪуџёсЂфсѓ┐сЃЃсЃЂсѓцсЃ│сѓ┐сЃЕсѓ»сѓисЃДсЃ│сѓњтѓЎсЂѕсЂЪсЃбсЃљсѓцсЃФсЃЌсЃЕсЃЃсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃасЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЌсЂІсЂЌсђЂсЃбсЃљсѓцсЃФGUIсѓесЃ╝сѓИсѓДсЃ│сЃѕсЂ»жЄЇУдЂсЂфсѓИсЃгсЃ│сЃъсЂФуЏ┤жЮбсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуюЪсЂ«сѓфсЃ│сЃЄсЃљсѓцсѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕ4BС╗ЦСИІ№╝ЅсЂ»ТђДУЃйсЂїСИЇтЇЂтѕєсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂСИђТќ╣сЂДТюЅУЃйсЂфсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕ7BС╗ЦСИісЂІсѓЅтДІсЂЙсѓІ№╝ЅсЂ»сЃбсЃљсѓцсЃФт▒ЋжќІсЂФсЂ»тцДсЂЇсЂЎсЂјсѓІсЂІсђЂсЂЙсЂЪсЂ»сѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕсЂїжФўсЂЎсЂјсѓІ№╝ѕСЙІ№╝џсѓ»сЃЕсѓдсЃЅт░ѓућесЂ«сѓ»сЃГсЃ╝сѓ║сЃЅсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣MLLM№╝ЅсЂесЂёсЂєтЋЈжАїсЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓњУДБТ▒║сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»LightAgentсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂ»сђЂсЃЄсЃљсѓцсѓ╣сЂесѓ»сЃЕсѓдсЃЅсЂ«тЇћтіЏсѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсЂдсђЂсѓфсЃ│сЃЄсЃљсѓцсѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕті╣ујЄсЂесѓ»сЃЕсѓдсЃЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«жФўсЂёУЃйтіЏсѓњт╝ЋсЂЇтЄ║сЂЎсЃбсЃљсѓцсЃФсѓесЃ╝сѓИсѓДсЃ│сЃѕтЪ║уЏцсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сѓйсЃфсЃЦсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЙсЂЪсђЂсЂЮсѓїсѓЅсЂ«Тгауѓ╣сѓњтЏъжЂ┐сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтЁиСйЊуџёсЂФсЂ»сђЂLightAgentсЂ»Qwen2.5-VL-3Bсѓњт╝итіЏсЂфТёЈТђЮТ▒║т«џсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФтљѕТѕљGUIсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІС║їТ«хжџјсЂ«SFTРєњGRPOсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂФсѓѕсѓіт╝итїќсЂЌсђЂжЎљсѓЅсѓїсЂЪсЃфсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«СИІсЂДт▒ЦТГ┤сѓцсЃ│сѓ┐сЃЕсѓ»сѓисЃДсЃ│сѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЎсѓІті╣ујЄуџёсЂфжЋиТюЪТјеУФќсЃАсѓФсЃІсѓ║сЃасѓњух▒тљѕсЂЌсђЂсЃфсѓбсЃФсѓ┐сѓцсЃасЂ«УцЄжЏЉТђДУЕЋСЙАсѓњжђџсЂўсЂдТїЉТѕдуџёсЂфсѓхсЃќсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сѓњсѓ»сЃЕсѓдсЃЅсЂФсѓесѓ╣сѓФсЃгсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂфсЂЈсђЂсѓфсЃ│сЃЄсЃљсѓцсѓ╣сЂ«сЂ┐сЂДт«ЪУАїсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњсЃЄсЃЋсѓЕсЃФсЃѕсЂесЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсѓфсЃ│сЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЂ«AndroidLabсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂісѓѕсЂ│тцџТДўсЂфсѓбсЃЌсЃфсЂДсЂ«т«ЪжеЊсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂLightAgentсЂ»тцДУдЈТеАсЂфсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЙсЂЪсЂ»У┐ЉсЂЦсЂЇсђЂсѓ»сЃЕсѓдсЃЅсѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕсѓњУЉЌсЂЌсЂЈтЅіТИЏсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - LightAgentсЂ»сђЂсЃбсЃљсѓцсЃФсЃЌсЃЕсЃЃсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃатљЉсЂЉсЂФсЃЄсЃљсѓцсѓ╣сЂесѓ»сЃЕсѓдсЃЅсЂ«тЇћтіЏсѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсЂЪGUIсѓесЃ╝сѓИсѓДсЃ│сЃѕсѓисѓ╣сЃєсЃасѓњТЈљТАѕсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂсѓфсЃЋсЃЕсѓцсЃ│ТђДУЃйсЂесѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕті╣ујЄсѓњСИАуФІсЂЌсђЂт╝итїќсЂЋсѓїсЂЪС║їТ«хжџјсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњжђџсЂўсЂджФўсЂёТёЈТђЮТ▒║т«џУЃйтіЏсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсђѓт«ЪжеЊсѓњжђџсЂўсЂдтцДУдЈТеАсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЎсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂсѓ»сЃЕсѓдсЃЅсѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕсѓњтцДт╣ЁсЂФтЅіТИЏсђѓ

paper-entry.tags: Pretraining, NLP, LanguageModel, Transformer, Supervised-FineTuning (SFT), ReinforcementLearning, OpenWeight, SSM (StateSpaceModel), MoE(Mixture-of-Experts), PostTraining, Hybrid

Joseph Jennings - Joyjit Daw - Julien Veron Vialard - Junkeun Yi - Jupinder Parmar - Kai Xu - Kan Zhu - Kari Briski - Katherine Cheung - Katherine Luna - Keshav Santhanam - Kevin Shih - Kezhi Kong - Khushi Bhardwaj - Krishna C. Puvvada - Krzysztof Pawelec - Kumar Anik - Lawrence McAfee - Laya Sleiman - Leon Derczynski - Li Ding - Lucas Liebenwein - Luis Vega - Maanu Grover - Maarten Van Segbroeck - Maer Rodrigues de Melo - Makesh Narsimhan Sreedhar - Manoj Kilaru - Maor Ashkenazi - Marc Romeijn - Mark Cai - Markus Kliegl - Maryam Moosaei - Matvei Novikov - Mehrzad Samadi - Melissa Corpuz - Mengru Wang - Meredith Price - Michael Boone - Michael Evans - Miguel Martinez - Mike Chrzanowski - Mohammad Shoeybi - Mostofa Patwary - Nabin Mulepati - Natalie Hereth - Nave Assaf - Negar Habibi - Neta Zmora - Netanel Haber - Nicola Sessions - Nidhi Bhatia - Nikhil Jukar - Nikki Pope - Nikolai Ludwig - Nima Tajbakhsh - Nirmal Juluru - Oleksii Hrinchuk - Oleksii Kuchaiev - Olivier Delalleau - Oluwatobi Olabiyi - Omer Ullman Argov - Ouye Xie - Parth Chadha - Pasha Shamis - Pavlo Molchanov - Pawel Morkisz - Peter Dykas - Peter Jin - Pinky Xu - Piotr Januszewski - Pranav Prashant Thombre - Prasoon Varshney - Pritam Gundecha - Qing Miao - Rabeeh Karimi Mahabadi - Ran El-Yaniv - Ran Zilberstein - Rasoul Shafipour - Rich Harang - Rick Izzo - Rima Shahbazyan - Rishabh Garg - Ritika Borkar - Ritu Gala - Riyad Islam - Roger Waleffe - Rohit Watve - Roi Koren - Ruoxi Zhang - Russell J. Hewett - Ryan Prenger - Ryan Timbrook - Sadegh Mahdavi - Sahil Modi - Samuel Kriman - Sanjay Kariyappa - Sanjeev Satheesh - Saori Kaji - Satish Pasumarthi - Sean Narentharen - Sean Narenthiran - Seonmyeong Bak - Sergey Kashirsky - Seth Poulos - Shahar Mor - Shanmugam Ramasamy - Shantanu Acharya - Shaona Ghosh - Sharath Turuvekere Sreenivas - Shelby Thomas - Shiqing Fan - Shreya Gopal - Shrimai Prabhumoye - Shubham Pachori - Shubham Toshniwal - Shuoyang Ding - Siddharth Singh - Simeng Sun - Smita Ithape - Somshubra Majumdar - Soumye Singhal - Stefania Alborghetti - Stephen Ge - Sugam Dipak Devare - Sumeet Kumar Barua - Suseella Panguluri - Suyog Gupta - Sweta Priyadarshi - Syeda Nahida Akter - Tan Bui - Teodor-Dumitru Ene - Terry Kong - Thanh Do - Tijmen Blankevoort - Tom Balough - Tomer Asida - Tomer Bar Natan - Tugrul Konuk - Twinkle Vashishth - Udi Karpas - Ushnish De - Vahid Noorozi - Vahid Noroozi - Venkat Srinivasan - Venmugil Elango - Vijay Korthikanti - Vitaly Kurin - Vitaly Lavrukhin - Wanli Jiang - Wasi Uddin Ahmad - Wei Du - Wei Ping - Wenfei Zhou - Will Jennings - William Zhang - Wojciech Prazuch - Xiaowei Ren - Yashaswi Karnati - Yejin Choi - Yev Meyer - Yi-Fu Wu - Yian Zhang - Ying Lin - Yonatan Geifman - Yonggan Fu - Yoshi Subara - Yoshi Suhara - Yubo Gao - Zach Moshe - Zhen Dong - Zihan Liu - Zijia Chen - Zijie Yan # Abstract - We present Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3B, a Mixture-of-Experts hybrid Mamba-Transformer language model. Nemotron 3 Nano was pretrained on 25 trillion text tokens, including more than 3 trillion new unique tokens over Nemotron 2, followed by supervised fine tuning and large-scale RL on diverse environments. Nemotron 3 Nano achieves better accuracy than our previous generation Nemotron 2 Nano while activating less than half of the parameters per forward pass. It achieves up to 3.3x higher inference throughput than similarly-sized open models like GPT-OSS-20B and Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507, while also being more accurate on popular benchmarks. Nemotron 3 Nano demonstrates enhanced agentic, reasoning, and chat abilities and supports context lengths up to 1M tokens. We release both our pretrained Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3B Base and post-trained Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3B checkpoints on Hugging Face. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂMixture-of-ExpertsсЃЈсѓцсЃќсЃфсЃЃсЃЅMamba-TransformerУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓІNemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3BсѓњуЎ║УАесЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓNemotron 3 NanoсЂ»сђЂNemotron 2сЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂд3тЁєС╗ЦСИісЂ«Тќ░сЂЌсЂёсЃдсЃІсЃ╝сѓ»сЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњтљФсѓђ25тЁєсЂ«сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂДС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсђЂсЂЮсЂ«тЙїсђЂсЂЋсЂЙсЂќсЂЙсЂфуњ░тбЃсЂДсЂ«уЏБУдќС╗ўсЂЇсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂетцДУдЈТеАсЂфт╝итїќтГду┐њсЂїУАїсѓЈсѓїсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓNemotron 3 NanoсЂ»сђЂтЅЇСИќС╗БсЂ«Nemotron 2 NanoсѓѕсѓісѓѓжФўсЂёу▓Йт║дсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂфсЂїсѓЅсђЂсЃЋсѓЕсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃЉсѓ╣сЂћсЂесЂФтЇітѕєТюфТ║ђсЂ«сЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂЌсЂІТ┤╗ТђДтїќсЂЌсЂЙсЂЏсѓЊсђѓGPT-OSS-20BсѓёQwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507сЂфсЂЕсЂ«тљїсѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂеТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЌсЂдсђЂТюђтцД3.3тђЇсЂ«ТјеУФќсѓ╣сЃФсЃ╝сЃЌсЃЃсЃѕсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсђЂС║║Т░ЌсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсѓѓсѓѕсѓіжФўсЂёу▓Йт║дсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓNemotron 3 NanoсЂ»сђЂсѓесЃ╝сѓИсѓДсЃ│сЃѕуџёсђЂТјеУФќсђЂсЃЂсЃБсЃЃсЃѕУЃйтіЏсЂїтљЉСИісЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂТюђтцД1MсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂ«сѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕжЋисѓњсѓхсЃЮсЃ╝сЃѕсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТИѕсЂ┐сЂ«Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3B BaseсЂесђЂсЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТИѕсЂ┐сЂ«Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3BсЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсѓњHugging FaceсЂДтЁгжќІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3BсЂ»сђЂMixture-of-ExpertsсЃЈсѓцсЃќсЃфсЃЃсЃЅMamba-TransformerУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂ25тЁєсЂ«сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂДС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсђЂуЏБУдќС╗ўсЂЇсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂет╝итїќтГду┐њсѓњухїсЂду▓Йт║дсѓњтљЉСИісђѓтЅЇСИќС╗БсЂ«Nemotron 2 NanoсѓѕсѓісѓѓжФўу▓Йт║дсЂДсђЂсЃЋсѓЕсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃЉсѓ╣сЂћсЂесЂФтЇітѕєТюфТ║ђсЂ«сЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњТ┤╗ТђДтїќсЂЌсђЂтљїсѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂеТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЌсЂдТюђтцД3.3тђЇсЂ«ТјеУФќсѓ╣сЃФсЃ╝сЃЌсЃЃсЃѕсѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓсѓесЃ╝сѓИсѓДсЃ│сЃѕуџёсђЂТјеУФќсђЂсЃЂсЃБсЃЃсЃѕУЃйтіЏсЂїтљЉСИісЂЌсђЂТюђтцД1MсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂ«сѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕжЋисѓњсѓхсЃЮсЃ╝сЃѕсђѓС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТИѕсЂ┐сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»Hugging FaceсЂДтЁгжќІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Xiaomi MiMo-VL-Miloco Technical Report, Jiaze Li+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, LanguageModel, MultiModal, Reasoning, OpenWeight, VideoGeneration/Understandings, VisionLanguageModel, KeyPoint Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.17436 # Authors - Jiaze Li - Jingyang Chen - Yuxun Qu - Shijie Xu - Zhenru Lin - Junyou Zhu - Boshen Xu - Wenhui Tan - Pei Fu - Jianzhong Ju - Zhenbo Luo - Jian Luan # Abstract - We open-source MiMo-VL-Miloco-7B and its quantized variant MiMo-VL-Miloco-7B-GGUF, a pair of home-centric vision-language models that achieve strong performance on both home-scenario understanding and general multimodal reasoning. Built on the MiMo-VL-7B backbone, MiMo-VL-Miloco-7B is specialized for smart-home environments, attaining leading F1 scores on gesture recognition and common home-scenario understanding, while also delivering consistent gains across video benchmarks such as Video-MME, Video-MMMU, and Charades-STA, as well as language understanding benchmarks including MMMU-Pro and MMLU-Pro. In our experiments, MiMo-VL-Miloco-7B outperforms strong closed-source and open-source baselines on home-scenario understanding and several multimodal reasoning benchmarks. To balance specialization and generality, we design a two-stage training pipeline that combines supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning based on Group Relative Policy Optimization, leveraging efficient multi-domain data. We further incorporate chain-of-thought supervision and token-budget-aware reasoning, enabling the model to learn knowledge in a data-efficient manner while also performing reasoning efficiently. Our analysis shows that targeted home-scenario training not only enhances activity and gesture understanding, but also improves text-only reasoning with only modest trade-offs on document-centric tasks. Model checkpoints, quantized GGUF weights, and our home-scenario evaluation toolkit are publicly available at https://github.com/XiaoMi/xiaomi-mimo-vl-miloco to support research and deployment in real-world smart-home applications. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂMiMo-VL-Miloco-7BсЂісѓѕсЂ│сЂЮсЂ«жЄЈтГљтїќсЃљсЃфсѓбсЃ│сЃѕсЂДсЂѓсѓІMiMo-VL-Miloco-7B-GGUFсѓњсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣тїќсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ»сђЂт«Хт║ГСИГт┐ЃсЂ«УдќУдџсЂеУеђУфъсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃџсѓбсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂт«Хт║ГсѓисЃісЃфсѓфсЂ«уљєУДБсЂеСИђУѕгуџёсЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФТјеУФќсЂ«СИАТќ╣сЂДтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓMiMo-VL-7BсЃљсЃЃсѓ»сЃюсЃ╝сЃ│сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂёсЂдТДІу»ЅсЂЋсѓїсЂЪMiMo-VL-Miloco-7BсЂ»сђЂсѓ╣сЃъсЃ╝сЃѕсЃЏсЃ╝сЃауњ░тбЃсЂФуЅ╣тїќсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂсѓИсѓДсѓ╣сЃЂсЃБсЃ╝УфЇУГўсѓёСИђУѕгуџёсЂфт«Хт║ГсѓисЃісЃфсѓфсЂ«уљєУДБсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдтЁѕжђ▓уџёсЂфF1сѓ╣сѓ│сѓбсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЙсЂЪсђЂVideo-MMEсђЂVideo-MMMUсђЂCharades-STAсЂфсЂЕсЂ«сЃЊсЃЄсѓфсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сѓёсђЂMMM-ProсђЂMMLU-ProсЂфсЂЕсЂ«УеђУфъуљєУДБсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсѓѓСИђУ▓ФсЂЌсЂЪтљЉСИісѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«т«ЪжеЊсЂДсЂ»сђЂMiMo-VL-Miloco-7BсЂїт«Хт║ГсѓисЃісЃфсѓфсЂ«уљєУДБсѓёсЂёсЂЈсЂцсЂІсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФТјеУФќсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсђЂт╝итіЏсЂфсѓ»сЃГсЃ╝сѓ║сЃЅсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂісѓѕсЂ│сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«сЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃ│сѓњСИітЏъсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїуб║УфЇсЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓуЅ╣тїќсЂеСИђУѕгТђДсЂ«сЃљсЃЕсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњтЈќсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂті╣ујЄуџёсЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсЂЪсѓ░сЃФсЃ╝сЃЌуЏИт»ЙсЃЮсЃфсѓисЃ╝ТюђжЂЕтїќсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈт╝итїќтГду┐њсЂеуЏБУдќС╗ўсЂЇсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсЂЪС║їТ«хжџјсЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЉсѓцсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсЃ│сѓњУеГУеѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂсЃЂсѓДсЃ╝сЃ│сЃ╗сѓфсЃќсЃ╗сѓйсЃ╝сЃѕсЂ«уЏБУдќсЂесЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│С║ѕу«ЌсѓњУђЃТЁ«сЂЌсЂЪТјеУФќсѓњухёсЂ┐УЙ╝сѓђсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂїсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐ті╣ујЄуџёсЂФуЪЦУГўсѓњтГдсЂ│сЂфсЂїсѓЅсђЂті╣ујЄуџёсЂФТјеУФќсѓњУАїсЂѕсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂФсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«тѕєТъљсЂДсЂ»сђЂсѓ┐сЃ╝сѓ▓сЃЃсЃѕсѓњухъсЂБсЂЪт«Хт║ГсѓисЃісЃфсѓфсЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂїсђЂТ┤╗тІЋсѓёсѓИсѓДсѓ╣сЃЂсЃБсЃ╝сЂ«уљєУДБсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂасЂЉсЂДсЂфсЂЈсђЂТќЄТЏИСИГт┐ЃсЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсѓѓсѓЈсЂџсЂІсЂфсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃЅсѓфсЃЋсЂДсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«сЂ┐сЂ«ТјеУФќсѓњТћ╣тќёсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсђЂжЄЈтГљтїќсЂЋсѓїсЂЪGGUFсѓдсѓДсѓцсЃѕсђЂсЂісѓѕсЂ│т«Хт║ГсѓисЃісЃфсѓфУЕЋСЙАсЃёсЃ╝сЃФсѓГсЃЃсЃѕсЂ»сђЂт«ЪСИќуЋїсЂ«сѓ╣сЃъсЃ╝сЃѕсЃЏсЃ╝сЃасѓбсЃЌсЃфсѓ▒сЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІуаћуЕХсЂет▒ЋжќІсѓњТћ»ТЈ┤сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂhttps://github.com/XiaoMi/xiaomi-mimo-vl-miloco сЂДтЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - MiMo-VL-Miloco-7BсЂесЂЮсЂ«жЄЈтГљтїќсЃљсЃфсѓбсЃ│сЃѕсѓњсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣тїќсЂЌсђЂт«Хт║ГСИГт┐ЃсЂ«УдќУдџсЂеУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂесЂЌсЂдтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сђѓуЅ╣сЂФсѓ╣сЃъсЃ╝сЃѕсЃЏсЃ╝сЃауњ░тбЃсЂФуЅ╣тїќсЂЌсђЂсѓИсѓДсѓ╣сЃЂсЃБсЃ╝УфЇУГўсѓёсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФТјеУФќсЂДжФўсЂёF1сѓ╣сѓ│сѓбсѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓС║їТ«хжџјсЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЉсѓцсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсЃ│сѓњУеГУеѕсЂЌсђЂті╣ујЄуџёсЂфТјеУФќсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсђѓт«Хт║ГсѓисЃісЃфсѓфсЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂїТ┤╗тІЋуљєУДБсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕТјеУФќсЂФсѓѓті╣Тъюсѓњуц║сЂЎсђѓсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂесЃёсЃ╝сЃФсѓГсЃЃсЃѕсЂ»тЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсђЂсѓ╣сЃъсЃ╝сЃѕсЃЏсЃ╝сЃасѓбсЃЌсЃфсѓ▒сЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂ«уаћуЕХсЂФУ▓буї«сђѓ

maintainer-comment-text.p: home-scenario gesture recognitionсЂеdaily activity recognitionсЂДGemini-2.5-ProсѓњСИітЏъсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓуЅ╣т«џсЂ«сЃдсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сѓ╣сЂФуЅ╣тїќсЂЌсЂцсЂцсђЂgenericсЂфсЃдсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњТљЇсЂфсѓЈсЂфсЂёсѓѕсЂєсЂфсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњтГду┐њсЂЌсЂЪсЂёта┤тљѕсЂ»тЈѓУђЃсЂФсЂфсѓІсЂІсѓѓсЂЌсѓїсЂфсЂёсђѓ сЂЙсЂџSFTсЂДhome-scenarioсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐[^1] + GeneralсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«DataMixсЂДreasoning patternсѓњтГду┐њсЂЋсЂЏсђЂtokenсЂ«efficiencyсѓњжФўсѓЂсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФCoTсЃЉсѓ┐сЃ╝сЃ│сѓњТјњжЎцсЂЌdirect answerсѓњсЂЎсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂфсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐№╝ѕсЂісѓѕсЂ│prompting№╝ЅсЂДсѓѓтГду┐њсЂЋсЂЏсѓІсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓіhome-scenarioсЂДсЂ«ТјеУФќУЃйтіЏсЂїт╝итїќсЂЋсѓїсѓІсђѓSFTсЂ»full parameter tuningсЂДт«ЪТќйсЂЋсѓїсђЂoptimizerсЂ»AdamWсђѓсЃљсЃЃсЃЂсѓхсѓцсѓ║128, warmup ratio 0.03, learning rate 1 * 10^-5сђѓсѓ╣сѓ▒сѓИсЃЦсЃ╝сЃЕсЂФсЂцсЂёсЂдсЂ»УеўУ┐░сЂїсЂфсЂёсѓѕсЂєсЂФУдІсЂѕсѓІсђѓ сЂЮсЂ«тЙїсђЂСИђУѕгуџёсЂфсЃдсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сѓ╣№╝ѕVideo Understanding (temporal groundingсЂФсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сѓФсѓ╣), GUI Grounding, Multimodal Reasoning №╝ѕуЅ╣сЂФSTEMсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐№╝Ѕ№╝ЅсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњућесЂёсЂдGRPOсЂДRLсѓњсЂЎсѓІсђѓТўјсѓЅсЂІсЂФу░АтЇўсЃ╗жЏБсЂЌсЂЎсЂјсѓІсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ»жЎцтцќсђѓRLсЂ«rewardсЂ» `r_acc + r_format`сЂ«уиџтйбУБют«ї№╝ѕС┐ѓТЋ░сЂ»accL: 0.9, format: 0.1№╝ЅсЂДт«џуЙЕсЂЋсѓїсѓІсђѓr_accсЂ»сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂћсЂесЂФуЋ░сЂфсЂБсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓVideo UnderstandingсЂДсЂ»С║ѕТИгсЂЌсЂЪqueryсЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂїС║ѕТИгсЂЌсЂЪtimespanсЂеgoldсЂ«timespanсЂ«overlapсЂїсЂЕсЂ«уеІт║дсЂѓсѓІсЂІсѓњaccсЂесЂЌсђЂGUI GroundingсЂДсЂ»bounding boxсѓњС║ѕТИгсЂЌpred/goldсЂ«overlapсѓњaccсЂесЂЎсѓІсђѓMultimodal ReasoninghсЂ»STEMсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂфсЂ«сЂДтЏъуГћсЂїСИђУЄ┤сЂЎсѓІсЂІсѓњbinaryсЂ«accсЂесЂЌсЂдСИјсЂѕсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂ»сђЂсѓбсЃђсЃЌсѓ┐сЃ╝сЂДLLMсЂеТјЦуХџсЂЎсѓІсѓ┐сѓцсЃЌсЂ«сѓѓсЂ«сЂДсђЂтІЋућ╗/ућ╗тЃЈсЂ«BackboneсЂФсЂ»ViTсѓњућесЂёсЂдсђЂMLPсЂ«сѓбсЃђсЃЌсѓ┐сЃ╝сѓњТїЂсЂАсЂёсЂдLLMсЂ«тЁЦтіЏсЂесЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ [^1]: volunteerсЂФсѓѕсѓІhome-scenarioсЂДсЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐СйюТѕљ; ruleсѓњУдЈт«џсЂЌvolunteerсЂФуљєУДБсЂЌсЂдсѓѓсѓЅсЂёсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐тЈјжЏєсђѓсЂЮсЂ«тЙїуаћуЕХУђЁсЂїСйјтЊЂУ│фсЂфсѓѓсЂ«сѓњжЎцтцќ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] T5Gemma 2: Seeing, Reading, and Understanding Longer, Biao Zhang+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: NLP, MultiModal, SmallModel, MultiLingual, OpenWeight, Encoder-Decoder, KeyPoint Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.14856 # Authors - Biao Zhang - Paul Suganthan - Ga├Фl Liu - Ilya Philippov - Sahil Dua - Ben Hora - Kat Black - Gus Martins - Omar Sanseviero - Shreya Pathak - Cassidy Hardin - Francesco Visin - Jiageng Zhang - Kathleen Kenealy - Qin Yin - Olivier Lacombe - Armand Joulin - Tris Warkentin - Adam Roberts # Abstract - We introduce T5Gemma 2, the next generation of the T5Gemma family of lightweight open encoder-decoder models, featuring strong multilingual, multimodal and long-context capabilities. T5Gemma 2 follows the adaptation recipe (via UL2) in T5Gemma -- adapting a pretrained decoder-only model into an encoder-decoder model, and extends it from text-only regime to multimodal based on the Gemma 3 models. We further propose two methods to improve the efficiency: tied word embedding that shares all embeddings across encoder and decoder, and merged attention that unifies decoder self- and cross-attention into a single joint module. Experiments demonstrate the generality of the adaptation strategy over architectures and modalities as well as the unique strength of the encoder-decoder architecture on long context modeling. Similar to T5Gemma, T5Gemma 2 yields comparable or better pretraining performance and significantly improved post-training performance than its Gemma 3 counterpart. We release the pretrained models (270M-270M, 1B-1B and 4B-4B) to the community for future research. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂт╝итіЏсЂфтцџУеђУфъсђЂтцџсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсђЂжЋиТќЄсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕУЃйтіЏсѓњтѓЎсЂѕсЂЪУ╗йжЄЈсЂфсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«T5GemmaсЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сЂ«ТгАСИќС╗БсЂДсЂѓсѓІT5Gemma 2сѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓT5Gemma 2сЂ»сђЂT5GemmaсЂ«жЂЕт┐юсЃгсѓисЃћ№╝ѕUL2сѓњС╗ІсЂЌсЂд№╝ЅсЂФтЙЊсЂёсђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝т░ѓућесЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФжЂЕт┐юсЂЋсЂЏсђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«сЂ┐сЂ«жаўтЪЪсЂІсѓЅGemma 3сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈтцџсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЂИсЂеТІАт╝хсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂті╣ујЄсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«2сЂцсЂ«Тќ╣Т│ЋсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎ№╝џсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЂесЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝жќЊсЂДтЁесЂдсЂ«тЪІсѓЂУЙ╝сЂ┐сѓњтЁ▒ТюЅсЂЎсѓІухљтљѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪтЇўУфътЪІсѓЂУЙ╝сЂ┐сђЂсЂісѓѕсЂ│сЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЂ«УЄфти▒Т│еТёЈсЂесѓ»сЃГсѓ╣Т│еТёЈсѓњтЇўСИђсЂ«тЁ▒тљїсЃбсѓИсЃЦсЃ╝сЃФсЂФух▒тљѕсЂЎсѓІсЃъсЃ╝сѓИсЃЅсѓбсЃєсЃ│сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂДсЂЎсђѓт«ЪжеЊсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂесЃбсЃђсЃфсЃєсѓБсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсѓІжЂЕт┐юТѕдуЋЦсЂ«СИђУѕгТђДсЂесђЂжЋиТќЄсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЃбсЃЄсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂ«уІгУЄфсЂ«т╝исЂ┐сЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓT5GemmaсЂетљїТДўсЂФсђЂT5Gemma 2сЂ»сђЂGemma 3сЂ«т»Йт┐юсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂеТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЌсЂдсђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТђДУЃйсЂ»тљїуГЅсЂЙсЂЪсЂ»сЂЮсѓїС╗ЦСИісЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂС║ІтЙїтГду┐њТђДУЃйсЂ»тцДт╣ЁсЂФтљЉСИісЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂС╗ітЙїсЂ«уаћуЕХсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТИѕсЂ┐сЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕ270M-270MсђЂ1B-1BсђЂ4B-4B№╝Ѕсѓњсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсЂФтЁгжќІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - T5Gemma 2сЂ»сђЂУ╗йжЄЈсЂфсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂтцџУеђУфъсЃ╗тцџсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃ╗жЋиТќЄсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕУЃйтіЏсѓњтѓЎсЂѕсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓT5GemmaсЂ«жЂЕт┐юсЃгсѓисЃћсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЇсђЂсЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝т░ѓућесЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФТІАт╝хсЂЌсђЂті╣ујЄтљЉСИісЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФтЪІсѓЂУЙ╝сЂ┐сЂ«тЁ▒ТюЅсЂесЃъсЃ╝сѓИсЃЅсѓбсЃєсЃ│сѓисЃДсЃ│сѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓт«ЪжеЊсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂжЋиТќЄсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЃбсЃЄсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІт╝исЂ┐сЂїуб║УфЇсЂЋсѓїсђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТђДУЃйсЂ»Gemma 3сЂетљїуГЅС╗ЦСИісђЂС║ІтЙїтГду┐њТђДУЃйсЂ»тцДт╣ЁсЂФтљЉСИісЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓС╗ітЙїсђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТИѕсЂ┐сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсЂФтЁгжќІС║ѕт«џсЂДсЂЎсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Bolmo: Byteifying the Next Generation of Language Models, Benjamin Minixhofer+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, action_wanted, OpenWeight, OpenSource, Selected Papers/Blogs, KeyPoint Notes, Byte-level
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - preprint: https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.15586 # Authors - Benjamin Minixhofer - Tyler Murray - Tomasz Limisiewicz - Anna Korhonen - Luke Zettlemoyer - Noah A. Smith - Edoardo M. Ponti - Luca Soldaini - Valentin Hofmann # Abstract - We introduce Bolmo, the first family of competitive fully open byte-level language models (LMs) at the 1B and 7B parameter scales. In contrast to prior research on byte-level LMs, which focuses predominantly on training from scratch, we train Bolmo by byteifying existing subword-level LMs. Byteification enables overcoming the limitations of subword tokenization - such as insufficient character understanding and efficiency constraints due to the fixed subword vocabulary - while performing at the level of leading subword-level LMs. Bolmo is specifically designed for byteification: our architecture resolves a mismatch between the expressivity of prior byte-level architectures and subword-level LMs, which makes it possible to employ an effective exact distillation objective between Bolmo and the source subword model. This allows for converting a subword-level LM to a byte-level LM by investing less than 1\% of a typical pretraining token budget. Bolmo substantially outperforms all prior byte-level LMs of comparable size, and outperforms the source subword-level LMs on character understanding and, in some cases, coding, while coming close to matching the original LMs' performance on other tasks. Furthermore, we show that Bolmo can achieve inference speeds competitive with subword-level LMs by training with higher token compression ratios, and can be cheaply and effectively post-trained by leveraging the existing ecosystem around the source subword-level LM. Our results finally make byte-level LMs a practical choice competitive with subword-level LMs across a wide set of use cases. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂ1BсЂісѓѕсЂ│7BсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃФсЂДсЂ«уФХС║ЅтіЏсЂ«сЂѓсѓІт«їтЁесѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфсЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕLM№╝ЅсЂ«ТюђтѕЮсЂ«сЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сЂДсЂѓсѓІBolmoсѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«сЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІуаћуЕХсЂ»СИ╗сЂФсѓ╝сЃГсЂІсѓЅсЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂФуёдуѓ╣сѓњтйЊсЂдсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсЂїсђЂBolmoсЂ»ТЌбтГўсЂ«сѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсѓњсЃљсѓцсЃѕтїќсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњУАїсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЃљсѓцсЃѕтїќсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂсѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│тїќсЂ«жЎљуЋїсђЂсЂЎсЂфсѓЈсЂАСИЇтЇЂтѕєсЂфТќЄтГЌуљєУДБсѓётЏ║т«џсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅУфътйЎсЂФсѓѕсѓІті╣ујЄтѕХу┤ёсѓњтЁІТюЇсЂЌсЂцсЂцсђЂтЁѕжђ▓уџёсЂфсѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂетљїуГЅсЂ«сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓBolmoсЂ»сЃљсѓцсЃѕтїќсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФуЅ╣тѕЦсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂісѓісђЂтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«сЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂесѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂесЂ«жќЊсЂ«УАеуЈЙтіЏсЂ«СИЇСИђУЄ┤сѓњУДБТ▒║сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂBolmoсЂесѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂесЂ«жќЊсЂДті╣ТъюуџёсЂфТГБуб║сЂфУњИуЋЎуЏ«уџёсѓњТјАућесЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂсѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсѓњсЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂФтцЅТЈЏсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂтЁИтъІуџёсЂфС║ІтЅЇсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│С║ѕу«ЌсЂ«1№╝ЁТюфТ║ђсЂ«ТіЋУ│ЄсЂДТИѕсЂ┐сЂЙсЂЎсђѓBolmoсЂ»сђЂтљїуГЅсЂ«сѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сЂЎсЂ╣сЂдсЂ«тЙЊТЮЦсЂ«сЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсѓњтцДт╣ЁсЂФСИітЏъсѓісђЂТќЄтГЌуљєУДБсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсЂ»сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂта┤тљѕсЂФсѓѕсЂБсЂдсЂ»сѓ│сЃ╝сЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсѓѓтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂС╗ќсЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсЂ»тЁЃсЂ«LMсЂ«сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сЂФУ┐ЉсЂЦсЂЈсЂЊсЂесЂїсЂДсЂЇсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂBolmoсЂ»жФўсЂёсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│тюДуИ«Т»ћсЂДсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂсѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂеуФХС║ЅтіЏсЂ«сЂѓсѓІТјеУФќжђЪт║дсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂДсЂЇсђЂсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂ«тЉетЏ▓сЂФсЂѓсѓІТЌбтГўсЂ«сѓесѓ│сѓисѓ╣сЃєсЃасѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂт«ЅСЙАсЂІсЂцті╣ТъюуџёсЂФсЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂїтЈ»УЃйсЂДсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«ухљТъюсЂ»сђЂсЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂїт║Ѓу»ёсЂфсЃдсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сѓ╣сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂеуФХС║ЅсЂДсЂЇсѓІт«ЪућеуџёсЂфжЂИТіъУѓбсЂесЂфсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - BolmoсЂ»сђЂ1BсЂісѓѕсЂ│7BсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«сЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂТЌбтГўсЂ«сѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсѓњсЃљсѓцсЃѕтїќсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂЋсѓїсђЂсѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│тїќсЂ«жЎљуЋїсѓњтЁІТюЇсЂЌсЂцсЂцтљїуГЅсЂ«сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЅ╣тѕЦсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪBolmoсЂ»сђЂсѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂесЂ«жќЊсЂДті╣ТъюуџёсЂфУњИуЋЎсѓњУАїсЂёсђЂСйјсѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕсЂДсЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂФтцЅТЈЏтЈ»УЃйсЂДсЂЎсђѓBolmoсЂ»тЙЊТЮЦсЂ«сЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂТќЄтГЌуљєУДБсѓёсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂДтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂТјеУФќжђЪт║дсѓѓуФХС║ЅтіЏсЂїсЂѓсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓухљТъюсЂесЂЌсЂдсђЂсЃљсѓцсЃѕсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂ»сѓхсЃќсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃгсЃЎсЃФLMсЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІт«ЪућеуџёсЂфжЂИТіъУѓбсЂесЂфсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ

maintainer-comment-text.p: сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсѓњbyteтѕЌсЂ«у│╗тѕЌсЂесЂЌсЂдУДБжЄѕсЂЌтЁЦтЄ║тіЏсѓњУАїсЂєУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђѓсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂесЂЌсЂдсЂ»сђЂbyteтѕЌсѓњtokenтїќсЂЌbyteтѕЌтЇўСйЇсЂДembeddingтїќРєњmLSTMсЂФсѓѕсЂБсЂдсЂЮсѓїсѓЅсЂїcontextсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІТЃЁта▒сѓњТїЂсЂБсЂЪуіХТЁІсЂДсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂЋсѓїРєњ1сЃљсѓцсЃѕтЁѕсЂ«contextсѓњућесЂёсЂдтЇўУфъсЂ«тбЃуЋїсѓњС║ѕТИгсЂЎсѓІсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕсЂЊсЂ«жЃетѕєсЂ»causalсЂДсЂ»сЂфсЂЈbi-directional№╝ЅсЂФсѓѕсЂБсЂдтбЃуЋїсѓњУфЇУГўсЂЌсђЂтбЃуЋїсЂЙсЂДтЈ»тцЅжЋисЂДembeddingсѓњpoolingсЂЌсЃЉсЃЃсЃЂсѓњтйбТѕљсЂЌсђЂOlmo3сЂ«тЁЦтіЏсЂесЂЎсѓІ№╝ѕсЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сЂ»сЂЮсЂ«жђєсЂ«ТЊЇСйюсѓњсЂЌсЂдТюђухѓуџёсЂФУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«headсѓњућесЂёсѓІ№╝Ѕсђѓ сѓ╣сѓ»сЃЕсЃЃсЃЂсЂІсѓЅByte Latent TransformerсЂ«сѓѕсЂєсЂфсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњтГду┐њсЂЎсѓІсЂ«сЂДсЂ»сЂфсЂЈсђЂ2-stageсЂДтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсѓІсђѓсЂЙсЂџOlmo3сѓњfreezeсЂЌсђЂС╗ќсЂ« local encoder, local decoder, boundary predictor, and language modeling headсЂ«сЂ┐сѓњтГду┐њсЂЎсѓІсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓіsubwordсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂетљїТДўсЂ«ТїЎтІЋсѓњтГду┐њсЂДсЂЇсѓІсђѓсЂЮсЂ«сЂєсЂѕсЂДсђЂOlmo3сЂ«freezeсѓњУДБжЎцсЂЌтЁеСйЊсѓњтГду┐њсЂЎсѓІсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂOlmo3сЂФС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсЂЪуЪЦУГўсѓёТїЎтІЋсѓњТюђтцДжЎљсЂФТ┤╗ућесЂЎсѓІ№╝ѕ=сѓѓсЂесѓѓсЂеsubwordсЂДтІЋСйюсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЪсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњbyteсЃгсЃЎсЃФсЂДтІЋСйюсЂЎсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂФуХЎуХџтГду┐њсЂЎсѓІ№╝Ѕсђѓ >The Bolmo architecture. Tokenization & Embedding T transforms the input text into one representation per byte. The representations are contextualized with the local encoder E consisting of mLSTM blocks. The boundary predictor B decides where to place patch boundaries using one byte of future context. The representations are then Pooled,

- [Paper Note] MEGABYTE: Predicting Million-byte Sequences with Multiscale Transformers, Lili Yu+, NeurIPS'23, 2023.05

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Nemotron-Cascade: Scaling Cascaded Reinforcement Learning for General-Purpose Reasoning Models, Boxin Wang+, arXiv'25, 2025.12
paper-entry.tags: General, NLP, LanguageModel, Alignment, ReinforcementLearning, Reasoning, OpenWeight, OpenSource, read-later, RLVR, Selected Papers/Blogs, CrossDomain, KeyPoint Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.13607 # Authors - Boxin Wang - Chankyu Lee - Nayeon Lee - Sheng-Chieh Lin - Wenliang Dai - Yang Chen - Yangyi Chen - Zhuolin Yang - Zihan Liu - Mohammad Shoeybi - Bryan Catanzaro - Wei Ping # Abstract - Building general-purpose reasoning models with reinforcement learning (RL) entails substantial cross-domain heterogeneity, including large variation in inference-time response lengths and verification latency. Such variability complicates the RL infrastructure, slows training, and makes training curriculum (e.g., response length extension) and hyperparameter selection challenging. In this work, we propose cascaded domain-wise reinforcement learning (Cascade RL) to develop general-purpose reasoning models, Nemotron-Cascade, capable of operating in both instruct and deep thinking modes. Departing from conventional approaches that blend heterogeneous prompts from different domains, Cascade RL orchestrates sequential, domain-wise RL, reducing engineering complexity and delivering state-of-the-art performance across a wide range of benchmarks. Notably, RLHF for alignment, when used as a pre-step, boosts the model's reasoning ability far beyond mere preference optimization, and subsequent domain-wise RLVR stages rarely degrade the benchmark performance attained in earlier domains and may even improve it (see an illustration in Figure 1). Our 14B model, after RL, outperforms its SFT teacher, DeepSeek-R1-0528, on LiveCodeBench v5/v6/Pro and achieves silver-medal performance in the 2025 International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI). We transparently share our training and data recipes. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - СИђУѕгуЏ«уџёсЂ«ТјеУФќсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњт╝итїќтГду┐њ№╝ѕRL№╝ЅсЂДТДІу»ЅсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂ»сђЂТјеУФќТЎѓсЂ«т┐юуГћжЋисѓёТцюУе╝жЂЁт╗ХсЂ«тцДсЂЇсЂфтцЅтІЋсѓњтљФсѓђсђЂт«ЪУ│фуџёсЂфсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│жќЊсЂ«уЋ░У│фТђДсѓњС╝┤сЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сѓѕсЂєсЂфтцЅтІЋсЂ»RLсѓцсЃ│сЃЋсЃЕсѓњУцЄжЏЉсЂФсЂЌсђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњжЂЁсЂЈсЂЌсђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓФсЃфсѓГсЃЦсЃЕсЃа№╝ѕСЙІсЂѕсЂ░сђЂт┐юуГћжЋисЂ«ТІАт╝х№╝ЅсѓёсЃЈсѓцсЃЉсЃ╝сЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«жЂИТіъсѓњтЏ░жЏБсЂФсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТюгуаћуЕХсЂДсЂ»сђЂСИђУѕгуЏ«уџёсЂ«ТјеУФќсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњжќІуЎ║сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂсѓФсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃЅсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│тѕЦт╝итїќтГду┐њ№╝ѕCascade RL№╝ЅсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсђЂNemotron-CascadeсЂ»сђЂТїЄуц║сЃбсЃ╝сЃЅсЂеТи▒сЂёТђЮУђЃсЃбсЃ╝сЃЅсЂ«СИАТќ╣сЂДтІЋСйюсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїсЂДсЂЇсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЋ░сЂфсѓІсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂІсѓЅсЂ«уЋ░У│фсЂфсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсѓњТиитљѕсЂЎсѓІтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«сѓбсЃЌсЃГсЃ╝сЃЂсЂІсѓЅжЏбсѓїсђЂCascade RLсЂ»жаєТгАсЂ«сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│тѕЦRLсѓњУф┐ТЋ┤сЂЌсђЂсѓесЃ│сѓИсЃІсѓбсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂ«УцЄжЏЉсЂЋсѓњУ╗йТИЏсЂЌсђЂт╣Ёт║ЃсЂёсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЅ╣сЂФсђЂТЋ┤тљѕТђДсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«RLHFсѓњтЅЇТ«хжџјсЂесЂЌсЂдСй┐ућесЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«ТјеУФќУЃйтіЏсЂ»тЇўсЂфсѓІтЦйсЂ┐сЂ«ТюђжЂЕтїќсѓњУХЁсЂѕсЂдтцДт╣ЁсЂФтљЉСИісЂЌсђЂсЂЮсЂ«тЙїсЂ«сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│тѕЦRLVRТ«хжџјсЂДсЂ»сђЂС╗ЦтЅЇсЂ«сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂДжЂћТѕљсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сЂїтіБтїќсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂ»сЂ╗сЂесѓЊсЂЕсЂфсЂЈсђЂсѓђсЂЌсѓЇТћ╣тќёсЂЋсѓїсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓѓсЂѓсѓісЂЙсЂЎ№╝ѕтЏ│1сЂФуц║сЂЎсѓѕсЂєсЂФ№╝ЅсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«14BсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂRLтЙїсЂФсђЂSFTТЋЎтИФсЂДсЂѓсѓІDeepSeek-R1-0528сѓњLiveCodeBench v5/v6/ProсЂДСИітЏъсѓісђЂ2025т╣┤тЏйжџЏТЃЁта▒сѓфсЃфсЃ│сЃћсЃЃсѓ»№╝ѕIOI№╝ЅсЂДжіђсЃАсЃђсЃФсЂ«сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂесЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«сЃгсѓисЃћсѓњжђЈТўјсЂФтЁ▒ТюЅсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - СИђУѕгуЏ«уџёсЂ«ТјеУФќсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњт╝итїќтГду┐њ№╝ѕRL№╝ЅсЂДТДІу»ЅсЂЎсѓІжџЏсЂ«Уф▓жАїсЂФт»ЙтЄдсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсђЂсѓФсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃЅсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│тѕЦт╝итїќтГду┐њ№╝ѕCascade RL№╝ЅсѓњТЈљТАѕсђѓNemotron-CascadeсЂ»сђЂТїЄуц║сЃбсЃ╝сЃЅсЂеТи▒сЂёТђЮУђЃсЃбсЃ╝сЃЅсЂДтІЋСйюсЂЌсђЂуЋ░сЂфсѓІсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсѓњжаєТгАУф┐ТЋ┤сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂсѓесЃ│сѓИсЃІсѓбсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂ«УцЄжЏЉсЂЋсѓњУ╗йТИЏсЂЌсђЂТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњт«ЪуЈЙсђѓRLHFсѓњтЅЇТ«хжџјсЂесЂЌсЂдСй┐ућесЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДТјеУФќУЃйтіЏсЂїтљЉСИісЂЌсђЂсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│тѕЦRLТ«хжџјсЂДсѓѓсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сЂїТћ╣тќёсЂЋсѓїсѓІсђѓ14BсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂLiveCodeBenchсЂДтёфсѓїсЂЪухљТъюсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂ2025т╣┤тЏйжџЏТЃЁта▒сѓфсЃфсЃ│сЃћсЃЃсѓ»сЂДжіђсЃАсЃђсЃФсѓњуЇ▓тЙЌсђѓсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂесЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«сЃгсѓисЃћсѓѓтЁ▒ТюЅсђѓ

maintainer-comment-text.p: тЙЊТЮЦсЂ«RLсЂ»сЂЎсЂ╣сЂдсЂ«сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњmixсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсЂісЂЊсЂфсѓїсЂдсЂЇсЂЪсЂїсђЂтђІсђЁсЂ«сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњтђІтѕЦсЂФRLсЂЌсђЂcascadingТќ╣т╝ЈсЂДжЂЕуће (Cascade RL) сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІ№╝ѕт«ЪжџЏсЂ»УЉЌУђЁсѓЅсЂ«тЁѕУАїуаћуЕХсЂДmath->codingсЂ«cascadingсЂ»т«ЪТќйсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЪсЂїсђЂсЂЮсѓїсѓњсѓѕсѓіт║Ѓу»ёсЂфсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│(RLHF -> instruction following -> math -> coding -> software engineering)сЂФжЂЕућесЂЌсЂЪсђЂсЂесЂёсЂєуаћуЕХ№╝Ѕсђѓ cascadingсЂФсЂ»сЂёсЂЈсЂцсЂІсЂ«сЃАсЃфсЃЃсЃѕсЂїсЂѓсѓіRLсЂ«тГду┐њжђЪт║дсѓњТћ╣тќёсЂДсЂЇсѓІ№╝ѕсЂѓсѓІсЂёсЂ»RLсЂ«сѓцсЃ│сЃЋсЃЕсЂ«УцЄжЏЉТђДсѓњуиЕтњїсЂДсЂЇсѓІ№╝Ѕ - сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂћсЂесЂ«verificationсЂ«жђЪт║дсЂ«жЂЋсЂёсЂФсѓѕсЂБсЂдтГду┐њжђЪт║дсѓњТљЇсЂфсЂєсЂЊсЂесЂїсЂфсЂё№╝ѕe.g. ТЋ░тГдсЂ«rule-basedсЂфverificationсЂ»ТЌЕсЂёсЂїcodingсЂ»жЂЁсЂё№╝Ѕ - сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂћсЂесЂФтЄ║тіЏжЋисЂ»уЋ░сЂфсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсѓфсЃ│сЃЮсЃфсѓисЃ╝RLсѓњжЂЕућесЂЎсѓІсЂеті╣ујЄсЂїУљйсЂАсѓІ№╝ѕжЋисЂёсЃгсѓ╣сЃЮсЃ│сѓ╣сЂ«ућЪТѕљсѓњтЙЁсЂЪсЂфсЂЉсѓїсЂ░сѓЅсЂфсЂёсЂЪсѓЂ№╝Ѕ ТюгуаћуЕХсЂДтЙЌсѓЅсѓїсЂЪтѕЕуѓ╣сЂесЂЌсЂдсЂ»Figure 1сѓњтЈѓУђЃсЂФУеђтЈісЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсЂї - RLHF, instruction followingсѓњС║ІтЅЇсЂФжЂЕућесЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂФсѓѕсЂБсЂдсђЂтЙїТ«хсЂ«reasoningсЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓѓтљЉСИісЂЎсѓІ№╝ѕreasoningсЂ«warmupсЂФсЂфсѓІ№╝Ѕ - тіасЂѕсЂдт┐юуГћсЂ«жЋисЂЋсЂ«тЅіТИЏсЂФсЂцсЂфсЂїсѓІ - RLсЂ»catastrophic forgettingсЂФт╝исЂЈсђЂтЅЇТ«хсЂДт«ЪТќйсЂЌсЂЪсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂ«ТђДУЃйсЂїтЙїТ«хсЂ«сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂ«RLсЂФсѓѕсЂБсЂдТђДУЃйсЂїтіБтїќсЂЌсЂфсЂё - сЂесЂёсЂБсЂдсѓѓFigure 2сѓњУдІсѓІсЂесђЂcodingсЂеsoftware engineeringсЂ»ухљТДІсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│У┐ЉсЂёсЂ«сЂДсЂ»сЃ╗сЃ╗сЃ╗№╝ЪсЂесЂёсЂєТ░ЌсЂ»сЂЎсѓІсЂїсЃ╗сЃ╗сЃ╗сђѓ - RLсЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІсѓФсЃфсѓГсЃЦсЃЕсЃатГду┐њсѓёсЃЈсѓцсЃЉсЃ╝сЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњсЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂћсЂесЂФТюђжЂЕсЂфсѓѓсЂ«сѓњжЂЕућесЂДсЂЇсѓІ С╗ќсЂФсѓѓthinking/non-thinking сЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїУеђтЈісЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсЂїУфГсѓЂсЂдсЂёсЂфсЂёсђѓ
