- Scaling Data-Constrained Language Models, Niklas Muennighoff+, NeurIPS'23 сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТЎѓсЂФ4 epochсЂЙсЂДсЂ»ТђДУЃйсЂ«Тћ╣тќёт╣ЁсЂїтцДсЂЇсЂёсЂета▒тЉісЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЪсЂїсђЂSFTсЂДсѓѓ5 epochуеІт║дсЂЙсЂДтГду┐њсЂЎсѓІсЂеУЅ»сЂёТеАТДўсђѓ сЂЙсЂЪсђЂSFT dataсѓњscalingсЂЋсЂЏсѓІжџЏсЂ»сђЂpromptсЂ«ТЋ░сЂасЂЉсЂДсЂфсЂЈсђЂpromptтЇўСйЇсЂ«responseТЋ░сѓњтбЌсѓёсЂЎсЂ«сЂїті╣Тъюуџё

paper-entry.title: QwenLong-CPRS: Towards $\infty$-LLMs with Dynamic Context Optimization, Weizhou Shen+, arXiv'25
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, LongSequence, OpenWeight
paper-entry.issue-body:
# URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18092 # Authors - Weizhou Shen - Chenliang Li - Fanqi Wan - Shengyi Liao - Shaopeng Lai - Bo Zhang - Yingcheng Shi - Yuning Wu - Gang Fu - Zhansheng Li - Bin Yang - Ji Zhang - Fei Huang - Jingren Zhou - Ming Yan # Abstract - This technical report presents QwenLong-CPRS, a context compression framework designed for explicit long-context optimization, addressing prohibitive computation overhead during the prefill stage and the "lost in the middle" performance degradation of large language models (LLMs) during long sequence processing. Implemented through a novel dynamic context optimization mechanism, QwenLong-CPRS enables multi-granularity context compression guided by natural language instructions, achieving both efficiency gains and improved performance. Evolved from the Qwen architecture series, QwenLong-CPRS introduces four key innovations: (1) Natural language-guided dynamic optimization, (2) Bidirectional reasoning layers for enhanced boundary awareness, (3) Token critic mechanisms with language modeling heads, and (4) Window-parallel inference. Comprehensive evaluations across five benchmarks (4K-2M word contexts) demonstrate QwenLong-CPRS's threefold effectiveness: (1) Consistent superiority over other context management methods like RAG and sparse attention in both accuracy and efficiency. (2) Architecture-agnostic integration with all flagship LLMs, including GPT-4o, Gemini2.0-pro, Claude3.7-sonnet, DeepSeek-v3, and Qwen2.5-max, achieves 21.59$\times$ context compression alongside 19.15-point average performance gains; (3) Deployed with Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct, QwenLong-CPRS surpasses leading proprietary LLMs by 4.85 and 10.88 points on Ruler-128K and InfiniteBench, establishing new SOTA performance. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - сЂЊсЂ«ТіђУАЊта▒тЉіТЏИсЂДсЂ»сђЂТўјуц║уџёсЂфжЋиТќЄсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕТюђжЂЕтїќсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕтюДуИ«сЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»QwenLong-CPRSсѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂ»сђЂсЃЌсЃгсЃЋсѓБсЃФТ«хжџјсЂДсЂ«Уеѕу«ЌсѓфсЃ╝сЃљсЃ╝сЃўсЃЃсЃЅсѓњУ╗йТИЏсЂЌсђЂжЋисЂёсѓисЃ╝сѓ▒сЃ│сѓ╣тЄдуљєСИГсЂ«тцДУдЈТеАУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕLLMs№╝ЅсЂ«сђїСИГжќЊсЂДсЂ«тќфтц▒сђЇсЂФсѓѕсѓІТђДУЃйСйјСИІсЂФт»ЙтЄдсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТќ░сЂЌсЂётІЋуџёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕТюђжЂЕтїќсЃАсѓФсЃІсѓ║сЃасѓњжђџсЂўсЂдт«ЪУБЁсЂЋсѓїсЂЪQwenLong-CPRSсЂ»сђЂУЄфуёХУеђУфъсЂ«ТїЄуц║сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈтцџТ«хжџјсЂ«сѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕтюДуИ«сѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЌсђЂті╣ујЄсЂ«тљЉСИісЂеТђДУЃйсЂ«Тћ╣тќёсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ
QwenсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсѓисЃфсЃ╝сѓ║сЂІсѓЅжђ▓тїќсЂЌсЂЪQwenLong-CPRSсЂ»сђЂ4сЂцсЂ«жЄЇУдЂсЂфжЮЕТќ░сѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎ№╝џ(1) УЄфуёХУеђУфъсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈтІЋуџёТюђжЂЕтїќсђЂ(2) тбЃуЋїУфЇУГўсѓњт╝итїќсЂЎсѓІтЈїТќ╣тљЉТјеУФќт▒цсђЂ(3) УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЃўсЃЃсЃЅсѓњТїЂсЂцсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓ»сЃфсЃєсѓБсЃЃсѓ»сЃАсѓФсЃІсѓ║сЃасђЂ(4) сѓдсѓБсЃ│сЃЅсѓдСИдтѕЌТјеУФќсђѓ
5сЂцсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»№╝ѕ4K-2MтЇўУфъсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕ№╝ЅсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсѓІтїЁТІгуџёсЂфУЕЋСЙАсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂQwenLong-CPRSсЂ«СИЅжЄЇсЂ«ті╣ТъюсЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪ№╝џ(1) у▓Йт║дсЂеті╣ујЄсЂ«СИАТќ╣сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсђЂRAGсѓёсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓбсЃєсЃ│сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂфсЂЕсЂ«С╗ќсЂ«сѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕу«АуљєТЅІТ│ЋсЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдСИђУ▓ФсЂЌсЂЪтёфСйЇТђДсѓњТїЂсЂцсђѓ(2) GPT-4oсђЂGemini2.0-proсђЂClaude3.7-sonnetсђЂDeepSeek-v3сђЂQwen2.5-maxсѓњтљФсѓђсЂЎсЂ╣сЂдсЂ«СИ╗УдЂсЂфLLMсЂесЂ«сѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБжЮъСЙЮтГўсЂ«ух▒тљѕсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂ21.59тђЇсЂ«сѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕтюДуИ«сЂе19.15сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ«т╣│тЮЄТђДУЃйтљЉСИісѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓ(3) Qwen2.5-32B-InstructсЂетЁ▒сЂФт▒ЋжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂЪQwenLong-CPRSсЂ»сђЂRuler-128KсЂісѓѕсЂ│InfiniteBenchсЂДсЂ«сЃфсЃ╝сЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сЃЌсЃГсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсѓесѓ┐сЃфLLMsсЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдсЂЮсѓїсЂъсѓї4.85сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂісѓѕсЂ│10.88сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ«тёфСйЇТђДсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂТќ░сЂЪсЂфSOTAТђДУЃйсѓњуб║уФІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - QwenLong-CPRSсЂ»сђЂжЋиТќЄсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕТюђжЂЕтїќсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«Тќ░сЂЌсЂёсЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсђЂLLMsсЂ«ТђДУЃйСйјСИІсѓњУ╗йТИЏсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓУЄфуёХУеђУфъТїЄуц║сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈтцџТ«хжџјсЂ«сѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕтюДуИ«сѓњт«ЪуЈЙсЂЌсђЂті╣ујЄсЂеТђДУЃйсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсѓІ4сЂцсЂ«жЮЕТќ░сѓњт░јтЁЦсђѓ5сЂцсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсђЂС╗ќсЂ«ТЅІТ│ЋсЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдтёфСйЇТђДсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂСИ╗УдЂсЂфLLMсЂесЂ«ух▒тљѕсЂДтцДт╣ЁсЂфсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕтюДуИ«сЂеТђДУЃйтљЉСИісѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓQwenLong-CPRSсЂ»Тќ░сЂЪсЂфSOTAТђДУЃйсѓњуб║уФІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ

paper-entry.title: QwenLong-L1: Towards Long-Context Large Reasoning Models with Reinforcement Learning, Fanqi Wan+, arXiv'25
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, LongSequence, OpenWeight, read-later
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.17667 # Authors - Fanqi Wan - Weizhou Shen - Shengyi Liao - Yingcheng Shi - Chenliang Li - Ziyi Yang - Ji Zhang - Fei Huang - Jingren Zhou - Ming Yan # Abstract - Recent large reasoning models (LRMs) have demonstrated strong reasoning capabilities through reinforcement learning (RL). These improvements have primarily been observed within the short-context reasoning tasks. In contrast, extending LRMs to effectively process and reason on long-context inputs via RL remains a critical unsolved challenge. To bridge this gap, we first formalize the paradigm of long-context reasoning RL, and identify key challenges in suboptimal training efficiency and unstable optimization process. To address these issues, we propose QwenLong-L1, a framework that adapts short-context LRMs to long-context scenarios via progressive context scaling. Specifically, we utilize a warm-up supervised fine-tuning (SFT) stage to establish a robust initial policy, followed by a curriculum-guided phased RL technique to stabilize the policy evolution, and enhanced with a difficulty-aware retrospective sampling strategy to incentivize the policy exploration. Experiments on seven long-context document question-answering benchmarks demonstrate that QwenLong-L1-32B outperforms flagship LRMs like OpenAI-o3-mini and Qwen3-235B-A22B, achieving performance on par with Claude-3.7-Sonnet-Thinking, demonstrating leading performance among state-of-the-art LRMs. This work advances the development of practical long-context LRMs capable of robust reasoning across information-intensive environments. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - ТюђУ┐ЉсЂ«тцДУдЈТеАТјеУФќсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕLRMs№╝ЅсЂ»сђЂт╝итїќтГду┐њ№╝ѕRL№╝ЅсѓњжђџсЂўсЂдт╝итіЏсЂфТјеУФќУЃйтіЏсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«Тћ╣тќёсЂ»СИ╗сЂФуЪГсЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«ТјеУФќсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдУд│т»ЪсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЮсѓїсЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдсђЂLRMsсѓњжЋисЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«тЁЦтіЏсЂФті╣ТъюуџёсЂФжЂЕућесЂЌсђЂТјеУФќсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂ»СЙЮуёХсЂесЂЌсЂджЄЇУдЂсЂфТюфУДБТ▒║сЂ«Уф▓жАїсЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сѓ«сЃБсЃЃсЃЌсѓњтЪІсѓЂсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂсЂЙсЂџжЋисЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕТјеУФќRLсЂ«сЃЉсЃЕсЃђсѓцсЃасѓњтйбт╝ЈтїќсЂЌсђЂТюђжЂЕсЂДсЂфсЂёсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ті╣ујЄсЂеСИЇт«Ѕт«џсЂфТюђжЂЕтїќсЃЌсЃГсѓ╗сѓ╣сЂесЂёсЂєСИ╗УдЂсЂфУф▓жАїсѓњуЅ╣т«џсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«тЋЈжАїсЂФт»ЙтЄдсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуЪГсЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«LRMsсѓњжЋисЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«сѓисЃісЃфсѓфсЂФжЂЕт┐юсЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»QwenLong-L1сѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтЁиСйЊуџёсЂФсЂ»сђЂтаЁуЅбсЂфтѕЮТюЪсЃЮсЃфсѓисЃ╝сѓњуб║уФІсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сѓдсѓЕсЃ╝сЃасѓбсЃЃсЃЌуЏБУдќС╗ўсЂЇсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░№╝ѕSFT№╝Ѕсѓ╣сЃєсЃ╝сѓИсѓњтѕЕућесЂЌсђЂсЂЮсЂ«тЙїсђЂсЃЮсЃфсѓисЃ╝сЂ«жђ▓тїќсѓњт«Ѕт«џсЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сѓФсЃфсѓГсЃЦсЃЕсЃаТїЄт░јтъІТ«хжџјуџёRLТіђУАЊсѓњжЂЕућесЂЌсђЂсЃЮсЃфсѓисЃ╝сЂ«Тјбу┤бсѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«жЏБТўЊт║дУфЇУГўтъІсЂ«тЏъжАДуџёсѓхсЃ│сЃЌсЃфсЃ│сѓ░ТѕдуЋЦсЂДт╝итїќсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ7сЂцсЂ«жЋисЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«ТќЄТЏИУ│фтЋЈт┐юуГћсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІт«ЪжеЊсЂДсЂ»сђЂQwenLong-L1-32BсЂїOpenAI-o3-miniсѓёQwen3-235B-A22BсЂесЂёсЂБсЂЪС╗БУАеуџёсЂфLRMsсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂClaude-3.7-Sonnet-ThinkingсЂетљїуГЅсЂ«сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсђЂТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«LRMsсЂ«СИГсЂДтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓТюгуаћуЕХсЂ»сђЂТЃЁта▒жЏєу┤ётъІуњ░тбЃсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдтаЁуЅбсЂфТјеУФќсЂїтЈ»УЃйсЂфт«ЪућеуџёсЂфжЋисЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕLRMsсЂ«жќІуЎ║сѓњжђ▓сѓЂсѓІсѓѓсЂ«сЂДсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - жЋисЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«ТјеУФќсЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІLRMsсЂ«Уф▓жАїсѓњУДБТ▒║сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсђЂQwenLong-L1сЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сѓњТЈљТАѕсђѓсѓдсѓЕсЃ╝сЃасѓбсЃЃсЃЌуЏБУдќС╗ўсЂЇсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂесѓФсЃфсѓГсЃЦсЃЕсЃаТїЄт░јтъІТ«хжџјуџёRLсѓњућесЂёсЂдсЃЮсЃфсѓисЃ╝сЂ«т«Ѕт«џтїќсѓњтЏ│сѓісђЂжЏБТўЊт║дУфЇУГўтъІсЂ«тЏъжАДуџёсѓхсЃ│сЃЌсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂДТјбу┤бсѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сђѓт«ЪжеЊсЂДсЂ»сђЂQwenLong-L1-32BсЂїС╗ќсЂ«LRMsсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂЪсђѓ

paper-entry.title: AM-RADIO: Agglomerative Vision Foundation Model -- Reduce All Domains Into One, Mike Ranzinger+, CVPR'25
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, Transformer, FoundationModel, OpenWeight, CVPR
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.06709 # Authors - Mike Ranzinger - Greg Heinrich - Jan Kautz - Pavlo Molchanov # Abstract - A handful of visual foundation models (VFMs) have recently emerged as the backbones for numerous downstream tasks. VFMs like CLIP, DINOv2, SAM are trained with distinct objectives, exhibiting unique characteristics for various downstream tasks. We find that despite their conceptual differences, these models can be effectively merged into a unified model through multi-teacher distillation. We name this approach AM-RADIO (Agglomerative Model -- Reduce All Domains Into One). This integrative approach not only surpasses the performance of individual teacher models but also amalgamates their distinctive features, such as zero-shot vision-language comprehension, detailed pixel-level understanding, and open vocabulary segmentation capabilities. In pursuit of the most hardware-efficient backbone, we evaluated numerous architectures in our multi-teacher distillation pipeline using the same training recipe. This led to the development of a novel architecture (E-RADIO) that exceeds the performance of its predecessors and is at least 7x faster than the teacher models. Our comprehensive benchmarking process covers downstream tasks including ImageNet classification, ADE20k semantic segmentation, COCO object detection and LLaVa-1.5 framework. paper-entry.title: Qwen2.5-Omni Technical Report, Jin Xu+, arXiv'25
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, LanguageModel, MultiModal, SpeechProcessing, OpenWeight, Video
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.20215 # Authors - Jin Xu - Zhifang Guo - Jinzheng He - Hangrui Hu - Ting He - Shuai Bai - Keqin Chen - Jialin Wang - Yang Fan - Kai Dang - Bin Zhang - Xiong Wang - Yunfei Chu - Junyang Lin # Abstract - In this report, we present Qwen2.5-Omni, an end-to-end multimodal model designed to perceive diverse modalities, including text, images, audio, and video, while simultaneously generating text and natural speech responses in a streaming manner. To enable the streaming of multimodal information inputs, both audio and visual encoders utilize a block-wise processing approach. To synchronize the timestamps of video inputs with audio, we organize the audio and video sequentially in an interleaved manner and propose a novel position embedding approach, named TMRoPE(Time-aligned Multimodal RoPE). To concurrently generate text and speech while avoiding interference between the two modalities, we propose \textbf{Thinker-Talker} architecture. In this framework, Thinker functions as a large language model tasked with text generation, while Talker is a dual-track autoregressive model that directly utilizes the hidden representations from the Thinker to produce audio tokens as output. Both the Thinker and Talker models are designed to be trained and inferred in an end-to-end manner. For decoding audio tokens in a streaming manner, we introduce a sliding-window DiT that restricts the receptive field, aiming to reduce the initial package delay. Qwen2.5-Omni is comparable with the similarly sized Qwen2.5-VL and outperforms Qwen2-Audio. Furthermore, Qwen2.5-Omni achieves state-of-the-art performance on multimodal benchmarks like Omni-Bench. Notably, Qwen2.5-Omni's performance in end-to-end speech instruction following is comparable to its capabilities with text inputs, as evidenced by benchmarks such as MMLU and GSM8K. As for speech generation, Qwen2.5-Omni's streaming Talker outperforms most existing streaming and non-streaming alternatives in robustness and naturalness. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - Тюгта▒тЉісЂДсЂ»сђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсђЂућ╗тЃЈсђЂжЪ│тБ░сђЂтІЋућ╗сЂфсЂЕсЂ«тцџТДўсЂфсЃбсЃђсЃфсЃєсѓБсѓњУфЇУГўсЂЌсђЂтљїТЎѓсЂФсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂеУЄфуёХсЂфжЪ│тБ░т┐юуГћсѓњсѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃЪсЃ│сѓ░Тќ╣т╝ЈсЂДућЪТѕљсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсѓесЃ│сЃЅсЃёсЃ╝сѓесЃ│сЃЅсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђїQwen2.5-OmniсђЇсѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФТЃЁта▒тЁЦтіЏсЂ«сѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃЪсЃ│сѓ░сѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂжЪ│тБ░сЂісѓѕсЂ│УдќУдџсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЂ»сЃќсЃГсЃЃсѓ»тЇўСйЇсЂ«тЄдуљєсѓбсЃЌсЃГсЃ╝сЃЂсѓњтѕЕућесЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓжЪ│тБ░сЂетІЋућ╗сЂ«сѓ┐сѓцсЃасѓ╣сѓ┐сЃ│сЃЌсѓњтљїТюЪсЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂжЪ│тБ░сЂетІЋућ╗сѓњС║цС║њсЂФжаєт║ЈС╗ўсЂЉсЂдТЋ┤уљєсЂЌсђЂTMRoPE№╝ѕTime-aligned Multimodal RoPE№╝ЅсЂетЉ╝сЂ░сѓїсѓІТќ░сЂЌсЂёСйЇуй«тЪІсѓЂУЙ╝сЂ┐сѓбсЃЌсЃГсЃ╝сЃЂсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂежЪ│тБ░сѓњтљїТЎѓсЂФућЪТѕљсЂЌсђЂСИАсЃбсЃђсЃфсЃєсѓБжќЊсЂ«т╣▓ТИЅсѓњжЂ┐сЂЉсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђїThinker-TalkerсђЇсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сЃЋсЃгсЃ╝сЃасЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсЂ»сђЂThinkerсЂїсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕућЪТѕљсѓњТІЁтйЊсЂЎсѓІтцДУдЈТеАУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂесЂЌсЂдТЕЪУЃйсЂЌсђЂTalkerсЂ»ThinkerсЂІсѓЅсЂ«жџасѓїУАеуЈЙсѓњуЏ┤ТјЦтѕЕућесЂЌсЂджЪ│тБ░сЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњтЄ║тіЏсЂЎсѓІС║їжЄЇсЃѕсЃЕсЃЃсѓ»сЂ«УЄфти▒тЏътИ░сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂЎсђѓThinkerсЂеTalkerсЂ«СИАсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂсѓесЃ│сЃЅсЃёсЃ╝сѓесЃ│сЃЅсЂ«Тќ╣т╝ЈсЂДУеЊуи┤сЂісѓѕсЂ│ТјеУФќсЂЋсѓїсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓжЪ│тБ░сЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњсѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃЪсЃ│сѓ░Тќ╣т╝ЈсЂДсЃЄсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂтЈЌт«╣жЄјсѓњтѕХжЎљсЂЎсѓІсѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сѓдсѓБсЃ│сЃЅсѓдDiTсѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсђЂтѕЮТюЪсЃЉсЃЃсѓ▒сЃ╝сѓИжЂЁт╗ХсѓњТИЏт░ЉсЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуЏ«ТїЄсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓQwen2.5-OmniсЂ»сђЂтљїТДўсЂ«сѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«Qwen2.5-VLсЂеТ»ћУ╝ЃтЈ»УЃйсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂQwen2-AudioсѓњСИітЏъсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂQwen2.5-OmniсЂ»сђЂOmni-BenchсЂ«сѓѕсЂєсЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЅ╣сЂФсђЂсѓесЃ│сЃЅсЃёсЃ╝сѓесЃ│сЃЅсЂ«жЪ│тБ░ТїЄуц║сЂФтЙЊсЂєТђДУЃйсЂ»сђЂMMLUсѓёGSM8KсЂфсЂЕсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсѓѕсЂБсЂдуц║сЂЋсѓїсѓІсѓѕсЂєсЂФсђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕтЁЦтіЏсЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІУЃйтіЏсЂетљїуГЅсЂДсЂЎсђѓжЪ│тБ░ућЪТѕљсЂФжќбсЂЌсЂдсЂ»сђЂQwen2.5-OmniсЂ«сѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃЪсЃ│сѓ░TalkerсЂ»сђЂтаЁуЅбТђДсЂеУЄфуёХсЂЋсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсђЂТЌбтГўсЂ«сЂ╗сЂесѓЊсЂЕсЂ«сѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃЪсЃ│сѓ░сЂісѓѕсЂ│жЮъсѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃЪсЃ│сѓ░сЂ«С╗БТЏ┐ТЅІТ«хсѓњСИітЏъсЂБсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђїQwen2.5-OmniсђЇсЂ»сђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсђЂућ╗тЃЈсђЂжЪ│тБ░сђЂтІЋућ╗сѓњУфЇУГўсЂЌсђЂсѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃЪсЃ│сѓ░Тќ╣т╝ЈсЂДУЄфуёХсЂфжЪ│тБ░т┐юуГћсѓњућЪТѕљсЂЎсѓІсђѓжЪ│тБ░сЂеУдќУдџсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЂ»сЃќсЃГсЃЃсѓ»тЄдуљєсѓњућесЂёсђЂTMRoPEсЂФсѓѕсѓІТќ░сЂЌсЂёСйЇуй«тЪІсѓЂУЙ╝сЂ┐сЂДжЪ│тБ░сЂетІЋућ╗сЂ«тљїТюЪсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсђѓThinker-TalkerсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕућЪТѕљсЂежЪ│тБ░тЄ║тіЏсѓњт╣▓ТИЅсЂфсЂЈУАїсЂєсђѓQwen2.5-OmniсЂ»сђЂсѓесЃ│сЃЅсЃёсЃ╝сѓесЃ│сЃЅсЂДУеЊуи┤сЂЋсѓїсђЂжЪ│тБ░ТїЄуц║сЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІТђДУЃйсЂїсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕтЁЦтіЏсЂетљїуГЅсЂДсђЂсѓ╣сЃѕсЃфсЃ╝сЃЪсЃ│сѓ░TalkerсЂ»ТЌбтГўТЅІТ│ЋсѓњСИітЏъсѓІУЄфуёХсЂЋсѓњТїЂсЂцсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Tulu 3: Pushing Frontiers in Open Language Model Post-Training, Nathan Lambert+, COLM'25, 2024.11
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, Supervised-FineTuning (SFT), OpenWeight, OpenSource, COLM, DPO, PostTraining, read-later, RLVR, Selected Papers/Blogs
paper-entry.issue-body:
# URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.15124 # Authors - Nathan Lambert - Jacob Morrison - Valentina Pyatkin - Shengyi Huang - Hamish Ivison - Faeze Brahman - Lester James V. Miranda - Alisa Liu - Nouha Dziri - Shane Lyu - Yuling Gu - Saumya Malik - Victoria Graf - Jena D. Hwang - Jiangjiang Yang - Ronan Le Bras - Oyvind Tafjord - Chris Wilhelm - Luca Soldaini - Noah A. Smith - Yizhong Wang - Pradeep Dasigi - Hannaneh Hajishirzi # Abstract - Language model post-training is applied to refine behaviors and unlock new skills across a wide range of recent language models, but open recipes for applying these techniques lag behind proprietary ones. The underlying training data and recipes for post-training are simultaneously the most important pieces of the puzzle and the portion with the least transparency. To bridge this gap, we introduce Tulu 3, a family of fully-open state-of-the-art post-trained models, alongside its data, code, and training recipes, serving as a comprehensive guide for modern post-training techniques. Tulu 3, which builds on Llama 3.1 base models, achieves results surpassing the instruct versions of Llama 3.1, Qwen 2.5, Mistral, and even closed models such as GPT-4o-mini and Claude 3.5-Haiku. The training algorithms for our models include supervised finetuning (SFT), Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), and a novel method we call Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR). With Tulu 3, we introduce a multi-task evaluation scheme for post-training recipes with development and unseen evaluations, standard benchmark implementations, and substantial decontamination of existing open datasets on said benchmarks. We conclude with analysis and discussion of training methods that did not reliably improve performance. In addition to the Tulu 3 model weights and demo, we release the complete recipe -- including datasets for diverse core skills, a robust toolkit for data curation and evaluation, the training code and infrastructure, and, most importantly, a detailed report for reproducing and further adapting the Tulu 3 approach to more domains. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂ»сђЂТюђУ┐ЉсЂ«сЂЋсЂЙсЂќсЂЙсЂфУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдУАїтІЋсѓњТ┤Ќуи┤сЂЌсђЂТќ░сЂЌсЂёсѓ╣сѓГсЃФсѓњУДБТћЙсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФжЂЕућесЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсЂїсђЂсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«ТіђУАЊсѓњжЂЕућесЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфсЃгсѓисЃћсЂ»сђЂуІгУЄфсЂ«сѓѓсЂ«сЂФТ»ћсЂ╣сЂджЂЁсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«тЪ║уцјсЂесЂфсѓІсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂесЃгсѓисЃћсЂ»сђЂТюђсѓѓжЄЇУдЂсЂфУдЂу┤асЂДсЂѓсѓісЂфсЂїсѓЅсђЂТюђсѓѓжђЈТўјТђДсЂїТгасЂЉсЂдсЂёсѓІжЃетѕєсЂДсѓѓсЂѓсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сѓ«сЃБсЃЃсЃЌсѓњтЪІсѓЂсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»Tulu 3сѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂ»сђЂт«їтЁесЂФсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂсЂЮсЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сђЂсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃгсѓисЃћсЂесЂесѓѓсЂФТЈљСЙЏсЂЋсѓїсђЂуЈЙС╗БсЂ«сЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТіђУАЊсЂ«тїЁТІгуџёсЂфсѓгсѓцсЃЅсЂесЂЌсЂдТЕЪУЃйсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓTulu 3сЂ»Llama 3.1сЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњтЪ║сЂФсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂLlama 3.1сЂ«сѓцсЃ│сѓ╣сЃѕсЃЕсѓ»сѓисЃДсЃ│сЃљсЃ╝сѓИсЃДсЃ│сђЂQwen 2.5сђЂMistralсђЂсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсЂ»GPT-4o-miniсѓёClaude 3.5-HaikuсЂесЂёсЂБсЂЪсѓ»сЃГсЃ╝сѓ║сЃЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњСИітЏъсѓІухљТъюсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓбсЃФсѓ┤сЃфсѓ║сЃасЂФсЂ»сђЂТЋЎтИФсЂѓсѓісЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░№╝ѕSFT№╝ЅсђЂуЏ┤ТјЦуџёсЂфтЦйсЂ┐ТюђжЂЕтїќ№╝ѕDPO№╝ЅсђЂсЂісѓѕсЂ│сђїТцюУе╝тЈ»УЃйсЂфта▒жЁгсѓњућесЂёсЂЪт╝итїќтГду┐њ№╝ѕRLVR№╝ЅсђЇсЂетЉ╝сЂХТќ░сЂЌсЂёТЅІТ│ЋсЂїтљФсЂЙсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓTulu 3сЂДсЂ»сђЂсЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃгсѓисЃћсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»УЕЋСЙАсѓ╣сѓГсЃ╝сЃасѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсђЂжќІуЎ║сЂісѓѕсЂ│ТюфУдІсЂ«УЕЋСЙАсђЂТеЎТ║ќуџёсЂфсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»т«ЪУБЁсђЂсЂЮсЂЌсЂдсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІТЌбтГўсЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂ«тцДт╣ЁсЂфжЎцТЪЊсѓњУАїсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТюђтЙїсЂФсђЂсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњС┐Ажа╝ТђДжФўсЂЈтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсЂфсЂІсЂБсЂЪсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТЅІТ│ЋсЂ«тѕєТъљсЂеУГ░УФќсѓњУАїсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ
Tulu 3сЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓдсѓДсѓцсЃѕсЂесЃЄсЃбсЂФтіасЂѕсЂдсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»т«їтЁесЂфсЃгсѓисЃћсѓњтЁгжќІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсЂ»сђЂтцџТДўсЂфсѓ│сѓбсѓ╣сѓГсЃФсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсђЂсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓГсЃЦсЃгсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂеУЕЋСЙАсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«таЁуЅбсЂфсЃёсЃ╝сЃФсѓГсЃЃсЃѕсђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂесѓцсЃ│сЃЋсЃЕсѓ╣сЃѕсЃЕсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсђЂсЂЮсЂЌсЂдТюђсѓѓжЄЇУдЂсЂфсЂЊсЂесЂесЂЌсЂдсђЂTulu 3сѓбсЃЌсЃГсЃ╝сЃЂсѓњС╗ќсЂ«сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂФтєЇуЈЙсЂЌсђЂсЂЋсѓЅсЂФжЂЕт┐юсЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«УЕ│у┤░сЂфсЃгсЃЮсЃ╝сЃѕсЂїтљФсЂЙсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - Tulu 3сЂ»сђЂсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфсЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сЂДсђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓёсЃгсѓисЃћсѓњтЁгжќІсЂЌсђЂуЈЙС╗БсЂ«сЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТіђУАЊсЂ«сѓгсѓцсЃЅсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓLlama 3.1сѓњтЪ║сЂФсЂЌсђЂС╗ќсЂ«сѓ»сЃГсЃ╝сѓ║сЃЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњСИітЏъсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓТќ░сЂЌсЂёсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТЅІТ│ЋсЂесЂЌсЂдSFTсђЂDPOсђЂRLVRсѓњТјАућесЂЌсђЂсЃъсЃФсЃЂсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»УЕЋСЙАсѓ╣сѓГсЃ╝сЃасѓњт░јтЁЦсђѓсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓдсѓДсѓцсЃѕсѓёсЃЄсЃбсђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсђЂсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂфсЂЕсѓњтЁгжќІсЂЌсђЂС╗ќсЂ«сЃЅсЃАсѓцсЃ│сЂИсЂ«жЂЕт┐юсѓѓтЈ»УЃйсЂДсЂЎсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Quantifying Language Models' Sensitivity to Spurious Features in Prompt Design or: How I learned to start worrying about prompt formatting, Melanie Sclar+, ICLR'24, 2023.10
paper-entry.tags: Analysis, NLP, LanguageModel, Prompting, Evaluation, OpenWeight, ICLR, Selected Papers/Blogs
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.11324 # Authors - Melanie Sclar - Yejin Choi - Yulia Tsvetkov - Alane Suhr # Abstract - As large language models (LLMs) are adopted as a fundamental component of language technologies, it is crucial to accurately characterize their performance. Because choices in prompt design can strongly influence model behavior, this design process is critical in effectively using any modern pre-trained generative language model. In this work, we focus on LLM sensitivity to a quintessential class of meaning-preserving design choices: prompt formatting. We find that several widely used open-source LLMs are extremely sensitive to subtle changes in prompt formatting in few-shot settings, with performance differences of up to 76 accuracy points when evaluated using LLaMA-2-13B. Sensitivity remains even when increasing model size, the number of few-shot examples, or performing instruction tuning. Our analysis suggests that work evaluating LLMs with prompting-based methods would benefit from reporting a range of performance across plausible prompt formats, instead of the currently-standard practice of reporting performance on a single format. We also show that format performance only weakly correlates between models, which puts into question the methodological validity of comparing models with an arbitrarily chosen, fixed prompt format. To facilitate systematic analysis we propose FormatSpread, an algorithm that rapidly evaluates a sampled set of plausible prompt formats for a given task, and reports the interval of expected performance without accessing model weights. Furthermore, we present a suite of analyses that characterize the nature of this sensitivity, including exploring the influence of particular atomic perturbations and the internal representation of particular formats. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - тцДУдЈТеАУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕLLM№╝ЅсЂїУеђУфъТіђУАЊсЂ«тЪ║уЏцсѓ│сЃ│сЃЮсЃ╝сЃЇсЃ│сЃѕсЂесЂЌсЂдТјАућесЂЋсѓїсѓІСИГсђЂсЂЮсЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњТГБуб║сЂФуЅ╣ТђДтїќсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїжЄЇУдЂсЂДсЂЎсђѓсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕУеГУеѕсЂ«жЂИТіъсЂїсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«ТїЎтІЋсЂФт╝исЂЈтй▒жЪ┐сѓњСИјсЂѕсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсђЂсЂЊсЂ«УеГУеѕсЃЌсЃГсѓ╗сѓ╣сЂ»сђЂуЈЙС╗БсЂ«С║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТИѕсЂ┐ућЪТѕљУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњті╣ТъюуџёсЂФСй┐ућесЂЎсѓІСИісЂДжЄЇУдЂсЂДсЂЎсђѓТюгуаћуЕХсЂДсЂ»сђЂТёЈтЉ│сѓњС┐ЮТїЂсЂЎсѓІУеГУеѕжЂИТіъсЂ«жЄЇУдЂсЂфсѓ»сЃЕсѓ╣сЂДсЂѓсѓІсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІLLMсЂ«ТёЪт║дсЂФуёдуѓ╣сѓњтйЊсЂдсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂёсЂЈсЂцсЂІсЂ«т║ЃсЂЈСй┐ућесЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣LLMсЂ»сђЂт░ЉТЋ░сѓисЃДсЃЃсЃѕУеГт«џсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«тЙ«тдЎсЂфтцЅТЏ┤сЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдТЦхсѓЂсЂдТЋЈТёЪсЂДсђЂLLaMA-2-13BсѓњућесЂёсЂдУЕЋСЙАсЂЎсѓІсЂесђЂТюђтцД76сЂ«у▓Йт║дсЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ«ТђДУЃйти«сЂїУдІсѓЅсѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТёЪт║дсЂ»сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сѓхсѓцсѓ║сѓёт░ЉТЋ░сѓисЃДсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«СЙІсЂ«ТЋ░сѓњтбЌсѓёсЂЌсЂЪсѓісђЂТїЄуц║сЂ«сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњУАїсЂБсЂЪсѓісЂЌсЂдсѓѓТ«ІсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«тѕєТъљсЂ»сђЂсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«Тќ╣Т│ЋсЂДLLMсѓњУЕЋСЙАсЂЎсѓІСйюТЦГсЂ»сђЂуЈЙтюесЂ«ТеЎТ║ќуџёсЂфт«ЪУихсЂДсЂѓсѓІтЇўСИђсЂ«сЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂДсЂ«ТђДУЃйта▒тЉісЂДсЂ»сЂфсЂЈсђЂсЂѓсѓітЙЌсѓІУцЄТЋ░сЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсѓІТђДУЃйсЂ«у»ётЏ▓сѓњта▒тЉісЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДтѕЕуЏісѓњтЙЌсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїсЂДсЂЇсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуц║тћєсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЙсЂЪсђЂсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сЂїсЃбсЃЄсЃФжќЊсЂДт╝▒сЂЈуЏИжќбсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓѓуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂісѓісђЂТЂБТёЈуџёсЂФжЂИсЂ░сѓїсЂЪтЏ║т«џсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂДсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЎсѓІТќ╣Т│ЋУФќуџётдЦтйЊТђДсЂФуќЉтЋЈсѓњТіЋсЂњсЂІсЂЉсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓСйЊу│╗уџёсЂфтѕєТъљсѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуЅ╣т«џсЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдсЂѓсѓітЙЌсѓІсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«сѓхсЃ│сЃЌсЃфсЃ│сѓ░сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсѓњУ┐ЁжђЪсЂФУЕЋСЙАсЂЌсђЂсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«жЄЇсЂ┐сѓњтЈѓуЁДсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂфсЂЈТюЪтЙЁсЂЋсѓїсѓІсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сЂ«у»ётЏ▓сѓњта▒тЉісЂЎсѓІсѓбсЃФсѓ┤сЃфсѓ║сЃасђїFormatSpreadсђЇсѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂсЂЊсЂ«ТёЪт║дсЂ«ТђДУ│фсѓњуЅ╣ТђДтїќсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«СИђжђБсЂ«тѕєТъљсѓњТЈљуц║сЂЌсђЂуЅ╣т«џсЂ«тјЪтГљуџёсЂфТЉѓтІЋсЂ«тй▒жЪ┐сѓёуЅ╣т«џсЂ«сЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«тєЁжЃеУАеуЈЙсѓњТјбсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - LLMсЂ«ТђДУЃйуЅ╣ТђДтїќсЂїжЄЇУдЂсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕУеГУеѕсЂїсЃбсЃЄсЃФТїЎтІЋсЂФт╝исЂЈтй▒жЪ┐сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуц║сЂЎсђѓуЅ╣сЂФсђЂсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂФт»ЙсЂЎсѓІLLMсЂ«ТёЪт║дсЂФТ│еуЏ«сЂЌсђЂтЙ«тдЎсЂфтцЅТЏ┤сЂДТюђтцД76сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ«ТђДУЃйти«сЂїУдІсѓЅсѓїсѓІсђѓТёЪт║дсЂ»сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓхсѓцсѓ║сѓёт░ЉТЋ░сѓисЃДсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«ТЋ░сЂФСЙЮтГўсЂЏсЂџсђЂсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЂ«тцџТДўсЂфсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсѓІТђДУЃйу»ётЏ▓сЂ«та▒тЉісЂїт┐ЁУдЂсђѓсЃбсЃЄсЃФжќЊсЂ«сЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сЂїт╝▒сЂЈуЏИжќбсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂІсѓЅсђЂтЏ║т«џсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂДсЂ«Т»ћУ╝ЃсЂ«тдЦтйЊТђДсЂїуќЉтЋЈУдќсЂЋсѓїсѓІсђѓУ┐ЁжђЪсЂфсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕУЕЋСЙАсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сђїFormatSpreadсђЇсѓбсЃФсѓ┤сЃфсѓ║сЃасѓњТЈљТАѕсЂЌсђЂТЉѓтІЋсЂ«тй▒жЪ┐сѓётєЁжЃеУАеуЈЙсѓѓТјбсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Aria: An Open Multimodal Native Mixture-of-Experts Model, Dongxu Li+, arXiv'24, 2024.10
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, LanguageModel, MultiModal, OpenWeight, MoE(Mixture-of-Experts), VisionLanguageModel
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.05993 # Authors - Dongxu Li - Yudong Liu - Haoning Wu - Yue Wang - Zhiqi Shen - Bowen Qu - Xinyao Niu - Fan Zhou - Chengen Huang - Yanpeng Li - Chongyan Zhu - Xiaoyi Ren - Chao Li - Yifan Ye - Peng Liu - Lihuan Zhang - Hanshu Yan - Guoyin Wang - Bei Chen - Junnan Li # Abstract - Information comes in diverse modalities. Multimodal native AI models are essential to integrate real-world information and deliver comprehensive understanding. While proprietary multimodal native models exist, their lack of openness imposes obstacles for adoptions, let alone adaptations. To fill this gap, we introduce Aria, an open multimodal native model with best-in-class performance across a wide range of multimodal, language, and coding tasks. Aria is a mixture-of-expert model with 3.9B and 3.5B activated parameters per visual token and text token, respectively. It outperforms Pixtral-12B and Llama3.2-11B, and is competitive against the best proprietary models on various multimodal tasks. We pre-train Aria from scratch following a 4-stage pipeline, which progressively equips the model with strong capabilities in language understanding, multimodal understanding, long context window, and instruction following. We open-source the model weights along with a codebase that facilitates easy adoptions and adaptations of Aria in real-world applications. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - ТЃЁта▒сЂ»тцџТДўсЂфсЃбсЃђсЃфсЃєсѓБсЂДТЈљСЙЏсЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃЇсѓцсЃєсѓБсЃќAIсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂуЈЙт«ЪСИќуЋїсЂ«ТЃЁта▒сѓњух▒тљѕсЂЌсђЂтїЁТІгуџёсЂфуљєУДБсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФСИЇтЈ»ТгасЂДсЂЎсђѓТЌбтГўсЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсѓесѓ┐сЃфсЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃЇсѓцсЃєсѓБсЃќсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓѓтГўтюесЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсЂїсђЂсЂЮсЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│ТђДсЂ«ТгатдѓсЂ»ТјАућесѓёжЂЕт┐юсЂФжџют«│сѓњсѓѓсЂЪсѓЅсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сѓ«сЃБсЃЃсЃЌсѓњтЪІсѓЂсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»Ariaсѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓAriaсЂ»сђЂт╣Ёт║ЃсЂёсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсђЂУеђУфъсђЂсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдТюђжФўсЂ«сЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЎсѓІсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃЇсѓцсЃєсѓБсЃќсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂЎсђѓAriaсЂ»сђЂУдќУдџсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂћсЂесЂФ3.9BсђЂсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂћсЂесЂФ3.5BсЂ«Т┤╗ТђДтїќсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњТїЂсЂцсѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃѕсЂ«ТиитљѕсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂЎсђѓPixtral-12BсѓёLlama3.2-11BсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂсЂЋсЂЙсЂќсЂЙсЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдТюђжФўсЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсѓесѓ┐сЃфсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂеуФХС║ЅтіЏсЂїсЂѓсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂУеђУфъуљєУДБсђЂсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФуљєУДБсђЂжЋисЂёсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсѓдсѓБсЃ│сЃЅсѓдсђЂТїЄуц║сЂФтЙЊсЂєУЃйтіЏсѓњТ«хжџјуџёсЂФт╝итїќсЂЎсѓІ4Т«хжџјсЂ«сЃЉсѓцсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЂФтЙЊсЂБсЂдсђЂAriaсѓњсѓ╝сЃГсЂІсѓЅС║ІтЅЇсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂAriaсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓдсѓДсѓцсЃѕсЂесђЂт«ЪСИќуЋїсЂ«сѓбсЃЌсЃфсѓ▒сЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂФсЂісЂЉсѓІт«╣ТўЊсЂфТјАућесЂежЂЕт┐юсѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сѓњсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣тїќсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - AriaсЂ»сђЂсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃЇсѓцсЃєсѓБсЃќAIсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂУдќУдџсЂесЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂджФўсЂёТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ3.9BсЂ«УдќУдџсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂе3.5BсЂ«сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњТїЂсЂцсѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃѕсЂ«ТиитљѕсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂТЌбтГўсЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсѓесѓ┐сЃфсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњСИітЏъсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓУеђУфъуљєУДБсѓёсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФуљєУДБсѓњт╝итїќсЂЎсѓІ4Т«хжџјсЂ«сЃЉсѓцсЃЌсЃЕсѓцсЃ│сЂДС║ІтЅЇсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂЋсѓїсђЂсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓдсѓДсѓцсЃѕсЂесѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ»сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂесЂЌсЂдТЈљСЙЏсЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ

maintainer-comment-text.p: ТЈљТАѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪтйЊТЎѓ2024т╣┤10ТюѕТЎѓуѓ╣сЂДсђЂVisionсЂеText UnderstandingтЈїТќ╣сЂДсЂФт╝исЂётѕЮсѓЂсЂдсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂтѕЮсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФMoEсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂД№╝ѕтйЊТЎѓсЂЙсЂаУЕ▒жАїсЂФсЂфсЂБсЂдсЂёсЂфсЂІсЂБсЂЪDeepSeek-V2сѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсѓњТјАуће№╝ЅсђЂLongVideoсЂ«UnderstanidinpсЂДтйЊТЎѓсЂ«ТюђжФўТђДУЃйсЂДсЂѓсЂБсЂЪсЂесЂ«сЂЊсЂесђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report, DeepSeek-AI+, arXiv'24, 2024.12
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, action_wanted, OpenWeight, Selected Papers/Blogs, KeyPoint Notes, Reference Collection
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19437 # Authors - DeepSeek-AI - Aixin Liu - Bei Feng - Bing Xue - Bingxuan Wang - Bochao Wu - Chengda Lu - Chenggang Zhao - Chengqi Deng - Chenyu Zhang - Chong Ruan - Damai Dai - Daya Guo - Dejian Yang - Deli Chen - Dongjie Ji - Erhang Li - Fangyun Lin - Fucong Dai - Fuli Luo - Guangbo Hao - Guanting Chen - Guowei Li - H. Zhang - Han Bao - Hanwei Xu - Haocheng Wang - Haowei Zhang - Honghui Ding - Huajian Xin - Huazuo Gao - Hui Li - Hui Qu - J. L. Cai - Jian Liang - Jianzhong Guo - Jiaqi Ni - Jiashi Li - Jiawei Wang - Jin Chen - Jingchang Chen - Jingyang Yuan - Junjie Qiu - Junlong Li - Junxiao Song - Kai Dong - Kai Hu - Kaige Gao - Kang Guan - Kexin Huang - Kuai Yu - Lean Wang - Lecong Zhang - Lei Xu - Leyi Xia - Liang Zhao - Litong Wang - Liyue Zhang - Meng Li - Miaojun Wang - Mingchuan Zhang - Minghua Zhang - Minghui Tang - Mingming Li - Ning Tian - Panpan Huang - Peiyi Wang - Peng Zhang - Qiancheng Wang - Qihao Zhu - Qinyu Chen - Qiushi Du - R. J. Chen - R. L. Jin - Ruiqi Ge - Ruisong Zhang - Ruizhe Pan - Runji Wang - Runxin Xu - Ruoyu Zhang - Ruyi Chen - S. S. Li - Shanghao Lu - Shangyan Zhou - Shanhuang Chen - Shaoqing Wu - Shengfeng Ye - Shengfeng Ye - Shirong Ma - Shiyu Wang - Shuang Zhou - Shuiping Yu - Shunfeng Zhou - Shuting Pan - T. Wang - Tao Yun - Tian Pei - Tianyu Sun - W. L. Xiao - Wangding Zeng - Wanjia Zhao - Wei An - Wen Liu - Wenfeng Liang - Wenjun Gao - Wenqin Yu - Wentao Zhang - X. Q. Li - Xiangyue Jin - Xianzu Wang - Xiao Bi - Xiaodong Liu - Xiaohan Wang - Xiaojin Shen - Xiaokang Chen - Xiaokang Zhang - Xiaosha Chen - Xiaotao Nie - Xiaowen Sun - Xiaoxiang Wang - Xin Cheng - Xin Liu - Xin Xie - Xingchao Liu - Xingkai Yu - Xinnan Song - Xinxia Shan - Xinyi Zhou - Xinyu Yang - Xinyuan Li - Xuecheng Su - Xuheng Lin - Y. K. Li - Y. Q. Wang - Y. X. Wei - Y. X. Zhu - Yang Zhang - Yanhong Xu - Yanhong Xu - Yanping Huang - Yao Li - Yao Zhao - Yaofeng Sun - Yaohui Li - Yaohui Wang - Yi Yu - Yi Zheng - Yichao Zhang - Yifan Shi - Yiliang Xiong - Ying He - Ying Tang - Yishi Piao - Yisong Wang - Yixuan Tan - Yiyang Ma - Yiyuan Liu - Yongqiang Guo - Yu Wu - Yuan Ou - Yuchen Zhu - Yuduan Wang - Yue Gong - Yuheng Zou - Yujia He - Yukun Zha - Yunfan Xiong - Yunxian Ma - Yuting Yan - Yuxiang Luo - Yuxiang You - Yuxuan Liu - Yuyang Zhou - Z. F. Wu - Z. Z. Ren - Zehui Ren - Zhangli Sha - Zhe Fu - Zhean Xu - Zhen Huang - Zhen Zhang - Zhenda Xie - Zhengyan Zhang - Zhewen Hao - Zhibin Gou - Zhicheng Ma - Zhigang Yan - Zhihong Shao - Zhipeng Xu - Zhiyu Wu - Zhongyu Zhang - Zhuoshu Li - Zihui Gu - Zijia Zhu - Zijun Liu - Zilin Li - Ziwei Xie - Ziyang Song - Ziyi Gao - Zizheng Pan # Abstract - We present DeepSeek-V3, a strong Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language model with 671B total parameters with 37B activated for each token. To achieve efficient inference and cost-effective training, DeepSeek-V3 adopts Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) and DeepSeekMoE architectures, which were thoroughly validated in DeepSeek-V2. Furthermore, DeepSeek-V3 pioneers an auxiliary-loss-free strategy for load balancing and sets a multi-token prediction training objective for stronger performance. We pre-train DeepSeek-V3 on 14.8 trillion diverse and high-quality tokens, followed by Supervised Fine-Tuning and Reinforcement Learning stages to fully harness its capabilities. Comprehensive evaluations reveal that DeepSeek-V3 outperforms other open-source models and achieves performance comparable to leading closed-source models. Despite its excellent performance, DeepSeek-V3 requires only 2.788M H800 GPU hours for its full training. In addition, its training process is remarkably stable. Throughout the entire training process, we did not experience any irrecoverable loss spikes or perform any rollbacks. The model checkpoints are available at https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3. # Translation (by gpt-5-nano) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂуиЈсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐6710тёёсђЂтљёсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂФсЂцсЂЇ37BсЂ«сЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂїТ┤╗ТђДтїќсЂЋсѓїсѓІт╝итіЏсЂфMixture-of-Experts№╝ѕMoE№╝ЅУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђЂDeepSeek-V3сѓњТЈљуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТјеУФќсѓњті╣ујЄтїќсЂЌсђЂУеЊуи┤сѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕсѓњТіЉсЂѕсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсђЂDeepSeek-V3сЂ»Multi-head Latent Attention№╝ѕMLA№╝ЅсЂісѓѕсЂ│DeepSeekMoEсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсѓњТјАућесЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ»DeepSeek-V2сЂДтЙ╣т║ЋуџёсЂФТцюУе╝сЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂDeepSeek-V3сЂ»сЃГсЃ╝сЃЅсЃљсЃЕсЃ│сѓисЃ│сѓ░сЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂ«УБютіЕТљЇтц▒сѓњућесЂёсЂфсЂёТѕдуЋЦсѓњТЈљтћ▒сЂЌсђЂсѓѕсѓіт╝итіЏсЂфТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂ«сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│С║ѕТИгУеЊуи┤уЏ«ТеЎсѓњУеГт«џсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓDeepSeek-V3сѓњ14.8тЁєтђІсЂ«тцџТДўсЂДжФўтЊЂУ│фсЂфсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂДС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЌсђЂсЂЮсЂ«тЙїсђЂТЋЎтИФсЂѓсѓісЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂет╝итїќтГду┐њсЂ«Т«хжџјсѓњухїсЂдсђЂсЂЮсЂ«УЃйтіЏсѓњтЇЂтѕєсЂФТ┤╗ућесЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтїЁТІгуџёсЂфУЕЋСЙАсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂDeepSeek-V3сЂ»С╗ќсЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сѓ»сЃГсЃ╝сѓ║сЃЅсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЎсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтЇЊУХісЂЌсЂЪТђДУЃйсЂФсѓѓсЂІсЂІсѓЈсѓЅсЂџсђЂDeepSeek-V3сЂ«тЁеУеЊуи┤сЂФсЂ»сђЂу┤ё2,788,000ТЎѓжќЊсЂ«H800 GPUТЎѓжќЊсЂЌсЂІт┐ЁУдЂсЂѓсѓісЂЙсЂЏсѓЊсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂУеЊуи┤жЂјуеІсЂ»жЮътИИсЂФт«Ѕт«џсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂУеЊуи┤тЁеСйЊсѓњжђџсЂўсЂдтЏътЙЕСИЇУЃйсЂфТљЇтц▒сЂ«ТђЦтбЌсѓњухїжеЊсЂЏсЂџсђЂсЃГсЃ╝сЃФсЃљсЃЃсѓ»сѓѓУАїсЂёсЂЙсЂЏсѓЊсЂДсЂЌсЂЪсђѓсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ» https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3 сЂДтѕЕућетЈ»УЃйсЂДсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - уиЈсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐6710тёёсЂ«Mixture-of-Experts№╝ѕMoE№╝ЅУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФDeepSeek-V3сѓњТЈљТАѕсђѓMLAсЂісѓѕсЂ│DeepSeekMoEсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсѓњТјАућесЂЌсђЂті╣ујЄуџёсЂфТјеУФќсЂеСйјУеЊуи┤сѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсђѓУБютіЕТљЇтц▒сѓњућесЂёсЂџсђЂсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│С║ѕТИгУеЊуи┤уЏ«ТеЎсЂДТђДУЃйсѓњтљЉСИісђѓ14.8тЁєсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂДС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЌсђЂТЋЎтИФсЂѓсѓісЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњухїсЂдтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓС╗ќсЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂетЁѕуФ»сЂ«сѓ»сЃГсЃ╝сѓ║сЃЅсѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЌсђЂУеЊуи┤сЂ»у┤ё2,788,000ТЎѓжќЊсЂ«H800 GPUТЎѓжќЊсЂДт«Ѕт«џсЂЌсЂдУАїсѓЈсѓїсЂЪсђѓсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ»тЁгжќІСИГсђѓ

paper-entry.title: Phi-4 Technical Report, Marah Abdin+, arXiv'24
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, OpenWeight
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.08905 # Authors - Marah Abdin - Jyoti Aneja - Harkirat Behl - S├Еbastien Bubeck - Ronen Eldan - Suriya Gunasekar - Michael Harrison - Russell J. Hewett - Mojan Javaheripi - Piero Kauffmann - James R. Lee - Yin Tat Lee - Yuanzhi Li - Weishung Liu - Caio C. T. Mendes - Anh Nguyen - Eric Price - Gustavo de Rosa - Olli Saarikivi - Adil Salim - Shital Shah - Xin Wang - Rachel Ward - Yue Wu - Dingli Yu - Cyril Zhang - Yi Zhang # Abstract - We present phi-4, a 14-billion parameter language model developed with a training recipe that is centrally focused on data quality. Unlike most language models, where pre-training is based primarily on organic data sources such as web content or code, phi-4 strategically incorporates synthetic data throughout the training process. While previous models in the Phi family largely distill the capabilities of a teacher model (specifically GPT-4), phi-4 substantially surpasses its teacher model on STEM-focused QA capabilities, giving evidence that our data-generation and post-training techniques go beyond distillation. Despite minimal changes to the phi-3 architecture, phi-4 achieves strong performance relative to its size -- especially on reasoning-focused benchmarks -- due to improved data, training curriculum, and innovations in the post-training scheme. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«У│фсЂФСИГт┐Ѓсѓњуй«сЂёсЂЪсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃгсѓисЃћсЂДжќІуЎ║сЂЋсѓїсЂЪсђЂ140тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђїphi-4сђЇсѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂ╗сЂесѓЊсЂЕсЂ«УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂїСИ╗сЂФсѓдсѓДсЃќсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсЃ│сЃёсѓёсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂфсЂЕсЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сѓгсЃІсЃЃсѓ»сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂёсЂдС║ІтЅЇсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњУАїсЂєсЂ«сЂФт»ЙсЂЌсђЂphi-4сЂ»сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЌсЃГсѓ╗сѓ╣тЁеСйЊсЂФсѓЈсЂЪсЂБсЂдТѕдуЋЦуџёсЂФтљѕТѕљсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњтЈќсѓітЁЦсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓPhiсЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сЂ«С╗ЦтЅЇсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»СИ╗сЂФТЋЎтИФсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕуЅ╣сЂФGPT-4№╝ЅсЂ«УЃйтіЏсѓњУњИуЋЎсЂЎсѓІсѓѓсЂ«сЂДсЂЌсЂЪсЂїсђЂphi-4сЂ»STEMсЂФуёдуѓ╣сѓњтйЊсЂдсЂЪQAУЃйтіЏсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдТЋЎтИФсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњтцДт╣ЁсЂФСИітЏъсѓісђЂуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐ућЪТѕљсЂісѓѕсЂ│сЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТіђУАЊсЂїУњИуЋЎсѓњУХЁсЂѕсЂдсЂёсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓphi-3сЂ«сѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсЂФТюђт░ЈжЎљсЂ«тцЅТЏ┤сѓњтіасЂѕсЂЪсЂасЂЉсЂДсђЂphi-4сЂ»сЂЮсЂ«сѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдт╝итіЏсЂфсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуЅ╣сЂФТјеУФќсЂФуёдуѓ╣сѓњтйЊсЂдсЂЪсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсЂ»сђЂТћ╣тќёсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓФсЃфсѓГсЃЦсЃЕсЃасђЂсЂісѓѕсЂ│сЃЮсѓ╣сЃѕсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓ╣сѓГсЃ╝сЃасЂ«жЮЕТќ░сЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂсЂЮсЂ«ТђДУЃйсЂїтљЉСИісЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - 140тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсђїphi-4сђЇсЂ»сђЂтљѕТѕљсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњтЈќсѓітЁЦсѓїсЂЪсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂSTEMсЂФуЅ╣тїќсЂЌсЂЪQAУЃйтіЏсЂДТЋЎтИФсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњтцДт╣ЁсЂФСИітЏъсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЎсђѓphi-3сЂ«сѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсѓњТюђт░ЈжЎљсЂФтцЅТЏ┤сЂЌсЂЪсЂасЂЉсЂДсђЂТјеУФќсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсѓѓТћ╣тќёсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂесЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТЅІТ│ЋсЂФсѓѕсѓіт╝итіЏсЂфсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓ

paper-entry.title: Hunyuan-Large: An Open-Source MoE Model with 52 Billion Activated Parameters by Tencent, Xingwu Sun+, arXiv'24
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, SyntheticData, OpenWeight, OpenSource
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.02265 # Authors - Xingwu Sun - Yanfeng Chen - Yiqing Huang - Ruobing Xie - Jiaqi Zhu - Kai Zhang - Shuaipeng Li - Zhen Yang - Jonny Han - Xiaobo Shu - Jiahao Bu - Zhongzhi Chen - Xuemeng Huang - Fengzong Lian - Saiyong Yang - Jianfeng Yan - Yuyuan Zeng - Xiaoqin Ren - Chao Yu - Lulu Wu - Yue Mao - Jun Xia - Tao Yang - Suncong Zheng - Kan Wu - Dian Jiao - Jinbao Xue - Xipeng Zhang - Decheng Wu - Kai Liu - Dengpeng Wu - Guanghui Xu - Shaohua Chen - Shuang Chen - Xiao Feng - Yigeng Hong - Junqiang Zheng - Chengcheng Xu - Zongwei Li - Xiong Kuang - Jianglu Hu - Yiqi Chen - Yuchi Deng - Guiyang Li - Ao Liu - Chenchen Zhang - Shihui Hu - Zilong Zhao - Zifan Wu - Yao Ding - Weichao Wang - Han Liu - Roberts Wang - Hao Fei - Peijie She - Ze Zhao - Xun Cao - Hai Wang - Fusheng Xiang - Mengyuan Huang - Zhiyuan Xiong - Bin Hu - Xuebin Hou - Lei Jiang - Jiajia Wu - Yaping Deng - Yi Shen - Qian Wang - Weijie Liu - Jie Liu - Meng Chen - Liang Dong - Weiwen Jia - Hu Chen - Feifei Liu - Rui Yuan - Huilin Xu - Zhenxiang Yan - Tengfei Cao - Zhichao Hu - Xinhua Feng - Dong Du - Tinghao She - Yangyu Tao - Feng Zhang - Jianchen Zhu - Chengzhong Xu - Xirui Li - Chong Zha - Wen Ouyang - Yinben Xia - Xiang Li - Zekun He - Rongpeng Chen - Jiawei Song - Ruibin Chen - Fan Jiang - Chongqing Zhao - Bo Wang - Hao Gong - Rong Gan - Winston Hu - Zhanhui Kang - Yong Yang - Yuhong Liu - Di Wang - Jie Jiang # Abstract - In this paper, we introduce Hunyuan-Large, which is currently the largest open-source Transformer-based mixture of experts model, with a total of 389 billion parameters and 52 billion activation parameters, capable of handling up to 256K tokens. We conduct a thorough evaluation of Hunyuan-Large's superior performance across various benchmarks including language understanding and generation, logical reasoning, mathematical problem-solving, coding, long-context, and aggregated tasks, where it outperforms LLama3.1-70B and exhibits comparable performance when compared to the significantly larger LLama3.1-405B model. Key practice of Hunyuan-Large include large-scale synthetic data that is orders larger than in previous literature, a mixed expert routing strategy, a key-value cache compression technique, and an expert-specific learning rate strategy. Additionally, we also investigate the scaling laws and learning rate schedule of mixture of experts models, providing valuable insights and guidances for future model development and optimization. The code and checkpoints of Hunyuan-Large are released to facilitate future innovations and applications. Codes: https://github.com/Tencent/Hunyuan-Large Models: https://huggingface.co/tencent/Tencent-Hunyuan-Large # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - ТюгУФќТќЄсЂДсЂ»сђЂуЈЙтюеТюђтцДсЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«TransformerсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«т░ѓжќђт«ХТиитљѕсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓІHunyuan-Largeсѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂтљѕУеѕ3890тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂе520тёёсЂ«Т┤╗ТђДтїќсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњТїЂсЂАсђЂТюђтцД256KсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњтЄдуљєсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїсЂДсЂЇсЂЙсЂЎсђѓHunyuan-LargeсЂ«тёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњсђЂУеђУфъуљєУДБсѓёућЪТѕљсђЂУФќуљєТјеУФќсђЂТЋ░тГдуџётЋЈжАїУДБТ▒║сђЂсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сђЂжЋиТќЄсѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсђЂжЏєу┤ёсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂфсЂЕсЂ«сЂЋсЂЙсЂќсЂЙсЂфсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДтЙ╣т║ЋуџёсЂФУЕЋСЙАсЂЌсЂЪухљТъюсђЂLLama3.1-70BсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂсЂЋсѓЅсЂФтцДУдЈТеАсЂфLLama3.1-405BсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂеТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЌсЂдсѓѓтљїуГЅсЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓHunyuan-LargeсЂ«СИ╗сЂфуЅ╣тЙ┤сЂФсЂ»сђЂтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«ТќЄуї«сѓѕсѓісѓѓТАЂжЂЋсЂёсЂФтцДУдЈТеАсЂфтљѕТѕљсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сђЂТиитљѕт░ѓжќђт«ХсЃФсЃ╝сЃєсѓБсЃ│сѓ░ТѕдуЋЦсђЂсѓГсЃ╝сЃ╗сЃљсЃфсЃЦсЃ╝сѓГсЃБсЃЃсѓисЃЦтюДуИ«ТіђУАЊсђЂт░ѓжќђт«ХуЅ╣ТюЅсЂ«тГду┐њујЄТѕдуЋЦсЂїтљФсЂЙсѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂт░ѓжќђт«ХТиитљѕсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃфсЃ│сѓ░Т│ЋтЅЄсЂетГду┐њујЄсѓ╣сѓ▒сѓИсЃЦсЃ╝сЃФсЂФсЂцсЂёсЂдсѓѓУф┐ТЪ╗сЂЌсђЂС╗ітЙїсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФжќІуЎ║сЂеТюђжЂЕтїќсЂФтљЉсЂЉсЂЪУ▓┤жЄЇсЂфТ┤ът»ЪсЂеТїЄжЄЮсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓHunyuan-LargeсЂ«сѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂесЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ»сђЂС╗ітЙїсЂ«жЮЕТќ░сЂет┐юућесѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФтЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ сѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅ: https://github.com/Tencent/Hunyuan-Large сЃбсЃЄсЃФ: https://huggingface.co/tencent/Tencent-Hunyuan-Large # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - Hunyuan-LargeсЂ»сђЂ3890тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњТїЂсЂцсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«TransformerсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«т░ѓжќђт«ХТиитљѕсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂТюђтцД256KсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњтЄдуљєтЈ»УЃйсђѓУеђУфъуљєУДБсѓёућЪТѕљсђЂУФќуљєТјеУФќсЂфсЂЕсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДLLama3.1-70BсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂLLama3.1-405BсЂетљїуГЅсЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЎсђѓСИ╗сЂфуЅ╣тЙ┤сЂФсЂ»тцДУдЈТеАсЂфтљѕТѕљсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сђЂТиитљѕт░ѓжќђт«ХсЃФсЃ╝сЃєсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сђЂсѓГсЃ╝сЃ╗сЃљсЃфсЃЦсЃ╝сѓГсЃБсЃЃсѓисЃЦтюДуИ«сђЂт░ѓжќђт«ХуЅ╣ТюЅсЂ«тГду┐њујЄТѕдуЋЦсЂїтљФсЂЙсѓїсђЂС╗ітЙїсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФжќІуЎ║сЂФтљЉсЂЉсЂЪТ┤ът»ЪсѓѓТЈљСЙЏсђѓсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂесЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»тЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Phi-3 Technical Report: A Highly Capable Language Model Locally on Your Phone, Marah Abdin+, arXiv'24, 2024.04
paper-entry.tags: EfficiencyImprovement, NLP, LanguageModel, action_wanted, SmallModel, OpenWeight, One-Line Notes
paper-entry.issue-body:
# URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.14219 # Authors - Marah Abdin - Jyoti Aneja - Hany Awadalla - Ahmed Awadallah - Ammar Ahmad Awan - Nguyen Bach - Amit Bahree - Arash Bakhtiari - Jianmin Bao - Harkirat Behl - Alon Benhaim - Misha Bilenko - Johan Bjorck - S├Еbastien Bubeck - Martin Cai - Qin Cai - Vishrav Chaudhary - Dong Chen - Dongdong Chen - Weizhu Chen - Yen-Chun Chen - Yi-Ling Chen - Hao Cheng - Parul Chopra - Xiyang Dai - Matthew Dixon - Ronen Eldan - Victor Fragoso - Jianfeng Gao - Mei Gao - Min Gao - Amit Garg - Allie Del Giorno - Abhishek Goswami - Suriya Gunasekar - Emman Haider - Junheng Hao - Russell J. Hewett - Wenxiang Hu - Jamie Huynh - Dan Iter - Sam Ade Jacobs - Mojan Javaheripi - Xin Jin - Nikos Karampatziakis - Piero Kauffmann - Mahoud Khademi - Dongwoo Kim - Young Jin Kim - Lev Kurilenko - James R. Lee - Yin Tat Lee - Yuanzhi Li - Yunsheng Li - Chen Liang - Lars Liden - Xihui Lin - Zeqi Lin - Ce Liu - Liyuan Liu - Mengchen Liu - Weishung Liu - Xiaodong Liu - Chong Luo - Piyush Madan - Ali Mahmoudzadeh - David Majercak - Matt Mazzola - Caio C├Еsar Teodoro Mendes - Arindam Mitra - Hardik Modi - Anh Nguyen - Brandon Norick - Barun Patra - Daniel Perez-Becker - Thomas Portet - Reid Pryzant - Heyang Qin - Marko Radmilac - Liliang Ren - Gustavo de Rosa - Corby Rosset - Sambudha Roy - Olatunji Ruwase - Olli Saarikivi - Amin Saied - Adil Salim - Michael Santacroce - Shital Shah - Ning Shang - Hiteshi Sharma - Yelong Shen - Swadheen Shukla - Xia Song - Masahiro Tanaka - Andrea Tupini - Praneetha Vaddamanu - Chunyu Wang - Guanhua Wang - Lijuan Wang - Shuohang Wang - Xin Wang - Yu Wang - Rachel Ward - Wen Wen - Philipp Witte - Haiping Wu - Xiaoxia Wu - Michael Wyatt - Bin Xiao - Can Xu - Jiahang Xu - Weijian Xu - Jilong Xue - Sonali Yadav - Fan Yang - Jianwei Yang - Yifan Yang - Ziyi Yang - Donghan Yu - Lu Yuan - Chenruidong Zhang - Cyril Zhang - Jianwen Zhang - Li Lyna Zhang - Yi Zhang - Yue Zhang - Yunan Zhang - Xiren Zhou # Abstract - We introduce phi-3-mini, a 3.8 billion parameter language model trained on 3.3 trillion tokens, whose overall performance, as measured by both academic benchmarks and internal testing, rivals that of models such as Mixtral 8x7B and GPT-3.5 (e.g., phi-3-mini achieves 69% on MMLU and 8.38 on MT-bench), despite being small enough to be deployed on a phone. Our training dataset is a scaled-up version of the one used for phi-2, composed of heavily filtered publicly available web data and synthetic data. The model is also further aligned for robustness, safety, and chat format. We also provide parameter-scaling results with a 7B, 14B models trained for 4.8T tokens, called phi-3-small, phi-3-medium, both significantly more capable than phi-3-mini (e.g., respectively 75%, 78% on MMLU, and 8.7, 8.9 on MT-bench). To enhance multilingual, multimodal, and long-context capabilities, we introduce three models in the phi-3.5 series: phi-3.5-mini, phi-3.5-MoE, and phi-3.5-Vision. The phi-3.5-MoE, a 16 x 3.8B MoE model with 6.6 billion active parameters, achieves superior performance in language reasoning, math, and code tasks compared to other open-source models of similar scale, such as Llama 3.1 and the Mixtral series, and on par with Gemini-1.5-Flash and GPT-4o-mini. Meanwhile, phi-3.5-Vision, a 4.2 billion parameter model derived from phi-3.5-mini, excels in reasoning tasks and is adept at handling both single-image and text prompts, as well as multi-image and text prompts. # Translation (by gpt-5-nano) - phi-3-miniсѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ3.8тЇЂтёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂ3.3тЁєсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњућесЂёсЂдтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсђЂтГдУАЊсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂетєЁжЃесЃєсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«тЈїТќ╣сЂДТИгт«џсЂЋсѓїсЂЪуиЈтљѕуџёсЂфТђДУЃйсЂ»сђЂMixtral 8x7BсѓёGPT-3.5сЂфсЂЕсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФтї╣ТЋхсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎ№╝ѕСЙІ№╝џphi-3-miniсЂ»MMLUсЂД69%сђЂMT-benchсЂД8.38сѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂсѓ╣сЃъсЃ╝сЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ│СИісЂИсЂ«сЃЄсЃЌсЃГсѓцсЂїтЈ»УЃйсЂфсЂ╗сЂЕт░ЈтъІсЂДсЂЎ№╝ЅсђѓУеЊуи┤сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂ»phi-2сЂДућесЂёсѓЅсѓїсЂЪсѓѓсЂ«сЂ«сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃФсѓбсЃЃсЃЌуЅѕсЂДсђЂтЁгУАесЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсѓдсѓДсЃќсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњтцДжЄЈсЂФсЃЋсѓБсЃФсѓ┐сЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂЌсЂЪсѓѓсЂ«сЂетљѕТѕљсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂІсѓЅТДІТѕљсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»жаЉтЂЦТђДсЃ╗т«ЅтЁеТђДсЃ╗сЃЂсЃБсЃЃсЃѕтйбт╝ЈсЂ«уѓ╣сЂДсѓѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФТЋ┤тљѕТђДсѓњсЂесЂБсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ
сЂЙсЂЪсђЂ4.8тЁєсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњУеЊуи┤сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂесЂЌсЂдтГду┐њсЂЋсЂЏсЂЪ7BсЂісѓѕсЂ│14BсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃфсЃ│сѓ░ухљТъюсѓѓТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂphi-3-smallсЂісѓѕсЂ│phi-3-mediumсЂетЉ╝сЂ░сѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂёсЂџсѓїсѓѓphi-3-miniсѓѕсѓітцДт╣ЁсЂФжФўсЂёТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂСЙІсЂѕсЂ░MMLUсЂДсЂЮсѓїсЂъсѓї75%сђЂ78%сђЂMT-benchсЂДсЂЮсѓїсЂъсѓї8.7сђЂ8.9сЂесЂфсЂБсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ
жЋиУеђУфъсЃ╗сЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃ╗жЋиТќЄУёѕУЃйтіЏсѓњжФўсѓЂсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсђЂphi-3.5сѓисЃфсЃ╝сѓ║сЂесЂЌсЂдСИЅсЂцсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњт░јтЁЦсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓphi-3.5-miniсђЂphi-3.5-MoEсђЂphi-3.5-Visionсђѓ
phi-3.5-MoEсЂ»сђЂ16├Ќ3.8B MoEсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДТ┤╗ТђДсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ»6.6BсѓњТїЂсЂАсђЂУеђУфъТјеУФќсђЂТЋ░тГдсђЂсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсђЂтљїУдЈТеАсЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓйсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕLlama 3.1сѓёMixtralсѓисЃфсЃ╝сѓ║сЂфсЂЕ№╝ЅсѓѕсѓітёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсђЂGemini-1.5-FlashсЂісѓѕсЂ│GPT-4o-miniсЂетљїуГЅсЂ«Т░┤Т║ќсЂФсЂѓсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓСИђТќ╣сђЂphi-3.5-VisionсЂ»phi-3.5-miniсѓњсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂФсЂЌсЂЪ4.2тЇЂтёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂТјеУФќсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂФжЋисЂЉсђЂтЇўСИђућ╗тЃЈсЂесЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЂасЂЉсЂДсЂфсЂЈсђЂУцЄТЋ░ућ╗тЃЈсЂесЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЂ«тЄдуљєсЂФсѓѓуєЪуи┤сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - phi-3-miniсЂ»3.8тЇЂтёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂ3.3тЁєсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сѓњтГду┐њсЂЌсђЂMMLUсЂД69%сђЂMT-benchсЂД8.38сѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓсѓ╣сЃъсЃ╝сЃѕсЃЋсѓЕсЃ│сЂДсѓѓсЃЄсЃЌсЃГсѓцтЈ»УЃйсЂДсђЂphi-2сЂ«сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсѓњсЃЋсѓБсЃФсѓ┐сЃфсЃ│сѓ░сЂЌсЂдСйюТѕљсђѓphi-3-smallсЂеphi-3-mediumсЂ»сЂЮсѓїсЂъсѓї75%сђЂ78%сѓњMMLUсЂДжЂћТѕљсЂЌсђЂТђДУЃйсЂїтљЉСИісђѓсѓисЃфсЃ╝сѓ║сЂ«Тќ░сЃбсЃЄсЃФphi-3.5-miniсђЂphi-3.5-MoEсђЂphi-3.5-Visionсѓѓт░јтЁЦсђѓphi-3.5-MoEсЂ»тёфсѓїсЂЪУеђУфътЄдуљєУЃйтіЏсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЌсђЂphi-3.5-VisionсЂ»УцЄТЋ░ућ╗тЃЈсЂесЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсЂФт»Йт┐юсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Gemma: Open Models Based on Gemini Research and Technology, Gemma Team+, arXiv'24, 2024.03
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, action_wanted, OpenWeight, KeyPoint Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - preprint: https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.08295 - blog: https://blog.google/technology/developers/gemma-open-models/ # Authors - Gemma Team - Thomas Mesnard - Cassidy Hardin - Robert Dadashi - Surya Bhupatiraju - Shreya Pathak - Laurent Sifre - Morgane Rivi├еre - Mihir Sanjay Kale - Juliette Love - Pouya Tafti - L├Еonard Hussenot - Pier Giuseppe Sessa - Aakanksha Chowdhery - Adam Roberts - Aditya Barua - Alex Botev - Alex Castro-Ros - Ambrose Slone - Am├Еlie H├Еliou - Andrea Tacchetti - Anna Bulanova - Antonia Paterson - Beth Tsai - Bobak Shahriari - Charline Le Lan - Christopher A. Choquette-Choo - Cl├Еment Crepy - Daniel Cer - Daphne Ippolito - David Reid - Elena Buchatskaya - Eric Ni - Eric Noland - Geng Yan - George Tucker - George-Christian Muraru - Grigory Rozhdestvenskiy - Henryk Michalewski - Ian Tenney - Ivan Grishchenko - Jacob Austin - James Keeling - Jane Labanowski - Jean-Baptiste Lespiau - Jeff Stanway - Jenny Brennan - Jeremy Chen - Johan Ferret - Justin Chiu - Justin Mao-Jones - Katherine Lee - Kathy Yu - Katie Millican - Lars Lowe Sjoesund - Lisa Lee - Lucas Dixon - Machel Reid - Maciej Miku┼ѓa - Mateo Wirth - Michael Sharman - Nikolai Chinaev - Nithum Thain - Olivier Bachem - Oscar Chang - Oscar Wahltinez - Paige Bailey - Paul Michel - Petko Yotov - Rahma Chaabouni - Ramona Comanescu - Reena Jana - Rohan Anil - Ross McIlroy - Ruibo Liu - Ryan Mullins - Samuel L Smith - Sebastian Borgeaud - Sertan Girgin - Sholto Douglas - Shree Pandya - Siamak Shakeri - Soham De - Ted Klimenko - Tom Hennigan - Vlad Feinberg - Wojciech Stokowiec - Yu-hui Chen - Zafarali Ahmed - Zhitao Gong - Tris Warkentin - Ludovic Peran - Minh Giang - Cl├Еment Farabet - Oriol Vinyals - Jeff Dean - Koray Kavukcuoglu - Demis Hassabis - Zoubin Ghahramani - Douglas Eck - Joelle Barral - Fernando Pereira - Eli Collins - Armand Joulin - Noah Fiedel - Evan Senter - Alek Andreev - Kathleen Kenealy # Abstract - This work introduces Gemma, a family of lightweight, state-of-the art open models built from the research and technology used to create Gemini models. Gemma models demonstrate strong performance across academic benchmarks for language understanding, reasoning, and safety. We release two sizes of models (2 billion and 7 billion parameters), and provide both pretrained and fine-tuned checkpoints. Gemma outperforms similarly sized open models on 11 out of 18 text-based tasks, and we present comprehensive evaluations of safety and responsibility aspects of the models, alongside a detailed description of model development. We believe the responsible release of LLMs is critical for improving the safety of frontier models, and for enabling the next wave of LLM innovations. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - сЂЊсЂ«СйюТЦГсЂДсЂ»сђЂGemmaсЂесЂёсЂєУ╗йжЄЈсЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сЃЋсѓАсЃЪсЃфсЃ╝сѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂGeminiсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњСйюТѕљсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФСй┐ућесЂЋсѓїсЂЪуаћуЕХсЂеТіђУАЊсЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂёсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓGemmaсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂУеђУфъуљєУДБсђЂТјеУФќсђЂт«ЅтЁеТђДсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІтГдУАЊуџёсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДт╝итіЏсЂфсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сѓњуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂ2тёёсЂісѓѕсЂ│7тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«2сЂцсЂ«сѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњсЃфсЃфсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂЌсђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТИѕсЂ┐сЂісѓѕсЂ│сЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТИѕсЂ┐сЂ«сЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓGemmaсЂ»сђЂ18сЂ«сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂ«сЂєсЂА11сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂДтљїТДўсЂ«сѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњСИітЏъсЂБсЂдсЂісѓісђЂт«ЅтЁеТђДсЂеУ▓гС╗╗сЂ«тЂ┤жЮбсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІтїЁТІгуџёсЂфУЕЋСЙАсѓњТЈљуц║сЂЌсђЂсЃбсЃЄсЃФжќІуЎ║сЂ«УЕ│у┤░сЂфУфгТўјсѓњУАїсЂБсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓуДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ»сђЂLLMсЂ«У▓гС╗╗сЂѓсѓІсЃфсЃфсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂїТюђтЅЇуиџсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«т«ЅтЁеТђДсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсђЂТгАсЂ«Т│бсЂ«LLMжЮЕТќ░сѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФжЄЇУдЂсЂДсЂѓсѓІсЂеС┐АсЂўсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - GemmaсЂ»сђЂУ╗йжЄЈсЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂУеђУфъуљєУДБсѓёТјеУФќсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдт╝итіЏсЂфТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сђѓ2тёёсЂісѓѕсЂ│7тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТИѕсЂ┐сЂесЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░ТИѕсЂ┐сЂ«сЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсѓњтљФсѓђсђѓGemmaсЂ»сђЂ18сЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂ«сЂєсЂА11сЂДтљїсѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњУХЁсЂѕсђЂт«ЅтЁеТђДсЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІУЕ│у┤░сЂфУЕЋСЙАсЂесЃбсЃЄсЃФжќІуЎ║сЂ«УфгТўјсѓњТЈљСЙЏсђѓУ▓гС╗╗сЂѓсѓІLLMсЂ«сЃфсЃфсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂїт«ЅтЁеТђДтљЉСИісЂФт»ёСИјсЂЌсђЂТгАСИќС╗БсЂ«жЮЕТќ░сѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсЂеС┐АсЂўсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] OLMo: Accelerating the Science of Language Models, Dirk Groeneveld+, arXiv'24, 2024.02
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, action_wanted, OpenWeight, OpenSource, Selected Papers/Blogs, One-Line Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.00838 # Authors - Dirk Groeneveld - Iz Beltagy - Pete Walsh - Akshita Bhagia - Rodney Kinney - Oyvind Tafjord - Ananya Harsh Jha - Hamish Ivison - Ian Magnusson - Yizhong Wang - Shane Arora - David Atkinson - Russell Authur - Khyathi Raghavi Chandu - Arman Cohan - Jennifer Dumas - Yanai Elazar - Yuling Gu - Jack Hessel - Tushar Khot - William Merrill - Jacob Morrison - Niklas Muennighoff - Aakanksha Naik - Crystal Nam - Matthew E. Peters - Valentina Pyatkin - Abhilasha Ravichander - Dustin Schwenk - Saurabh Shah - Will Smith - Emma Strubell - Nishant Subramani - Mitchell Wortsman - Pradeep Dasigi - Nathan Lambert - Kyle Richardson - Luke Zettlemoyer - Jesse Dodge - Kyle Lo - Luca Soldaini - Noah A. Smith - Hannaneh Hajishirzi # Abstract - Language models (LMs) have become ubiquitous in both NLP research and in commercial product offerings. As their commercial importance has surged, the most powerful models have become closed off, gated behind proprietary interfaces, with important details of their training data, architectures, and development undisclosed. Given the importance of these details in scientifically studying these models, including their biases and potential risks, we believe it is essential for the research community to have access to powerful, truly open LMs. To this end, we have built OLMo, a competitive, truly Open Language Model, to enable the scientific study of language models. Unlike most prior efforts that have only released model weights and inference code, we release OLMo alongside open training data and training and evaluation code. We hope this release will empower the open research community and inspire a new wave of innovation. # Translation (by gpt-5-nano) - УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕLM№╝ЅсЂ»сђЂNLPуаћуЕХсЂетЋєућеУБйтЊЂТЈљСЙЏсЂ«тЈїТќ╣сЂДт║ЃсЂЈТЎ«тЈісЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтЋєТЦГуџёжЄЇУдЂТђДсЂїжФўсЂЙсѓІсЂФсЂцсѓїсЂдсђЂТюђсѓѓт╝итіЏсЂфсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»уІгУЄфсЂ«сѓцсЃ│сѓ┐сЃ╝сЃЋсѓДсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«УЃїтЙїсЂФжќЅсЂќсЂЋсѓїсђЂсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сђЂсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсђЂжќІуЎ║сЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІжЄЇУдЂсЂфУЕ│у┤░сЂ»тЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЏсѓЊсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«УЕ│у┤░сЂїсЂЊсѓїсѓЅсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњуДЉтГдуџёсЂФуаћуЕХсЂЎсѓІсЂєсЂѕсЂДсђЂсЃљсѓцсѓбсѓ╣сѓёТйютюеуџёсЂфсЃфсѓ╣сѓ»сѓњтљФсѓЂжЄЇУдЂсЂДсЂѓсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњУИЈсЂЙсЂѕсѓІсЂесђЂуаћуЕХсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсЂїт╝итіЏсЂДуюЪсЂФсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфLMсЂФсѓбсѓ»сѓ╗сѓ╣сЂДсЂЇсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїСИЇтЈ»ТгасЂасЂеС┐АсЂўсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂуФХС║ЅтіЏсЂїсЂѓсѓіуюЪсЂФсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓІOLMoсѓњТДІу»ЅсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«уДЉтГдуџёуаћуЕХсѓњтЈ»УЃйсЂФсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂДсЂЎсђѓтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«тцџсЂЈсЂ«тЈќсѓіухёсЂ┐сЂїсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«жЄЇсЂ┐сЂеТјеУФќсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂасЂЉсѓњтЁгжќІсЂЌсЂдсЂЇсЂЪсЂ«сЂФт»ЙсЂЌсЂдсђЂOLMoсЂ»тЁгжќІсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂесЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂісѓѕсЂ│УЕЋСЙАсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂесЂесѓѓсЂФтЁгжќІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сЃфсЃфсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂїсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфуаћуЕХсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсѓњтіЏсЂЦсЂЉсђЂТќ░сЂЪсЂфсѓцсЃјсЃЎсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сЂ«Т│бсѓњтќџУхисЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњТюЪтЙЁсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - OLMoсЂ»сђЂтИѓта┤сЂДсЂ«тЋєТЦГуџёжЄЇУдЂТђДсЂїжФўсЂЙсѓІСИГсђЂуюЪсЂФсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДNLPуаћуЕХсЂ«жђ▓т▒ЋсѓњуЏ«ТїЄсЂЎсђѓтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂїжЄЇсЂ┐сѓёТјеУФќсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂ«сЂ┐сѓњтЁгжќІсЂЎсѓІсЂ«сЂФт»ЙсЂЌсђЂOLMoсЂ»сЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓёУЕЋСЙАсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсѓѓтљФсѓЂсЂдтЁгжќІсЂЌсђЂуДЉтГдуџёуаћуЕХсЂ«тЪ║уЏцсѓњуб║уФІсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сЂфуаћуЕХсѓ│сЃЪсЃЦсЃІсЃєсѓБсЂ«тіЏсѓњт╝ЋсЂЇтЄ║сЂЌсђЂТќ░сЂЪсЂфсѓцсЃјсЃЎсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњТюЪтЙЁсЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Mixtral of Experts, Albert Q. Jiang+, arXiv'24, 2024.01
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, action_wanted, OpenWeight, MoE(Mixture-of-Experts), One-Line Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04088 # Authors - Albert Q. Jiang - Alexandre Sablayrolles - Antoine Roux - Arthur Mensch - Blanche Savary - Chris Bamford - Devendra Singh Chaplot - Diego de las Casas - Emma Bou Hanna - Florian Bressand - Gianna Lengyel - Guillaume Bour - Guillaume Lample - L├Еlio Renard Lavaud - Lucile Saulnier - Marie-Anne Lachaux - Pierre Stock - Sandeep Subramanian - Sophia Yang - Szymon Antoniak - Teven Le Scao - Th├Еophile Gervet - Thibaut Lavril - Thomas Wang - Timoth├Еe Lacroix - William El Sayed # Abstract - We introduce Mixtral 8x7B, a Sparse Mixture of Experts (SMoE) language model. Mixtral has the same architecture as Mistral 7B, with the difference that each layer is composed of 8 feedforward blocks (i.e. experts). For every token, at each layer, a router network selects two experts to process the current state and combine their outputs. Even though each token only sees two experts, the selected experts can be different at each timestep. As a result, each token has access to 47B parameters, but only uses 13B active parameters during inference. Mixtral was trained with a context size of 32k tokens and it outperforms or matches Llama 2 70B and GPT-3.5 across all evaluated benchmarks. In particular, Mixtral vastly outperforms Llama 2 70B on mathematics, code generation, and multilingual benchmarks. We also provide a model fine-tuned to follow instructions, Mixtral 8x7B - Instruct, that surpasses GPT-3.5 Turbo, Claude-2.1, Gemini Pro, and Llama 2 70B - chat model on human benchmarks. Both the base and instruct models are released under the Apache 2.0 license. # Translation (by gpt-5-nano) - уДЂсЂЪсЂАсЂ» Mixtral 8x7BсђЂсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃ╗сЃЪсѓ»сѓ╣сЃЂсЃБсЃ╝сЃ╗сѓфсЃќсЃ╗сѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃё№╝ѕSMoE№╝ЅУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓMixtral сЂ» Mistral 7B сЂетљїсЂўсѓбсЃ╝сѓГсЃєсѓ»сЃЂсЃБсѓњТјАућесЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсЂїсђЂтљёт▒цсЂї 8 тђІсЂ«сЃЋсѓБсЃ╝сЃЅсЃЋсѓЕсЃ»сЃ╝сЃЅсЃќсЃГсЃЃсѓ»№╝ѕсЂЎсЂфсѓЈсЂАсѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃѕ№╝ЅсЂДТДІТѕљсЂЋсѓїсѓІуѓ╣сЂїуЋ░сЂфсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓтљёсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂФсЂцсЂёсЂдсђЂтљёт▒цсЂДсЃФсЃ╝сЃєсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сЃЇсЃЃсЃѕсЃ»сЃ╝сѓ»сЂїуЈЙтюесЂ«уіХТЁІсѓњтЄдуљєсЂЎсѓІ 2 сЂцсЂ«сѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃѕсѓњжЂИТіъсЂЌсђЂсЂЮсѓїсѓЅсЂ«тЄ║тіЏсѓњухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтљёсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂ» 2 сЂцсЂ«сѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃѕсЂЌсЂІУдІсЂЙсЂЏсѓЊсЂїсђЂжЂИТіъсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃѕсЂ»тљёсѓ┐сѓцсЃасѓ╣сЃєсЃЃсЃЌсЂДуЋ░сЂфсѓІта┤тљѕсЂїсЂѓсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЮсЂ«ухљТъюсђЂтљёсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂ» 470тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂФсѓбсѓ»сѓ╗сѓ╣сЂДсЂЇсЂЙсЂЎсЂїсђЂТјеУФќТЎѓсЂФТ┤╗ТђДтїќсЂЋсѓїсѓІсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ»сѓЈсЂџсЂІ130тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂФсЂесЂЕсЂЙсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓMixtral сЂ» 32k сЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂ«ТќЄУёѕжЋисЂДУеЊуи┤сЂЋсѓїсђЂУЕЋСЙАсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЂЎсЂ╣сЂдсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂд Llama 2 70B сЂісѓѕсЂ│ GPT-3.5 сѓњСИітЏъсѓІсЂІсђЂтљїуГЅсЂесЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂесѓісѓЈсЂЉсђЂТЋ░тГдсђЂсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅућЪТѕљсђЂсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃфсЃ│сѓгсЃФсЂфсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂФсЂісЂёсЂд Mixtral сЂ» Llama 2 70B сѓњтцДт╣ЁсЂФСИітЏъсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓТїЄуц║сЂФтЙЊсЂєсѓѕсЂєтЙ«Уф┐ТЋ┤сЂЌсЂЪсЃбсЃЄсЃФ Mixtral 8x7B - Instruct сѓѓТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂС║║жќЊсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂД GPT-3.5 TurboсђЂClaude-2.1сђЂGemini ProсђЂLlama 2 70B - сЃЂсЃБсЃЃсЃѕсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњСИітЏъсѓісЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂеТїЄуц║С╗ўсЂЇсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«СИАТќ╣сЂ» Apache 2.0 сЃЕсѓцсѓ╗сЃ│сѓ╣сЂ«СИІсЂДтЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - Mixtral 8x7BсЂ»сђЂ8сЂцсЂ«сѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃѕсѓњТїЂсЂцсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃ╗сЃЪсѓ»сѓ╣сЃЂсЃБсЃ╝сЃ╗сѓфсЃќсЃ╗сѓесѓГсѓ╣сЃЉсЃ╝сЃёсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсђЂ470тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂФсѓбсѓ»сѓ╗сѓ╣сЂЌсЂцсЂцсђЂТјеУФќТЎѓсЂ»130тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«сЂ┐сЂїТ┤╗ТђДтїќсЂЋсѓїсѓІсђѓ32kсЃѕсЃ╝сѓ»сЃ│сЂ«ТќЄУёѕжЋисЂДУеЊуи┤сЂЋсѓїсђЂLlama 2 70BсЂісѓѕсЂ│GPT-3.5сѓњСИітЏъсѓІТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сђѓуЅ╣сЂФТЋ░тГдсѓёсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅућЪТѕљсЂДтёфсѓїсђЂТїЄуц║сЂФтЙЊсЂєсѓѕсЂєтЙ«Уф┐ТЋ┤сЂЌсЂЪсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓѓТЈљСЙЏсЂЋсѓїсђЂУцЄТЋ░сЂ«С║║жќЊсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДуФХтљѕсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњУХЁсЂѕсЂЪсђѓ

paper-entry.title: Improved Baselines with Visual Instruction Tuning, Haotian Liu+, N_A, CVPR'24
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, LanguageModel, QuestionAnswering, OpenWeight, CVPR, Selected Papers/Blogs, VisionLanguageModel, One-Line Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.03744 # Affiliations - Haotian Liu, N/A - Chunyuan Li, N/A - Yuheng Li, N/A - Yong Jae Lee, N/A # Abstract - Large multimodal models (LMM) have recently shown encouraging progress withvisual instruction tuning. In this note, we show that the fully-connectedvision-language cross-modal connector in LLaVA is surprisingly powerful anddata-efficient. With simple modifications to LLaVA, namely, usingCLIP-ViT-L-336px with an MLP projection and adding academic-task-oriented VQAdata with simple response formatting prompts, we establish stronger baselinesthat achieve state-of-the-art across 11 benchmarks. Our final 13B checkpointuses merely 1.2M publicly available data, and finishes full training in ~1 dayon a single 8-A100 node. We hope this can make state-of-the-art LMM researchmore accessible. Code and model will be publicly available. # Translation (by gpt-3.5-turbo) - тцДУдЈТеАсЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕLMM№╝ЅсЂ»сђЂТюђУ┐ЉсђЂУдќУдџуџёсЂфТїЄуц║сЂ«Уф┐ТЋ┤сЂФсЂісЂёсЂдті▒сЂ┐сѓњуц║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«сЃјсЃ╝сЃѕсЂДсЂ»сђЂLLaVAсЂ«т«їтЁесЂФТјЦуХџсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЊсѓИсЃДсЃ│-УеђУфъсЂ«сѓ»сЃГсѓ╣сЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсѓ│сЃЇсѓ»сѓ┐сЂїжЕџсЂЈсЂ╗сЂЕт╝итіЏсЂДсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐ті╣ујЄсЂїУЅ»сЂёсЂЊсЂесѓњуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓLLaVAсЂФу░АтЇўсЂфС┐«ТГБсѓњтіасЂѕсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂтЁиСйЊуџёсЂФсЂ»MLPсЃЌсЃГсѓИсѓДсѓ»сѓисЃДсЃ│сѓњСй┐ућесЂЌсЂЪCLIP-ViT-L-336pxсѓњСй┐ућесЂЌсђЂсѓисЃ│сЃЌсЃФсЂфт┐юуГћсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«сЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕсѓњТїЂсЂцтГдУАЊсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»ТїЄтљЉсЂ«VQAсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњУ┐йтіасЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂ11сЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃ│сѓњуб║уФІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТюђухѓуџёсЂф13BсЂ«сЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ»сђЂсѓЈсЂџсЂІсЂФ120СИЄсЂ«тЁгжќІсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњСй┐ућесЂЌсђЂтЇўСИђсЂ«8-A100сЃјсЃ╝сЃЅсЂДу┤ё1ТЌЦсЂДт«їтЁесЂфсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњухѓсЂѕсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂФсѓѕсѓісђЂТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«LMMсЂ«уаћуЕХсЂїсѓѕсѓісѓбсѓ»сѓ╗сѓ╣сЂЌсѓёсЂЎсЂЈсЂфсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњТюЪтЙЁсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂесЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»тЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-3.5-turbo) - LLaVAсЂ»сђЂсЃЊсѓИсЃДсЃ│сЂеУеђУфъсЂ«сѓ»сЃГсѓ╣сЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсѓ│сЃЇсѓ»сѓ┐сЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐ті╣ујЄсЂїжФўсЂЈт╝итіЏсЂфТђДУЃйсѓњТїЂсЂцсЂЊсЂесЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓCLIP-ViT-L-336pxсѓњСй┐ућесЂЌсђЂтГдУАЊсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»ТїЄтљЉсЂ«VQAсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњУ┐йтіасЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂДсђЂ11сЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«сЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃ│сѓњуб║уФІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ13BсЂ«сЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсЂ»сѓЈсЂџсЂІ120СИЄсЂ«тЁгжќІсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓњСй┐ућесЂЌсђЂ1ТЌЦсЂДт«їтЁесЂфсЃѕсЃгсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњухѓсЂѕсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅсЂесЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»тЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ

# Overview ућ╗тЃЈућЪТѕљсѓњсЂДсЂЇсѓІсѓЈсЂЉсЂДсЂ»сЂфсЂЈсђЂinputсЂесЂЌсЂдућ╗тЃЈсѓњТЅ▒сЂѕсѓІсЂ«сЂ┐сђѓ

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models, Hyung Won Chung+, JMLR'24, 2022.10
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, Supervised-FineTuning (SFT), InstructionTuning, action_wanted, OpenWeight, Selected Papers/Blogs, One-Line Notes, Scalability, JMLR
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - preprint: https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.11416 - JMLR: https://jmlr.org/papers/volume25/23-0870/23-0870.pdf # Authors - Hyung Won Chung - Le Hou - Shayne Longpre - Barret Zoph - Yi Tay - William Fedus - Yunxuan Li - Xuezhi Wang - Mostafa Dehghani - Siddhartha Brahma - Albert Webson - Shixiang Shane Gu - Zhuyun Dai - Mirac Suzgun - Xinyun Chen - Aakanksha Chowdhery - Alex Castro-Ros - Marie Pellat - Kevin Robinson - Dasha Valter - Sharan Narang - Gaurav Mishra - Adams Yu - Vincent Zhao - Yanping Huang - Andrew Dai - Hongkun Yu - Slav Petrov - Ed H. Chi - Jeff Dean - Jacob Devlin - Adam Roberts - Denny Zhou - Quoc V. Le - Jason Wei # Abstract - Finetuning language models on a collection of datasets phrased as instructions has been shown to improve model performance and generalization to unseen tasks. In this paper we explore instruction finetuning with a particular focus on (1) scaling the number of tasks, (2) scaling the model size, and (3) finetuning on chain-of-thought data. We find that instruction finetuning with the above aspects dramatically improves performance on a variety of model classes (PaLM, T5, U-PaLM), prompting setups (zero-shot, few-shot, CoT), and evaluation benchmarks (MMLU, BBH, TyDiQA, MGSM, open-ended generation). For instance, Flan-PaLM 540B instruction-finetuned on 1.8K tasks outperforms PALM 540B by a large margin (+9.4% on average). Flan-PaLM 540B achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmarks, such as 75.2% on five-shot MMLU. We also publicly release Flan-T5 checkpoints, which achieve strong few-shot performance even compared to much larger models, such as PaLM 62B. Overall, instruction finetuning is a general method for improving the performance and usability of pretrained language models. # Translation (by gpt-5-nano) - ТїЄуц║сЂесЂЌсЂдУАеуЈЙсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сѓ╗сЃЃсЃѕсЂ«жЏєтљѕСИісЂДУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂ»сђЂсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«ТђДУЃйсЂеТюфуЪЦсЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂИсЂ«СИђУѕгтїќсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЊсЂесЂїуц║сЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓТюгУФќТќЄсЂДсЂ»сђЂТїЄуц║сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњсђЂуЅ╣сЂФ(1) сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»ТЋ░сЂ«сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃфсЃ│сѓ░сђЂ(2) сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃфсЃ│сѓ░сђЂ(3) сЃЂсѓДсЃ╝сЃ│сЃ╗сѓфсЃќсЃ╗ТђЮУђЃсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂДсЂ«сЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂесЂёсЂєуѓ╣сЂФуёдуѓ╣сѓњтйЊсЂдсЂдТцюУејсЂЎсѓІсђѓСИіУ┐░сЂ«тЂ┤жЮбсѓњухёсЂ┐УЙ╝сѓЊсЂаТїЄуц║сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂ»сђЂPaLMсђЂT5сђЂUРђЉPaLMсЂесЂёсЂБсЂЪсЂЋсЂЙсЂќсЂЙсЂфсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓ»сЃЕсѓ╣сђЂсЃЌсЃГсЃ│сЃЌсЃѕУеГт«џ№╝ѕсѓ╝сЃГсѓисЃДсЃЃсЃѕсђЂFew-shotсђЂCoT№╝ЅсђЂсЂісѓѕсЂ│УЕЋСЙАсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»№╝ѕMMLUсђЂBBHсђЂTyDiQAсђЂMGSMсђЂсѓфсЃ╝сЃЌсЃ│сѓесЃ│сЃЅућЪТѕљ№╝ЅсЂДТђДУЃйсѓњтіЄуџёсЂФтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуц║сЂЎсђѓСЙІсЂѕсЂ░сђЂFlanРђЉPaLM 540BсЂ»1,800С╗ХсЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂДТїЄуц║сЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњТќйсЂЎсЂесђЂPaLM 540BсѓњтцДт╣ЁсЂФСИітЏъсѓІ№╝ѕт╣│тЮЄсЂД+9.4%№╝ЅсђѓFlanРђЉPaLM 540BсЂ»сђЂ5сѓисЃДсЃЃсЃѕсЂ«MMLUсЂфсЂЕсЂёсЂЈсЂцсЂІсЂ«сЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂ75.2%сѓњУеўжї▓сЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓсЂЋсѓЅсЂФсђЂFlanРђЉT5сЂ«сЃЂсѓДсЃЃсѓ»сЃЮсѓцсЃ│сЃѕсѓњтЁгжќІсЃфсЃфсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂЌсЂдсЂісѓісђЂPaLM 62BсЂесЂёсЂБсЂЪсЂ»сѓІсЂІсЂФтцДУдЈТеАсЂфсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂеТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЌсЂдсѓѓт╝итіЏсЂфFew-shotТђДУЃйсѓњуЎ║ТЈ«сЂЎсѓІсђѓТѕЉсђЁсЂ»уиЈсЂўсЂдсђЂТїЄуц║сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂ»сђЂС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њТИѕсЂ┐УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«ТђДУЃйсЂеСй┐сЂёсѓёсЂЎсЂЋсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсѓІСИђУѕгуџёсЂфТЅІТ│ЋсЂДсЂѓсѓІсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - ТїЄуц║сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂ»сђЂУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«ТђДУЃйсЂеСИђУѕгтїќсѓњтљЉСИісЂЋсЂЏсѓІсђѓуЅ╣сЂФсђЂсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»ТЋ░сѓёсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓхсѓцсѓ║сЂ«сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃфсЃ│сѓ░сђЂсЃЂсѓДсЃ╝сЃ│сЃ╗сѓфсЃќсЃ╗ТђЮУђЃсЃЄсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂДсЂ«жЂЕућесЂїті╣ТъюуџёсђѓFlanРђЉPaLM 540BсЂ»1,800С╗ХсЂ«сѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂДсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сѓњУАїсЂёсђЂPaLM 540Bсѓњт╣│тЮЄ+9.4%СИітЏъсѓісђЂТюђтЁѕуФ»сЂ«ухљТъюсѓњтЄ║сЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓFlanРђЉT5сѓѓт╝итіЏсЂфFew-shotТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсђЂТїЄуц║сЂФтЪ║сЂЦсЂЈсЃЋсѓАсѓцсЃ│сЃЂсЃЦсЃ╝сЃІсЃ│сѓ░сЂїсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«ТђДУЃйтљЉСИісЂФт»ёСИјсЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњуб║УфЇсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ

paper-entry.title: PaLI-3 Vision Language Models: Smaller, Faster, Stronger, Xi Chen+, arXiv'23
paper-entry.tags: ComputerVision, NLP, LanguageModel, MultiModal, OpenWeight
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.09199 # Authors - Xi Chen - Xiao Wang - Lucas Beyer - Alexander Kolesnikov - Jialin Wu - Paul Voigtlaender - Basil Mustafa - Sebastian Goodman - Ibrahim Alabdulmohsin - Piotr Padlewski - Daniel Salz - Xi Xiong - Daniel Vlasic - Filip Pavetic - Keran Rong - Tianli Yu - Daniel Keysers - Xiaohua Zhai - Radu Soricut # Abstract - This paper presents PaLI-3, a smaller, faster, and stronger vision language model (VLM) that compares favorably to similar models that are 10x larger. As part of arriving at this strong performance, we compare Vision Transformer (ViT) models pretrained using classification objectives to contrastively (SigLIP) pretrained ones. We find that, while slightly underperforming on standard image classification benchmarks, SigLIP-based PaLI shows superior performance across various multimodal benchmarks, especially on localization and visually-situated text understanding. We scale the SigLIP image encoder up to 2 billion parameters, and achieves a new state-of-the-art on multilingual cross-modal retrieval. We hope that PaLI-3, at only 5B parameters, rekindles research on fundamental pieces of complex VLMs, and could fuel a new generation of scaled-up models. # Translation (by gpt-4o-mini) - ТюгУФќТќЄсЂДсЂ»сђЂPaLI-3сѓњу┤╣С╗ІсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсѓїсЂ»сђЂтљїТДўсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂеТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЌсЂд10тђЇт░ЈтъІсЂДжФўжђЪсЂІсЂцт╝итіЏсЂфУдќУдџУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕVLM№╝ЅсЂДсЂЎсђѓсЂЊсЂ«т╝итіЏсЂфТђДУЃйсѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂтѕєжАъуЏ«уџёсЂДС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсЂЪVision Transformer№╝ѕViT№╝ЅсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂесђЂт»ЙуЁДуџёсЂФ№╝ѕSigLIP№╝ЅС║ІтЅЇтГду┐њсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњТ»ћУ╝ЃсЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓТеЎТ║ќуџёсЂфућ╗тЃЈтѕєжАъсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДсЂ»УІЦт╣▓сЂ«ТђДУЃйСИЇУХ│сЂїУдІсѓЅсѓїсѓІсѓѓсЂ«сЂ«сђЂSigLIPсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«PaLIсЂ»сђЂуЅ╣сЂФсЃГсЃ╝сѓФсЃфсѓ╝сЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сѓёУдќУдџуџёсЂФжќбжђБсЂЌсЂЪсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕуљєУДБсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдсђЂсЂЋсЂЙсЂќсЂЙсЂфсЃъсЃФсЃЂсЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЌсЂЙсЂЎсђѓSigLIPућ╗тЃЈсѓесЃ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃђсЃ╝сѓњ20тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂФсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃФсѓбсЃЃсЃЌсЂЌсђЂтцџУеђУфъсѓ»сЃГсѓ╣сЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФТцюу┤бсЂДТќ░сЂЪсЂфТюђтЁѕуФ»сѓњжЂћТѕљсЂЌсЂЙсЂЌсЂЪсђѓPaLI-3сЂ»сѓЈсЂџсЂІ50тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂДсђЂУцЄжЏЉсЂфVLMсЂ«тЪ║ТюгуџёсЂфУдЂу┤асЂФжќбсЂЎсѓІуаћуЕХсѓњтєЇуЄЃсЂЋсЂЏсђЂТќ░сЂЪсЂфсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃФсѓбсЃЃсЃЌсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«СИќС╗БсѓњС┐Ѓжђ▓сЂЎсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњТюЪтЙЁсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-4o-mini) - PaLI-3сЂ»сђЂтЙЊТЮЦсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂФТ»ћсЂ╣сЂд10тђЇт░ЈтъІсЂДжФўжђЪсЂфУдќУдџУеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФ№╝ѕVLM№╝ЅсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂуЅ╣сЂФсЃГсЃ╝сѓФсЃфсѓ╝сЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сѓёУдќУдџуџёсЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕуљєУДБсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдтёфсѓїсЂЪТђДУЃйсѓњуц║сЂЎсђѓSigLIPсЃЎсЃ╝сѓ╣сЂ«PaLIсЂ»сђЂ20тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂФсѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃФсѓбсЃЃсЃЌсЂЋсѓїсђЂтцџУеђУфъсѓ»сЃГсѓ╣сЃбсЃ╝сЃђсЃФТцюу┤бсЂДТќ░сЂЪсЂфТюђтЁѕуФ»сѓњжЂћТѕљсђѓ50тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«PaLI-3сЂ»сђЂVLMсЂ«уаћуЕХсѓњтєЇуЄЃсЂЋсЂЏсѓІсЂЊсЂесѓњТюЪтЙЁсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ

paper-entry.title: Mistral 7B, Albert Q. Jiang+, N_A, arXiv'23
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, OpenWeight, KeyPoint Notes
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.06825 # Affiliations - Albert Q. Jiang, N/A - Alexandre Sablayrolles, N/A - Arthur Mensch, N/A - Chris Bamford, N/A - Devendra Singh Chaplot, N/A - Diego de las Casas, N/A - Florian Bressand, N/A - Gianna Lengyel, N/A - Guillaume Lample, N/A - Lucile Saulnier, N/A - L├Еlio Renard Lavaud, N/A - Marie-Anne Lachaux, N/A - Pierre Stock, N/A - Teven Le Scao, N/A - Thibaut Lavril, N/A - Thomas Wang, N/A - Timoth├Еe Lacroix, N/A - William El Sayed, N/A # Abstract - We introduce Mistral 7B v0.1, a 7-billion-parameter language model engineeredfor superior performance and efficiency. Mistral 7B outperforms Llama 2 13Bacross all evaluated benchmarks, and Llama 1 34B in reasoning, mathematics, andcode generation. Our model leverages grouped-query attention (GQA) for fasterinference, coupled with sliding window attention (SWA) to effectively handlesequences of arbitrary length with a reduced inference cost. We also provide amodel fine-tuned to follow instructions, Mistral 7B -- Instruct, that surpassesthe Llama 2 13B -- Chat model both on human and automated benchmarks. Ourmodels are released under the Apache 2.0 license. # Translation (by gpt-3.5-turbo) - Mistral 7B v0.1сЂ»сђЂтёфсѓїсЂЪсЃЉсЃЋсѓЕсЃ╝сЃъсЃ│сѓ╣сЂеті╣ујЄсѓњт«ЪуЈЙсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФУеГУеѕсЂЋсѓїсЂЪ70тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂMistral 7BсЂ»сђЂсЂЎсЂ╣сЂдсЂ«УЕЋСЙАсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂДLlama 2 13BсѓњСИітЏъсѓісђЂТјеУФќсђЂТЋ░тГдсђЂсЂісѓѕсЂ│сѓ│сЃ╝сЃЅућЪТѕљсЂФсЂісЂёсЂдLlama 1 34BсѓњтЄїжДЋсЂЌсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтйЊуцЙсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂжФўжђЪсЂфТјеУФќсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФсѓ░сЃФсЃ╝сЃЌтїќсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсѓ»сѓесЃфсѓбсЃєсЃ│сѓисЃДсЃ│№╝ѕGQA№╝ЅсѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсђЂТјеУФќсѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕсѓњтЅіТИЏсЂЌсЂфсЂїсѓЅС╗╗ТёЈсЂ«жЋисЂЋсЂ«сѓисЃ╝сѓ▒сЃ│сѓ╣сѓњті╣ТъюуџёсЂФтЄдуљєсЂЎсѓІсЂЪсѓЂсЂФсѓ╣сЃЕсѓцсЃЄсѓБсЃ│сѓ░сѓдсѓБсЃ│сЃЅсѓдсѓбсЃєсЃ│сѓисЃДсЃ│№╝ѕSWA№╝ЅсѓњухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓсЂЙсЂЪсђЂТїЄуц║сЂФтЙЊсЂєсѓѕсЂєсЂФтЙ«Уф┐ТЋ┤сЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓІMistral 7B -- InstructсѓњТЈљСЙЏсЂЌсђЂсЂЊсѓїсЂ»Llama 2 13B -- ChatсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњС║║жќЊсЂісѓѕсЂ│УЄфтІЋтїќсЂЋсѓїсЂЪсЃЎсЃ│сЃЂсЃъсЃ╝сѓ»сЂ«СИАТќ╣сЂДСИітЏъсЂБсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓтйЊуцЙсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»Apache 2.0сЃЕсѓцсѓ╗сЃ│сѓ╣сЂ«СИІсЂДтЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ # Summary (by gpt-3.5-turbo) - Mistral 7B v0.1сЂ»сђЂ70тёёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂ«УеђУфъсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂДсЂѓсѓісђЂжФўжђЪсЂфТјеУФќсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФGQAсѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсђЂSWAсѓњухёсЂ┐тљѕсѓЈсЂЏсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓсЂЙсЂЪсђЂMistral 7B -- InstructсЂ»Llama 2 13B -- ChatсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓњСИітЏъсЂБсЂдсЂісѓісђЂApache 2.0сЃЕсѓцсѓ╗сЃ│сѓ╣сЂ«СИІсЂДтЁгжќІсЂЋсѓїсЂдсЂёсЂЙсЂЎсђѓ

maintainer-comment-text.p: Mistral Large Mixtral-8x22B-v0.1, 2024 сЂфсЂЕсЂ«сЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓѓтЈѓуЁДсЂ«сЂЊсЂе сЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ«сѓ╣сѓ▒сЃ╝сЃФсЂїтцДсЂЇсЂЈсЂфсѓІсЂесђЂinferenceсЂ«latencyсЂїжЂЁсЂЈсЂфсѓісђЂУеѕу«Ќсѓ│сѓ╣сЃѕсЂїтцДсЂЇсЂЈсЂфсѓісЂЎсЂјсЂдт«ЪућеуџёсЂДсЂфсЂёсЂ«сЂДсђЂт░ЈсЂЋсЂёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐сЂДу┤аТЌЕсЂёinferenceт«ЪуЈЙсЂЌсЂЪсЂёсѓѕсЂГсђЂсЂесЂёсЂєсЃбсЃЂсЃЎсЃ╝сѓисЃДсЃ│сђѓ сЂЮсЂ«сЂЪсѓЂсЂФсђЂSlidingWindowAttentionсЂеGroupQueryAttention [Paper Note] GQA: Training Generalized Multi-Query Transformer Models from Multi-Head Checkpoints, Joshua Ainslie+, arXiv'23, 2023.05 сѓњТ┤╗ућесЂЌсЂдсЂёсѓІсђѓ сѓѕсѓіт░ЈсЂЋсЂёсЃЉсЃЕсЃАсЃ╝сѓ┐ТЋ░сЂДLlama2сѓњТДўсђЁсЂфсѓ┐сѓ╣сѓ»сЂДoutperformсЂЌ Instruction Tuningсѓњт«ЪТќйсЂЌсЂЪсЃбсЃЄсЃФсЂ»сђЂ13BсЃбсЃЄсЃФсѓѕсѓісѓѓChatbotArenaсЂДжФўсЂёElo RateсѓњуЇ▓тЙЌсЂЌсЂЪсђѓ
сѓ│сЃ│сЃєсѓГсѓ╣сЃѕжЋисЂ»8192

paper-entry.title: [Paper Note] Orca 2: Teaching Small Language Models How to Reason, Arindam Mitra+, arXiv'23, 2023.11
paper-entry.tags: NLP, LanguageModel, Supervised-FineTuning (SFT), Chain-of-Thought, action_wanted, SmallModel, OpenWeight, Reading Reflections
paper-entry.issue-body: # URL - https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.11045 # Authors - Arindam Mitra - Luciano Del Corro - Shweti Mahajan - Andres Codas - Clarisse Simoes - Sahaj Agarwal - Xuxi Chen - Anastasia Razdaibiedina - Erik Jones - Kriti Aggarwal - Hamid Palangi - Guoqing Zheng - Corby Rosset - Hamed Khanpour - Ahmed Awadallah # Abstract - Orca 1 learns from rich signals, such as explanation traces, allowing it to outperform conventional instruction-tuned models on benchmarks like BigBench Hard and AGIEval. In Orca 2, we continue exploring how improved training signals can enhance smaller LMs' reasoning abilities. Research on training small LMs has often relied on imitation learning to replicate the output of more capable models. We contend that excessive emphasis on imitation may restrict the potential of smaller models. We seek to teach small LMs to employ different solution strategies for different tasks, potentially different from the one used by the larger model. For example, while larger models might provide a direct answer to a complex task, smaller models may not have the same capacity. In Orca 2, we teach the model various reasoning techniques (step-by-step, recall then generate, recall-reason-generate, direct answer, etc.). More crucially, we aim to help the model learn to determine the most effective solution strategy for each task. We evaluate Orca 2 using a comprehensive set of 15 diverse benchmarks (corresponding to approximately 100 tasks and over 36,000 unique prompts). Orca 2 significantly surpasses models of similar size and attains performance levels similar or better to those of models 5-10x larger, as assessed on complex tasks that test advanced reasoning abilities in zero-shot settings. make Orca 2 weights publicly available at aka.ms/orca-lm to support research on the development, evaluation, and alignment of smaller LMs # Translation (by gpt-5-nano) -
